 Your system administrator may modify the workflows so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures
described in What do you want to do?.
You can update documents without going through the more formal change management process. See Create and manage changes to plant documents.
 You need to claim your workflow step when multiple users are assigned to the same user role. To claim a workflow step, select Actions 
 > Claim
Workflow Step.
What does the change request status mean?
Status
Description
Open
The change request has been created.
In Progress
The change request has been prepared, and has not yet been approved.
Approved
The change request has been approved, but not yet carried out.
Completed
The closure approval step has been completed and any conflicts have been resolved.
Rejected
The change request has been rejected.
Extension Requested
The temporary change request has had an extension requested.
Implemented
The temporary change request has had an extension approved.
Closed
Temporary changes have been performed and the temporary change request has been closed.
Change request workflow
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Change request workflow (modified in an update).
 In the change request workflow, the following steps are available only at the plant level and are not available when the change request is at project
level.
Prepare the change request
Evaluate feasibility
The change request workflow provides a simple process for tracking, reviewing, and coordinating change requests. Your administrator might change the
change request workflow to meet your company requirements.
Temporary change request workflow
A temporary change request is used to manage a change that has an expiration date. The change request is created and progresses through a workflow
where the change is carried out. When the expiration date is approaching, the request is sent into the Review temporary change workflow where the change
is either reverted back to its original state or extended until a later date.
 The change request is sent to the Review temporary change workflow a number of days before the expiration date is reached. The number of days
is set by your system administrator as described in Schedule a task to start a review temporary change workflow.
Initial temporary change request workflow
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
Review temporary change workflow
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
What do you want to do?
Create a change request
Create a change request within a markup layer
Prepare the change request
Analyze the impact of the requested change
Evaluate the change request
Coordinate the change request
Review and approve or reject the change
Correct a change request failure
Resolve conflicts at project scope
Resolve conflicts for merge
Complete the change request
Evaluate whether a temporary change is still required
Approve a temporary change extension
View the workflow of change request
Search for change requests
Create a change request
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a change request (modified in an update).
1. In the Change Management feature set, select Create Change Request or Create Temporary Change Request as required.
 You can also create a change request from the Actions 
 menu of a tag or a current signed-off document, or from a current signed-off
document's View and Markup page. For more information on creating a change request from the View and Markup page, see Create a change request
within a markup layer.
2. Type the required information on all tabs and select Next.
 When creating a temporary change request, select the date to revert the changes in Expiration date. The change request will enter the Revert
change workflow a defined number of days before this date.
3. Attach any related files from your computer to the change request.
4. Select:
Prepare Change to send the change request to the Prepare Content step. This step allows you to update the change request with related
documents and tags, and to mark up the changes you are requesting. For more information, see Prepare the change request.
Finish to create a To Do List task for the Evaluate feasibility step to be carried out. For more information, see Evaluate the change request.
Create a change request within a markup layer
For all the file types associated with a document, you can create a change request from within a markup layer. It is a two-step process:
You place a specific image, called a change request pin, on the file, and enter the details of the change request.
The Web Client creates the change request, which is displayed in your To Do list for you to Prepare the change request. The Web Client also attaches a
changemark to the change request pin, so that you can see the change request information on the View and Markup with Change Request page.
1. Search for the required document.
2. From the document's Actions menu, select View and Markup with Change Request.
 This option is available only for the current signed-off version of the document.
3. If you are viewing a Hexagon 2D drawing with a markup rendition (PDF) generated, click the Markup tab.
4. Select Markup 
, and select:
New markup to add the change request pin in a new markup layer
Open for edit to add the change request pin to an existing markup layer
5. From the expanded toolbar, select Image.
6. Click Image 
, and then click Image Options 
 to choose the image your organization uses for change request pins.
Even if several images are available, only one can be used as a change request pin. Change requests are only created when you choose the image that
your organization uses for change request pins.
 The default image is GreenActionPin 
. However, your administrator might have changed it using the SDACreateClassObjAndRelFromMarkup
client API.
7. Click anywhere in the file to place the change request pin.
 To move or resize the change request pin, click Select 
, and click the change request pin.
8. Create the change request as described in Create a change request.
9. Select Markup 
, and select Save Markup.
Change request pins can only be deleted if the corresponding change request is not in a workflow. They are deleted in the View and Markup with
Change Request page.
When a change request is completed any related change request pins are automatically deleted.
Prepare the change request
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Prepare the change request (modified in an update).
1. Select the Change Request in your To Do List 
, and then select Actions 
 > Prepare change.
 The Prepare Change page is automatically displayed when you select Prepare Change when creating a change request.
The Prepare Change page provides a single location to update your change request, such as:
Adding/Updating details
Marking up the changes that you are requesting
Attaching drawings, files, or other documents
Relating documents and tags
2. Carry out the required tasks as described below and select Complete to send the change request to the Evaluate feasibility step. For more information,
see Evaluate the change request.
 You can also select Save as Draft to save the change request so that you can continue working on it later.
You can do the following in the same page when you proceed to further steps:
Review any rejected change actions
Initiate an internal change action
Edit or add details
Select Update to add or change details of the change request.
Relate documents and tags
1. Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to display the documents or tags in the Results window.
2. Select the documents or tags to include.
You can also search for documents or tags in the search bar.
You can use the Filter by options to return more targeted search results.
3. Select RELATE.
The related documents and tags are displayed in their respective DOCUMENT VERSIONS and TAGS tabs. You can use these tabs to relate additional
documents and tags:
a. In the relevant tab, search for required documents or tags using the search bar.
b. Select the documents or tags in the list of results, and select RELATE.
You can also use FILTER options when searching for documents.
Attach files
Select Manage related files to attach files from your computer to the change request.
View potential conflicts and impacts
Select Potential Conflicts or Potential Impacts to Analyze the impact of the requested change.
Markup documents with comments and actions
1. Select 
 next to a related document to show the available versions.
2. Select the document to open the View and Markup page.
 If the document is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Validator, you can select
Show repeated results 
 in the Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more than one change request. For more information,
see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
3. Select 
 followed by New markup.
4. Select the type of markup from the Annotations list.
5. Select Save markup once you have completed your changes.
Manage markup layers
Select Manage Markup Layers to choose the markup layers to include in the consolidated files of the change request. For more information, see
Manage markup layers.
 In the Manage Markup Layers page, any markup layers created for the change request are automatically selected.
Analyze the impact of the requested change
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Analyze the impact of the requested change (modified in an update).
The Change Impact Analysis page allows you to do the following:
Add and remove documents or tags from the action.
Review conflicts with other actions.
Send technical queries about documents or tags in your or another person's change action.
 The items in the Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts lists can be filtered so that you can easily find the required items.
Add or remove documents or tags
When you select a tag or document, additional tags and documents that could be impacted by the change are displayed and can be easily added to the
change request.
1. Select the tag or document in the list.
2. In the Potential Impacts list, select the tags or documents to add, and select Apply to relate them to your change request.
If the item is already related, you can remove it from the change request. Select Delete 
 next to the tag or document to remove it.
 Expand 
 any document to find out more about its status and issue purpose.
If you open the document in the View and Markup page (and it is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data
Warehouse using Data Validator), you can select Show repeated results 
 in the Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more
than one change request. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
Review conflicts
1. Select the tag or document related to the change request.
2. In the Potential Conflicts list, review the items that could conflict with your requested change.
 Only items with the status of In Progress or Issued are displayed.
3. Select the item to view the information and responses for the change request.
4. Select Impact Analysis from the item's  Edit menu to view its impact analysis information.
Create an internal technical query
1. Select the tag or document related to the change request.
2. Select  Edit > Create an Internal Technical Query to ask questions about the tags or documents involved.
Evaluate the change request
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Evaluate the change request (modified in an update).
Why should I evaluate the feasibility of a change request?
Once a change request is created, the feasibility of the request can be evaluated. If one or more users can carry out the task, claim the step first and then
evaluate it. You can review the impact analysis and identify the potential conflicts of the submitted change request.
1. Select Evaluate feasibility in your To Do List, and then select Evaluate Change on the Actions menu.
Select Update to assign a project to your change and implement the changes at the project level.
Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to relate a document or tag to the change request. For more information, see Relate documents and
tags to the change request given below.
SSelect Manage Markup Layers to choose the markup layers to include in the consolidated files of the change request. For more information, see
Manage markup layers.
Use Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts to Analyze the impact of the requested change.
2. Select:
Approve to complete the evaluation step and send it to the next step.
 If this is a temporary change request, a merged PDF rendition of the change request is created, including markups.
Rework to return the change to the Prepare Change Request step for the originator to make additional changes.
The rejection reason is displayed on the Prepare Change page so that the originator can see why it was rejected.
When a change request is sent for rework at the Evaluate feasibility step, a new revision of the change request is created.
Reject Change to reject the change request and send it to the Completion Notice step. For more information, see Rejecting a change request.
3. If you selected Reject Change or Rework, enter the reason.
If you open the document in the View and Markup page (and it is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data
Warehouse using Data Validator), you can select Show repeated results 
 in the Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more
than one change request. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
If the change request has any ongoing actions, internal change actions, technical queries, or transmittals, you cannot use Reject Change.
After the Evaluate feasibility step, a merged PDF rendition of the change request is created, including markups and attached files. This PDF rendition is
available for the change request even after it is closed.
Relate documents and tags to the change request
1. Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to display the documents or tags in the Results window.
2. Select the documents or tags to include.
You can also search for documents or tags in the search bar.
You can use the Filter by options to return more targeted search results.
3. Select RELATE.
The related documents and tags are displayed in their respective DOCUMENT VERSIONS and TAGS tabs. You can use these tabs to relate additional
documents and tags:
a. In the relevant tab, search for required documents or tags using the search bar.
b. Select the documents or tags in the list of results, and select RELATE.
You can also use FILTER options when searching for documents.
Coordinate the change request
What does a coordinate change request imply?
You are now responsible to execute the change request. You can execute the change request using the document lifecycle workflow using the internal change
action. If your change request involves external contractors, please contact your sales representative to purchase and configure HxGN SDx Projects.
You can now access the current and latest version of the documents that represent the engineering changes.
For HxGN SDx Projects users
Documents are assigned to relevant contractors to allow the communication with external contractors. For more information, see Communicate with
stakeholders in your project.
Why do I need a project?
Assign a project to implement the requirements of the change request at the project scope.
 Your scope must be set to a project to implement the change request at the project level.
How do I assign a project?
1. Select Update on any of the workflow steps you have come across till now to set your change request to a particular project scope.
2. Change your scope from plant level to the assigned project level.
3. Select 
 and select your project from the Query list.
4. Select Save.
Who can associate a document to different contracts in different projects and what follows?
 A document can be related to different contracts in different projects.
A project engineer can associate multiple documents to a contract through the relationship builder. The software then sets the contract's external organization
by default as the collaborating organization for the documents according to the contract needs.
Set an action or contract to the documents related to your change request. A warning message is indicated if a document is not set to either an action or
a contract.
Set to project scope if you require the change request to be implemented at project level.
You must have the MoC Owner role assigned to coordinate the change.
1. Select Coordinate change implementation in your To Do List, and then select Coordinate Implementation on the Actions menu.
2. For each document that needs changes, select  > Create Internal Change Action and create the internal change action as described in Create an
internal change action.
3. Use Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts to Analyze the impact of the requested change.
4. Select Complete when the requested changes are implemented.
To review the proposed solutions, log on as a user with the MoC Approver role to Review and approve or reject the change to prepare it for the next workflow
step.
 Once a change request is claimed to the project level, all related documents, files, and tags from both the plant and project scopes are displayed in
the project scope. A new revision of the document is created and attached to the change request in the project scope.
 You can view the documents and files attached to the change request from both plant and project scopes in the DOCUMENT VERSIONS and ALL
FILES tabs. However, if the Query by selected configurations only option is selected in the Web Client Settings, only documents, files, and tags attached
to the change request in the current scope will be displayed. For more information on the Query by selected configurations only option, see Query by
selected configurations only.
Review and approve or reject the change
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Review and approve or reject the change (modified in an update).
1. Select the review or approval task from your To Do List, and then select the appropriate step on the Actions menu.
2. If required when reviewing, relate any documents and tags to the change request.
How can I relate documents and tags when reviewing the change request?
 When reviewing the change request or a temporary change request, you can relate documents and tags only if they are created at the
project level and have a working or current status. You can also relate a signed off document which is revised at the project level.
1. Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to display the documents or tags in the Results window.
2. Select the documents or tags to include.
You can also search for documents or tags in the search bar.
You can use the Filter by options to return more targeted search results.
3. Select RELATE.
The related documents and tags are displayed in their respective DOCUMENT VERSIONS and TAGS tabs. You can use these tabs to relate
additional documents and tags:
a. In the relevant tab, search for required documents or tags using the search bar.
b. Select the documents or tags in the list of results, and select RELATE.
You can also use FILTER options when searching for documents.
3. Select:
Approve to complete the workflow step and send it to the next step.
Rework to return the change to a previous step.
The reason entered for the rework is displayed on the Coordinate Implementation page so that the change coordinator can see why it was
sent for reworking.
When a change request is sent for rework at the Review Proposed Solution step, a new revision of the change request is created.
Reject Change to reject the change request and send it to the Completion Notice step. For more information, see Rejecting a change request.
4. If you selected Reject Change or Rework, enter the reason.
If you open the document in the View and Markup page (and it is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data
Warehouse using Data Validator), you can select Show repeated results 
 in the Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more
than one change request. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
If the change request has any ongoing actions, internal change actions, technical queries, or transmittals, you cannot use Reject Change.
At the Review proposed solutions step, you cannot approve the step if there is an internal change action in progress on an object related to the change
request.
Rejecting a change request
When you reject a change request, enter a reason for the rejection. It is then sent to the Completion Notice step, and the details of the change request
are updated with the rejection reason, rejection date, and the name of the user who rejected the change.
If an attached document has already been revised, the latest revisions of the document are terminated.
If the change request is assigned to a project when it is rejected, any associated documents and tags are unclaimed from the project, and the scope of
the change request is set back to plant level.
If the change request has any ongoing actions, internal change actions, technical queries, or transmittals, it cannot be rejected.
Correct a change request failure
 You must have the MoC Owner role to correct the failures.
1. Select the Correct Failure step in your To Do List, and then select Correct Failure on the Actions menu.
2. Select Manage Related Information in the Correct Failures page to relate documents, tags, and other objects as required.
3. Select Complete if the failure is corrected, or Reject to close the change request. The change request then progresses to the next step.
For more information on rejecting a change request, see Rejecting a change request.
Resolve conflicts at project scope
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Resolve conflicts at project scope (modified in an update).
 You must have a Discipline Lead role assigned to complete this step.
1. Set your configuration to the project scope.
2. Select Resolve Conflicts on the Actions menu.
 Resolve conflicts in drawings and documents by using relevant authoring tools.
3. Select Resolve Conflicts.
4. Select the filter options to see what you want to see.
 Select the Only Conflicts or All Attributes option before selecting the values you want to keep.
5. Select the values that you want to accept.
6. Select a property to expand it and see the differences between the plant and project values.
A banner explains if the object:
Has no properties or relationships
Is in conflict and what needs resolving
Was created in the current project or configuration
Has been deleted in the plant, or at a higher configuration, but the decision was to keep it in the current project, or configuration, and not terminate
it
Has no conflicts
For each object, you decide if the change should be merged into the plant or if the original value remains.
7. Select Save & Resolve to resolve the conflicts.
8. Select Complete.
To review and accept the construction changes log on as a user with the MoC Owner role, and for closure approval log on as a user with the MoC Approver
role, see Review and approve or reject the change.
Resolve conflicts for merge
1. Select Resolve Conflicts for Merge in your To Do List, and select Resolve Conflicts on the Actions menu.
2. Check for conflicts and resolve before merge.
For better performance, the drawing representation objects and off page connectors are ignored when displaying the conflicts in the Resolve
Conflicts window. When merging, these objects are considered from the project level.
When no conflicts are identified or all conflicts are resolved, the workflow merges documents and tags to the plant configuration.
When a change request is not associated with any document or tag, the workflow fails to proceed further.
3. Select Complete to merge all documents, tags, change actions and change request to the plant configuration.
 The change request is now set to plant scope and includes all related objects.
Complete the change request
Select Completion notice in your To Do List, and select Completed on the Actions menu.
 When a change request is completed any related change request pins are automatically deleted.
Evaluate whether a temporary change is still required
1. Select Evaluate Change in your To Do List, and then select Evaluate Change on the Actions menu.
2. Select:
Extend if the change is still required and should be extended.
 If you select to extend the change, you must enter a reason for the extension.
Close if the change should be reverted to its original state.
Approve a temporary change extension
1. Select Approve extension in your To Do List, and then select Review Change on the Actions menu.
2. Select Update.
3. In the Expiration date field, select the new date to revert the changes, and then select Update.
4. Select Approve to approve the extension.
 Rework sends the change back to the Evaluate change step.
View the workflow of change request
1. In the Change Management feature set, select Change Requests.
2. Select View workflow 
 on the Actions menu of the change request.
In the View Workflow page, you can view the progress of the workflow.
Search for change requests
Use the search options in the Change Management feature set to find change requests.
 If the document containing the change requests is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data
Validator, you can run a Change Request Name visual intelligence report to view impacted tags. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze
your data.
Create and manage changes to plant documents
You can update documents without going through a formal change management process. The workflow steps are similar to the formal change management
process but have a less rigorous approval process. The software will help you:
Create an internal change action for the document
Prepare the action
Make the document changes
Compare documents
Review and approve or reject the change
Remove changes
Resolve conflicts for merge before merging into the plant
 Click a box in the diagram to go to the associated procedure.
Create an internal change action
1. Select the documents and tags that need changes.
You can select more than one document or one tag and create an internal change action.
You can select Update to change any details of the Tags to change the effective impact of it to the change request.
You must sign off documents at the plant level before you can change them in a project.
2. Select Create Internal Change Action on the Actions menu of the document.
A revision of each document is created and related to the action.
If configured by your administrator, existing document relationships can be automatically inherited and assigned to the new document revision,
when revising a document attached to the change action.
If you are in the plant or project scope, you can create an internal change action for signed off documents created at the plant scope, but not for
working and superseded documents.
If you are in the project scope, you can create an internal change action for signed off and working documents created at the project scope, but not
for superseded documents.
3. Provide other details as needed.
If you select Approver Role, then a single user in that role must claim the step in their To Do List and perform the review.
If you select more than one Approver User, each user must perform the review.
If any of the approvers reject their review, the action is revised and returned to the implementor.
If all of the approvers approve the review, the action continues to the merge step in the workflow.
The software creates PDF renditions of documents that have attached files, including markups. PDF renditions improve the readability for the
recipient.
4. Select Finish.
Prepare the action
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Prepare the action (modified in an update).
1. Select the action in your To Do List 
, and then select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
The Prepare Content page provides a single location to update your actions, such as:
Adding/Updating details
Marking up the changes that you are requesting
Attaching drawings, files, or other documents
Relating documents and tags
You can also use Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts to review any potential impacts and conflicts of the action. This is done in the same way as
for change requests, see Analyze the impact of the requested change.
2. Select Complete.
Edit or add details
Select Update to add or change details.
Relate documents and tags
1. Select Relationship Builder.
2. Search for the objects that you want to relate to the action.
3. Select the objects, and then select Relate.
Attach files
Select Manage related files to attach files from your computer to the action.
Markup documents with comments and actions
1. Select 
 next to the document to show the available versions.
2. Select the document to open the View and Markup page.
3. Select 
 followed by New markup.
4. Select the type of markup from the Annotations list.
5. Select Save markup once you have completed your changes.
Make the document changes
1. Select the action in your To Do List, and then select Implement Changes on the Actions menu.
 If the implementation is assigned to a role, and not a user, you must first claim the step.
2. Select a reviewer from the tab to see any responses about the change from the project team.
 Use 
 to filter the responses.
3. Expand the document to see any attached documents.
The current document is the original document.
The rendition contains any markup layers.
4. Select Edit files on the document to download supporting viewable files, such as PDFs and make the changes.
 If multiple files are attached, a zip file is downloaded.
5. Check in the document when changes are complete.
6. Select Edit 
 to respond to the requested changes.
7. Select Complete.
The change action returns to the requester for approval.
The next steps are to:
Review and approve or reject the change
Resolve conflicts at project scope before merging into the plant.
Remove changes
1. Select the documents that were changed.
2. Select Actions > Files, and then Terminate.
Compare documents
You can compare two non-Hexagon file format documents to see what changes have been made to the design. For example, for a change action, you can
compare an original document created at project level with the latest document at plant level.
1. Select the change, and select Compare Changes from the Actions menu.
2. In the Document version, Files section, enable the Compare files toggle.
3. Select Add to compare for the two files that you want to compare.
4. Select Compare to compare the two documents.
For more information on comparing documents, see Comparing files using the viewer.
Create and manage data books
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Data Books
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/paths/62c7cdacc48fc19ea18abe20/>
A data book is a collection of structured documents that contain sections or chapters with other documents. You can create various types of data books such
as vendor data books, fabrication records, systems data books, and so on.
Why do you need data books?
As a document controller, you maintain a lot of maintenance records, fabrication records, vendor records, and so on. A data book helps you maintain records
as a collection of structured documents.
Create a data book
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a data book (modified in an update).
1. In the Data Books feature set, select Create Data Book.
2. Select a data book type, followed by Next.
 When assigning classification to the object, you can use the Classification search bar to search and filter for the appropriate classifications based
on their Name and Description.
3. Use the Item owner(s) list to specify one or more data book owner(s). If the owner is not assigned, the current user (who creates the data book) will be
set as the data book owner by default.
 Only the data book owner(s) can update, manage, and terminate a data book. They are also the only user who can generate the consolidated
PDF for a data book.
4. Complete the remaining information, and select Finish.
Update a data book
Search for the data book, and then select Actions 
 > Update Data Book.
Terminate a data book
Search for the data book, and then select Actions 
 > Terminate. Objects are successfully terminated.
Manage data books
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a data book chapter (modified in an update) and Relate documents and tags to
a data book chapter (modified in an update).
You can create and order chapters in your data book.
In the Data Books feature set, select the data book, and then select Actions 
 > Manage Data Book.
 Only the data book owner can manage a data book. The data book owner is one or more user(s) selected from the Item owner(s) list for the data
book or the user who created the data book if an owner was not selected.
Edit the description
Select 
 > Edit 
 to edit the description of the data book or its chapters.
Create a chapter
You can create two types of data book chapters:
Dynamic - Displays the latest version of the attached document.
Static - Displays a specific version of the attached document.
To create a data book chapter:
1. Select 
 to add a new chapter.
2. Type the required information, and select the Chapter Type from the list.
You can view the description of the data book chapters and sub-chapters from the data book's tree view, provided if the description exists for the
chapters or sub-chapters.
You can expand or collapse the data book structure at chapter and data book level.
 The data book chapter(s) and sub-chapter(s) which has content are indicated by the icon 
. An empty data book chapter or sub-chapter is indicated
by the icon 
.
Reorder chapters
Select Reorder 
 and drag the data book chapters to where you want to see them in your data book.
You can also reorder data book chapters from the Data Book Chapters feature.
When a consolidated PDF is generated, the chapters are included in the selected order.
Relate objects to the data book
1. Select 
 to relate objects to the selected data book or chapter.
2. In the Relationship Builder page, select the Search tab, and search for the objects to relate, such as documents, assets, models, or tags.
3. Select the required objects, followed by Relate.
 The Search feature only displays objects that can be related to the selected data book or chapter type.
 The objects related to the data book and its chapters can be filtered so that you can easily find the related items.
The data book is related to its chapters and their sub-chapters.
For any data book chapter, you can perform the following actions on the related documents and tags:
To sign off one or more documents, select corresponding document(s), and click Actions 
 > Sign Off.
To edit tags and their property values, select corresponding tag(s), and click Actions 
 > List Editing > List Edit.
Copy structure
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Copy Structure and Content in Data Books
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/62a029b4d0f4005f8becd38d>
1. Select a data book and select Actions 
 > Copy Structure.
2. Type the required information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied data book.
Documents and tags related to the data book are not copied.
The copied chapters are in the same order as in the original data book.
Reorder chapters of your original data book prior to copy for the required order of the data book.
Copy content
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Copy Structure and Content in Data Books
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/62a029b4d0f4005f8becd38d>
You can copy the content along with the related documents, tags, and models to your data book.
1. Select a data book and select Actions 
 > Copy Content.
2. Type the required information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied data book.
The copied data book consists of all existing content from the original data book.
Reorder chapters of your original data book before you copy, for the required order of the data book.
Generate a consolidated PDF for a data book
You can combine all the PDFs of documents and files attached to a data book and its chapters into a single consolidated PDF.
 Only the data book owner can generate a consolidated PDF. The data book owner is one or more user(s) selected from the Item owner(s) list for the
data book or the user who created the data book if an owner was not selected.
 The consolidated PDF generation of a data book is faster if you generate each individual PDF first for every file attached to the documents or data book
chapters. For more information on generating the PDFs for a file, see Generating PDFs.
How are documents attached to a data book organized?
When you generate the PDF of a data book, the documents and files attached to the data book chapters are organized according to the order arranged while
managing the data book.
1. Select the data book, and select Actions 
 > Generate Merged Rendition.
2. Select View Merged Rendition.
 You can configure the bookmarks for a merged rendition of a data book. Based on the defined settings, the bookmarks are displayed in the PDF
generated from the data book. For more information, see Configure bookmarks for the PDF generated from the data books.
Using work packages for plant operations
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®| Work Packages and Types of Work Packages
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65e0b3cdf554b733a09813e6>
Work packages support the planning workflow for plant maintenance or inspection work packages, or work orders, created in other plant operations software,
such as SAP. Once a work order is created, SDx Operations makes it easy to find and then add the needed documents, drawings, and other information to a
work package. You can mark up documents and drawings with comments or changes about the expected work, then attach them to the work package.
You can create two types of work packages:
Maintenance work package
Inspection work package
To learn more about the work packages that are available with SDx Projects, see Using work packages on a project.
If the Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) option is enabled in the System Options, the markups related to the work package are
automatically deleted when the work package is approved. For more information, see Select to automatically delete markups.
 You need to claim your workflow step when multiple users are assigned to the same user role. To claim a workflow step, select Actions 
 > Claim
Workflow Step.
Create a work package
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a work package (modified in an update).
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Creating a Work Package
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65e61c77995d144b274bf136>
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select Create <Type of Work Package> Work Packages.
2. Complete the required information.
If you are creating an inspection or maintenance work package, select an Approver role, and use the Approver user box to search for approvers
to add to your workflow.
If you are creating a CBS work package, select the Is Progress Item? check box to track the progress of documents based on the issue purpose.
3. Select:
Prepare Content to send the work package to the Prepare Content step. This step allows you to update the work package with related
documents, drawings, tags, and comments. For more information, see Prepare the content.
Finish to either send the work package for approval, or to complete the step.
 The work package is created with the Status set to Planned. The Status is updated to Active when the workflow completes or you can manually
update it to Closed, Executed, or Approved as required.
Prepare the content
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Prepare the content (modified in an update).
 You must be assigned a Planner role to complete this step.
The Prepare Content page is automatically displayed if you select Prepare Content when creating a work package. Alternatively, if you have saved a draft of
the work package, you can open it from your To Do List 
 by selecting Add drawings and documents or comments, followed by Actions 
 > Prepare
Content.
1. Select:
Manage Documents or Manage Tags to relate documents or tags to the work package. For more information, see Manage related information.
Relationship Builder to relate change requests to the work package. For more information, see Manage related information.
Manage Markup Layers to choose the markup layers to include in the consolidated files of the work package. For more information, see Manage
markup layers.
 In the Manage Markup Layers page, any markup layers created for the work package are automatically selected.
Manage related files to attach additional files from your computer to the work package. Attached files are displayed in the All Files tab.
Potential Conflicts or Potential Impacts to Analyze the impact of the work package.
View Work Package Scope 2D to show the tags related to the work package in a 2D drawing.
View Work Package Scope 3D to show the tags related to the work package in a 3D model.
 Select 
 next to a document to show the associated files. The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has
not yet been generated or because the base Hexagon file is not available. See Configure WebGL renditions for details of how to generate the web file.
Contact your administrator if the base file is unavailable.
2. Select COMPLETE to generate PDF renditions of documents that have attached files, including markups. The work package is then sent for approval if
required. A consolidated PDF is also generated including all the documents attached to the work package.
You can also select SAVE AS DRAFT to save the work package so that you can continue working on it later.
A watermark showing the current date is added to the PDFs so that it is easy to see when a printed copy is out of date. For more information on
configuring the watermark, see Configure watermarks. If you want to create a PDF with a customized watermark, use the workflow process steps
described in Create document PDF renditions and Create a consolidated PDF rendition object.
A PDF is not generated when a work package has no documents.
If a PDF rendition fails to generate and attach, the system directs the workflow to the Correct Failure step. For more information on PDF rendition errors, see
Correct PDF rendition generation failures.
Manage related information
Manage documents or tags
1. Select Manage Documents or Manage Tags to display the documents or tags in the Results window.
2. Select the documents or tags to include.
You can also search for documents or tags in the search bar.
You can use the Filter by options to return more targeted search results.
3. Select RELATE.
The related documents and tags are displayed in their respective DOCUMENT VERSIONS and TAGS tabs. You can use these tabs to relate additional
documents and tags:
a. In the relevant tab, search for required documents or tags using the search bar.
b. Select the documents or tags in the list of results, and select RELATE.
You can also use FILTER options when searching for documents.
Manage change requests
1. Select Relationship Builder, and use the Search tab to search for change requests to relate.
 A change request can only be related to a work package when it is in the same configuration.
2. Select the items you want to relate, and select Relate.
Analyze the impact of the work package
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Analyze the impact of the work package (modified in an update).
Work package scoping lets you assess the impacts and conflicts caused by documents and tags related to a work package or a change request.
You must have the Planner role assigned to scope the work package.
You can perform work package scoping when at least one document or tag is related.
The documents and tags associated with the work package must have relationships created for document-tag and tag-document.
If you open the document in the View and Markup page (and it is a published Hexagon model that has been transferred to the Facilities Data
Warehouse using Data Validator), you can select Show repeated results 
 in the Visual Intelligence panel to group and view tags belonging to more
than one work package. For more information, see Using visual intelligence to analyze your data.
Scope a work package
Select Potential Conflicts or Potential Impacts.
The items in the Potential Impacts and Potential Conflicts lists can be filtered so that you can easily find the required items.
You can also scope a work package by selecting the work package followed by Actions 
 > Work Package Scoping.
Add or remove documents or tags
When you select a tag, document, or change request, additional items that could be impacted are displayed. These can be easily added to the work package.
1. Select the tag, document, or change request in the list.
2. In the Potential Impacts list, select the tags and documents to add, and select Apply to relate them to your work package.
To remove a related item from the work package, select Delete 
 next to the item.
 Expand 
 any document to find out more about its status and issue purpose.
Review conflicts
1. Select the tag, document, or change request in the list.
2. In the Potential Conflicts list, review the items that could conflict with your work package.
 Work packages and change requests in the closed state are not listed.
3. Select the item to view the information for the work package.
4. Select Work Package Scoping from the item's  Edit menu to view its impact analysis information.
Update work package details
1. Select the work package, and select Actions 
 > Update.
2. Update the required information and select Update.
Review content
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Review work package content (modified in an update).
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Reviewing a Work Package
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65f3083e519b7d96a3778146>
1. Select the Review work package item in your To Do List 
, and then select Review work package from the Actions 
 menu.
2. Select:
 > Edit to add any comments.
Potential Impacts or Potential Conflicts to view the scope of the work package, see Analyze the impact of the work package.
View Work Package Scope 2D to show the tags related to the work package in a 2D drawing.
View Work Package Scope 3D to show the tags related to the work package in a 3D model.
3. Either Approve or Reject the work package.
 If the Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) option is enabled in the System Options, the markups related to the work package are
automatically deleted when the work package is approved. For more information, see Select to automatically delete markups.
View files
1. In the Work Packages feature set, select <Type of Work Package> Work Packages.
2. Select View and Markup 
 to view the consolidated files attached to a work package.
 If SDx has been configured to work with the j5 software, you can check that the required permits have been issued for this work package. For more
information, see View j5 work permits.
Select to automatically delete markups
If enabled, markups will be automatically removed when the workflow is approved. To enable this option:
In the system options, in the SDx only options details section, select the Allow workflow step to auto delete markup(s) option. For more information
on the system options, see Configure the system options.
Copy a work package
1. Select a work package and select Actions 
 > Copy.
2. Enter the required information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied work package.
Comments, documents, or files related to the original work package are not copied.
The copied work package has the same workflow as the original work package.
View j5 work permits in SDx
The j5 Control of Work application is used to control and track the progress of work permits.
 An administrator must configure SDx to work with the j5 Control of Work application to view the work permits related to a tag or work package in SDx.
For information on how to configure SDx to work with j5 Control of Work, see Configure SDx to work with j5.
1. Select the work package or tag of the j5 permits you want to view.
2. Select Actions 
 > View Permits.
The j5 Permits page displays the related permits and their statuses.
 For information about the j5 Control of Work application, see j5 Control of Work Help.
Capture the data
HxGN SDx Data Capture is a web-based solution for the Data Capture modules from the Desktop Client. When a large amount of unstructured and
unintelligent asset life cycle data needs to be captured and organized, Data Capture can be used to capture, review, validate and convert the data to an
intelligent and manageable format which in turn, streamlines work processes. The software enables you to view and efficiently manage large volumes of plant
information. Data Capture is a web-based solution, so you can easily access your data from anywhere. By using the software, you can save time and cost,
which would otherwise be spent in locating and validating the appropriate information from large amount of unstructured information. This delay can add to
operating costs.
Data Capture is an effective one-stop solution to:
Capture the unstructured data
Convert the unstructured data to a structured and manageable format
Resolve duplicates and inconsistencies in data
Validate the information
Update latest information
Search from large amount of information
Integrate information by creating relationships and cross-references
Generate and view reports for the processed information
View and manage the structured data from anywhere
View and manage Data Capture data using the Web Client
 To view and manage Data Capture data in the Web Client, you must first create and configure a site for Web Client, and then log on to the Web
Client as a user with Datacapture administrator or Datacapture controller role privileges. For more information on how to setup and configure Web Client site,
see Set up a new Web Client site.
Once you log on to the Web Client, you can:
View and perform tasks on documents
View, create, and update tags
 When tags are created manually or imported from another system, the software maintains the tag's uniqueness in relation to its area.
View business object relationships
Create document to tag relationships
View, create, perform tasks on the Facility Data Warehouse documents and attach them to the workflows
View and terminate work packages
View and create assets and models
Generate reports
View and hotspot Data Capture documents such as Hexagon 2D drawings, 3D models, and PDF files.
Extract data from the latest document.
Resolve duplicate documents.
 You can use the Advanced Search 
 in the Web Client to search for specific data.
For more information on how to quickly navigate the Web Client and perform relevant tasks, see Use the Web Client.
View and hotspot Data Capture documents
You can view and hotspot Data Capture documents, such as Hexagon 2D drawings, 3D models, and PDFs in the Web Client.
View 3D models
Select a 3D model document in the Data Capture documents search results list, and click View and Markup 
 on an object's Actions 
 menu to
view and hotspot the 3D WebGL viewer file (.binz) in the View and Markup pane. For more information on viewing 3D WebGL viewer files (.binz), see
Visualizing your data.
Markup options are not available when viewing the 3D WebGL viewer files (.binz) using View and Markup.
The Actions 
 menu is not available when viewing the objects in a 3D model, if the Data Capture 3D Reader Pre-Processor module processes
the 3D model based on either of the following configurations:
Piping elements in the 3D model are processed based on the relationship table.
Elements in the 3D model are processed based on the parent attribute configuration.
For more information, see Add a template.
View 2D drawings
1. Select a 2D drawing document in the Data Capture documents search results list, and click View and Markup 
 on an object's Actions 
 menu.
2. In the Select a file to view window:
To view and hotspot, select the respective 2D WebGL viewer file (.vecz) to open it in the View and Markup pane. For more information on viewing
2D WebGL viewer files (.vecz), see Visualizing your data.
To view and markup, select the non-Hexagon file to open it in the View and Markup pane. You can access the markup tools in the View and
Markup pane. For more information about the markup tools, see the viewer's Help.
 The viewer's Help might refer to functionality that is not available to you.
 You can also click View and Markup 
 to view the 3D WebGL viewer file (.binz) or the 2D WebGL viewer file (.vecz) in the View and Markup pane.
For the 2D drawing, after clicking View and Markup 
 , you must select the file type that you want to view in the View and Markup pane.
View PDFs
Select a document attached to a PDF file in the Data Capture documents search results list, and click View and Markup 
 on an object's Actions 
 menu
to view and hotspot the PDF file in the View and Markup pane.
To view and navigate to any tag in the 3D WebGL viewer file (.binz) or the 2D WebGL viewer file (.vecz), do the following:
Find and click any tag in the 3D WebGL viewer file (.binz) or the 2D WebGL viewer file (.vecz) and click Actions 
 > View Item in Drawing / Model.
For more information, see View, print, mark up, and navigate files.
In order to view a pipe line or a pipe run highlighted in a 3D model, you must process the .mdb2 and .zvf file of the 3D model using the Data Capture 3D
Reader Pre-Processor module. For more information on how to view an element in a 3D model using Web client, see View, print, mark up, and navigate
files. For more information on how to pre-process the 3D model information using Data Capture, see Add a template.
To graphically navigate to a tag in 2D drawing and 3D models, the Web Client uses the GraphicOID property information of a tag present in the drawing
representation object. The drawing representation object is created when extracting content from drawing files and 3D model files.
If the selected documents do not have WebGL viewer files associated with them, then you can generate the WebGL viewer files for those documents in
the Web Client by selecting the respective document from the Data Capture documents search results list. Then do the following:
For 2D drawings - Click Actions 
 > Files > Generate 2D WebGL Viewer Files
For 3D models - Click Actions 
 > Files > Generate 3D WebGL Viewer Files
Extract data from a document
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Extract data from a document (modified in an update).
You can extract data from a document thereby updating all relationships between tags and the document.
How can I configure DCOM to allow content extraction from Microsoft Office files?
You must enable DCOM permissions before HxGN SDx can access Microsoft Office applications.
 This is mandatory if you want to extract content from:
a Microsoft Excel file using the Data Capture Datasheet Reader Pre-Processor.
any Microsoft Office file (97-2003) using the Data Capture Office Reader Pre-Processor.
To set the DCOM configuration for the respective file type application, complete the following steps:
1. Click Start > Administrative Tools > Component Services.
2. In the tree view, expand Component Services > Computers > My Computer > DCOM Config.
3. Based on the Microsoft Office file type, locate and right-click the respective DCOM Config component service:
Microsoft Excel Application
Microsoft Word 97-2003
Microsoft PowerPoint Slide (97-2003)
4. On the shortcut menu, click Properties.
5. In the General tab, set the Authentication Level to None.
6. In the Identity tab, select The Launching User option.
7. In the Security tab, set the Launch and Activation Permissions to Customize, and click Edit.
a. Add the Administrators created by Server Manager.
b. Select the Allow check box for the following items:
Local Launch
Remote Launch
Local Activation
Remote Activation
Read
Special permissions
What is the purpose of a default template group?
When you use Data Capture Content Discovery Task in the Desktop Client or Extract Content in the Web Client to extract content from multiple documents,
the software automatically considers the templates and rules defined for the default template group. The default template group is considered only when a
PDF file or a drawing file is attached to the document. To successfully extract content, ensure that the templates and rules are configured for the template
group. However, if you have not chosen any template group as default, the software automatically considers a template group
DefaultDrawingTemplateGroup for extracting the content. This default template group is provided with the software.
In the Web Client, to extract content from a single document, the software automatically considers the templates and rules defined for the auto selected default
template group. The default template group is considered only when a PDF file or a drawing file is attached to the document. However, you have an option to
select and apply any other template group instead of the default template group. For more information, see Extract data from a document.
 For the auto selected default template group, the Match Tag Patterns option is pre-selected.
How is the content extracted from a document with more than one file attached?
When processing a document with more than one file attached, the following scenarios are considered:
If different file types are attached, the software first checks for a file with the ISPFNMasterFile interface, and content is extracted from that file.
If a file with the ISPFNMasterFile interface is not found, content is extracted from the file with the highest priority. By default, files with the .dwg file
extension are set as highest priority. However, the priority of the file can be changed in the Data Capture Central settings module in the Desktop Client.
For more information, see Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction.
If more than one file of highest priority is attached, then you must choose the file to extract the content from the Extract Content window.
How can I extract double-byte characters from the content?
By default, the Data Capture supports a few double-byte characters when extracting content from a document. To allow this, you must ask your administrator
to enable the Extract matching double-byte characters as alias tag option in the Desktop Client's Data Capture Administration module Master Tag
Patterns page. For more information, see Create a new master tag pattern.
Before you start content extraction, we recommend you check if the double-byte character you want to extract, is supported by the Data Capture, by
completing the following steps:
1. Go to https://www.dotnetfiddle.net <https://www.dotnetfiddle.net> in a browser.
2. Copy and paste the following Data Capture double-byte character conversion code snippet in the Code Editor box:
using System;
using System.Text;
public class Program
{
public static void Main()
{
Console.WriteLine(Encoding.GetEncoding(1252).GetChars(Encoding.Convert(Encoding.Unicode,
Encoding.GetEncoding(1252), Encoding.Unicode.GetBytes("<double-byte character to be converted>"))));
}
}
3. Click Run to execute the code and verify the following:
If the output is a single-byte character, the double-byte character is converted successfully.
If the output is a ?, the double-byte character conversion failed.
To configure Data Capture for allowing extraction of unsupported double-byte characters, do the following:
 Before doing this, set the query scope of your site to the (Scope Not Set) Configuration Top and load the Data Capture sample data. For
more information on how to load the sample data, see Load the sample data for your site.
1. In the Desktop Client, under the Document classifications tree, expand the Data Capture Administration node.
2. Under the Data Capture Administration node, right-click the Data Capture Miscellaneous node, and click Show Template Documents.
3. Right-click the Double Byte Substitution List template in the tree view, and click Edit > Check Out to check out the template and edit the
DoubleByteSubstitutionList.xml.
4. In the file, based on the type of double-byte character to be converted, copy and paste the following XML code and replace the child node value with the
double-byte character's Unicode value and the parent node value with the corresponding single-byte character's Unicode value.
<SubstituteChar UnicodeValue="Single-byte character's unicode value"> <!Parent node>
<SubstituteeChar>Double-byte character's unicode value</SubstituteeChar> <!Child node>
</SubstituteChar>
For example,
<SubstituteChar UnicodeValue="007E"> <!Unicode value for single-byte character tilda ~>
<SubstituteeChar>ff5e</SubstituteeChar> <! Unicode value for double-byte character tilda 〜>
</SubstituteChar>
This sample code converts a double-byte character 〜 to single-byte character ~.
5. Check in the template with the updated XML file.
 Data Capture applies the conditions in the template file when processing the content with double-byte characters.
To extract content from a document, use Web Client to perform the following steps:
 To extract content from 3D models .zvf and .mdb2 files, we recommend you to install Microsoft 365® Access Runtime (64-bit) on the HxGN SDx
application server.
1. Click Documents > All Documents.
2. To extract data, select a document from All Documents list, and click Actions 
 > Extract Content.
3. In the Extract Content window, do the following:
a. Select any file attached to the document from the Select File list.
b. Select a template group from the TemplateGroup/Template list to apply the processing rules using the corresponding Preprocessor Reader.
 From Update 14 onwards, for PDF and drawing files, a template group which is set as default in the Data Capture Drawing Reader Pre-
Processor and PDF Reader Pre-Processor is automatically selected and applied for extracting content. However, you can select and apply any template
group from the TemplateGroup/Template list to process the content. For more information, see Manage drawing reader pre-processor templates and
template groups and Manage PDF reader pre-processor templates.
4. Click OK.
 To use preprocessed content files for processing the file, click Show more and apply more options as follows:
Click this
To do this
Use Existing PreProcessed Content Files
Process the file using the preprocessed content XML file available.
 To extract content using the preprocessed content files, we recommend
you attach the ContentFile.xml along with the corresponding file to the
document. You must also attach GraphicsMapFile.xml if the file type supports
graphical navigation.
Reader Pre-Processor
Select appropriate Preprocessor Reader for processing the datasheet file.
For Hexagon 3D model
OleDB Provider box and type the
connection string.
Connect to the Microsoft 365â Access database.
Match Tag Patterns check box.
Extract the tags based on the tag patterns defined in the Tag Discovery Patterns
module.
Before preparing to work with large amount of data, based on the size of the data it is recommended to configure the LicenseTimeoutSeconds property
under Site Settings node in HxGN SDx Server Manager. This setting will prevent the license token to timeout, thereby allowing the session to retain.
To view the status of content extraction from a selected document:
Select Actions 
 menu, and click Show the detail form > Extract Content.
 For more information about the status of a document processed using the Data Capture, see Data Capture Document Status.
FDW tags are created without applying the ENS definition.
By default, the property Is Data Capture Rel is set to True on document to tag relationships SPFNDocRevMasterTag, SPFNDocRevAliasTag,
FDWDocRevTag and SPFNFDWDocRevChildTag for Data Capture tags.
You can also extract content from documents created with the files stored in your own third party document management system (DMS). For more
information on configuring SDx to support document with remote files, see Configure an external document management system (DMS) to store files.
Predefined tag discovery patterns are used to determine tag names during processing. Only the name property of associated objects is considered for
this purpose, other properties are ignored.
The master tag and the FDW tag are identified with the same icon 
. The alias tag is identified with the 
 icon.
In the Desktop Client, the FDW tag is identified with the 
 icon which is same for the master tag extracted using the Data Capture Content Discovery
Task module.
You can select Match Tag Patterns to extract the tags based on the tag patterns defined in the Tag Discovery Patterns module. For few file types, Match
Tag Patterns is pre-selected.
Except for the datasheet file, the Reader Pre-Processor is automatically selected based on the attached file type. For the datasheet file, you can select
one of the following options as the base reader:
Datasheet Reader
PDF Reader
You can view the base reader set for different file types in the Data Capture Central Settings module in the Desktop Client. For more information, see
Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction.
For PDF files and Microsoft Office files, by default PDF reader is selected as the base reader in the Data Capture Central Settings module in the
Desktop Client. For any file types other than the PDF files if the base reader is set as the PDF reader, when extracting content from such file types the
PDF reader generates Markup renditions which are used by the software to retrieve the tags details. For more information, see Manage file types and
prioritize them for content extraction.
After extracting data from the document, you can navigate to the document and tags in Web Client. For more information, see View and manage Data
Capture data using the Web Client.
Extract data from multiple documents
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Extract data from multiple documents (modified in an update).
The following procedure is used for extracting content from multiple documents which are in a working or current revision state. It extracts tags and their
relationships from multiple documents.
 To extract the content using the pre-processed content files, you must ensure that corresponding file is attached to the document along with the
ContentFile.xml. You must also attach GraphicsMapFile.xml if the file type supports graphical navigation.
How can I configure DCOM to allow content extraction from Microsoft Office files?
You must enable DCOM permissions before HxGN SDx can access Microsoft Office applications.
 This is mandatory if you want to extract content from:
a Microsoft Excel file using the Data Capture Datasheet Reader Pre-Processor.
any Microsoft Office file (97-2003) using the Data Capture Office Reader Pre-Processor.
To set the DCOM configuration for the respective file type application, complete the following steps:
1. Click Start > Administrative Tools > Component Services.
2. In the tree view, expand Component Services > Computers > My Computer > DCOM Config.
3. Based on the Microsoft Office file type, locate and right-click the respective DCOM Config component service:
Microsoft Excel Application
Microsoft Word 97-2003
Microsoft PowerPoint Slide (97-2003)
4. On the shortcut menu, click Properties.
5. In the General tab, set the Authentication Level to None.
6. In the Identity tab, select The Launching User option.
7. In the Security tab, set the Launch and Activation Permissions to Customize, and click Edit.
a. Add the Administrators created by Server Manager.
b. Select the Allow check box for the following items:
Local Launch
Remote Launch
Local Activation
Remote Activation
Read
Special permissions
What happens when content extraction from multiple documents fail?
When content extraction from multiple documents fail, a content discovery task is automatically created to find the problem. It does this by re-processing the
large document set into smaller and smaller batches, starting with batches of 100, then batches of 10, and finally in batches of 1. For each batch, a child
content discovery task is created under the master content discovery task.
For example, to re-process 1000 documents, 10 content discovery tasks are created, each with 100 documents. Each batch of 100 documents that fails would
then be re-processed into 10 child content discovery tasks, each with a batch size of 10 documents. Finally, each batch of 10 documents that fails is re-
processed with 10 child content discovery tasks, each with 1 document to find the failed document.
 Click Notifications 
 to view the issue in the failed document that corresponds to the failed child content discovery task.
How is the content extracted from multiple documents when multiple files are attached to one or more documents?
When processing multiple documents, where one or more documents have more than one file attached, the following scenarios are considered:
If different file types are attached, the software first checks for a file with the ISPFNMasterFile interface, and content is extracted from that file.
If a file with the ISPFNMasterFile interface is not found, content is extracted from the file with the highest priority. By default, files with the .dwg file
extension are set as highest priority. However, the priority of the file can be changed in the Data Capture Central settings module in the Desktop Client.
For more information, see, see Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction.
If more than one file of highest priority is attached, then the software fails to extract the content as it was not able to select the file.
When processing multiple documents, content cannot be extracted from a document to which multiple files are attached. In such scenario, you can
process each document at a time instead of multiple documents, which will allow you to select the file from which content will be extracted. For more
information, see Extract data from a document.
Based on the attached file type, the default reader is automatically selected to process the file and extract content. The reader is assigned based on the
default settings configured in Data Capture Central Settings module in the Desktop Client. For more information on the file types and the supported
readers, see Manage reader and application relationship and Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction.
1. Click Documents > All Documents.
2. To extract content from multiple documents, select two or more documents from All Documents list, and click Actions > Extract Content.
What is the purpose of a default template group?
When you use Data Capture Content Discovery Task in the Desktop Client or Extract Content in the Web Client to extract content from multiple documents,
the software automatically considers the templates and rules defined for the default template group. The default template group is considered only when a
PDF file or a drawing file is attached to the document. To successfully extract content, ensure that the templates and rules are configured for the template
group. However, if you have not chosen any template group as default, the software automatically considers a template group
DefaultDrawingTemplateGroup for extracting the content. This default template group is provided with the software.
In the Web Client, to extract content from a single document, the software automatically considers the templates and rules defined for the auto selected default
template group. The default template group is considered only when a PDF file or a drawing file is attached to the document. However, you have an option to
select and apply any other template group instead of the default template group. For more information, see Extract data from a document.
 For the auto selected default template group, the Match Tag Patterns option is pre-selected.
By default, the property Is Data Capture Rel is set to True on document to tag relationships SPFNDocRevMasterTag, SPFNDocRevAliasTag,
FDWDocRevTag and SPFNFDWDocRevChildTag for Data Capture tags.
Predefined tag discovery patterns are used to determine tag names during processing. Only the name property of associated objects is considered for
this purpose, other properties are ignored.
To extract content from the drawing and pdf files, the software applies the templates and rules from the template group which is set as default. For more
information, see Manage drawing reader pre-processor templates and template groups and Manage PDF reader pre-processor templates.
When complete, click Notifications 
 to view the status of content extraction.
FDW tags are created without applying the ENS definition.
In order to process documents to which 3D models are attached, you must ensure that corresponding pre-processed content files are available. In case,
pre-processed content files are not available, then you must process one document at a time instead of multiple documents. For more information on
how to extract content from a single document, see Extract data from a document.
When processing multiple documents, content cannot be extracted from a document to which multiple files are attached. In such scenario, you can
process each document at a time instead of multiple documents, which will allow you to select the file from which content will be extracted. For more
information, see Extract data from a document.
Based on the attached file type, the default reader is automatically selected to process the file and extract content. The reader is assigned based on the
default settings configured in Data Capture Central Settings module in the Desktop Client. For more information on the file types and the supported
readers, see Manage reader and application relationship and Manage file types and prioritize them for content extraction.
To view the status of content extraction from a selected document:
Select Actions 
 menu, and click Show the detail form > Extract Content.
 For more information about the status of a document processed using the Data Capture, see Data Capture Document Status.
Extract data from Data Capture documents attached to the workflow template
You can use Load Data Capture Documents in the Data Capture feature set to load the CSV file and attach supporting files to the documents specified in
the CSV file. The workflow template then creates Data Capture documents for any files attached to the loaded documents based on the mappings in the CSV
file and extracts the master and alias tags from them.
Load Data Capture documents to extract content
To load the Data Capture documents using a load job and extract content from them, use Web Client to complete the following steps:
1. In the Data Capture feature set, select Load Data Capture Documents.
2. In the Load Documents form, type Description in the Main details section.
 The following values are automatically assigned by the software:
Name in the Main details section
Classification in the Loader Options section
3. Click NEXT.
4. In the Select a csv file to attach pane, select the Local File System.
5. Click CHOOSE FILES, select a CSV file to add to the load job, and click NEXT.
 You can use the "Sample.csv" file available in the Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find
sample data on Smart Community.
6. In the Select supporting files to be uploaded to share pane, select the Local File System.
7. Click CHOOSE FILES, select the files along with the corresponding pre-processed content files.
 You can also attach the corresponding graphics map file and the navigation file to support graphical navigation when viewing the object.
8. Click FINISH to create a load job by attaching the objects to the workflow template and begin content extraction using the Content Discovery Task.
 We recommend you to view the status of the content extraction in the Content Discovery Tasks page from the Data Capture feature set.
For more information, see View Content Discovery Task Status.
 After successful content extraction, you can view the documents and its corresponding tags, and navigate to them. For more information, see View
and manage Data Capture data using the Web Client.
You can click Notifications to view the status of the content extraction.
View the status of the Load Job
To view the status and monitor progress of the load job created for loading the Data Capture documents, you can do the following:
1. In the Data Capture feature set,click Show Data Capture Loader Jobs.
2. On the My Load Jobs page, select a load job, and click 
 to expand it and monitor the progress.
 You can click Refresh 
 in the expanded view of the load job, to view its current progress.
 You can also view the Data Capture Loader job in the My Load Jobs page of Load Manager feature set. For more information, see Monitor the
load job progress.
View Content Discovery Task Status
To view the status of the content extraction from the documents:
In the Data Capture feature set, select Content Discovery Tasks to view the status of the content discovery task in the Status column of the
corresponding CDT job in the list.
 You can monitor the progress percentile in the Progress column of the corresponding CDT job in the list.
 If you are using this content discovery task for extracting tags from FDW documents, then this CDT job is not displayed in the Data Capture Content
Discovery Tasks module in the Desktop Client.
To view the status of the child content discovery tasks related to the parent CDT job, you must:
a. Select a content discovery task from Content Discovery Tasks list, and click Actions 
 > Relationships
b. From the Relationships menu, click Show Child Content Discovery Tasks and select a child content discovery task from the list to view the
status of the child content discovery task in the Status column.
Quality Control using Data Capture
You can use the Data Capture Quality Control feature set in the Web Client to correct any incorrect relationships existing between processed documents and
the organization items, like discipline, plant, and document type. As a reviewer, you can approve the documents once you have corrected all the relationships.
 Only administrators and reviewers can access this module.
Resolve duplicate documents
