Search for planned deliverables
What does a deliverable milestone include?
Deliverable milestones help track the progress of documents or deliverable items, and their issue purpose, that a contractor submits. A deliverable milestone
includes the following information:
Issue purpose - Specifies a single reason for sending a document.
Planned issue date - Specifies the date on which the actual revision of a deliverable item for a specific issue purpose is scheduled to be sent by the
contractor.
Receipt date - Specifies the date on which the document review workflow starts for a specific issue purpose.
Issue date - Specifies the revision issue date for the deliverable item for a specific issue purpose.
Issued revisions - Specifies all the revisions of a deliverable item.
Review Status - Specifies if the revision of a deliverable item is in review or completed.
Reviewed revision - Specifies the latest reviewed revision of a deliverable item.
Date returned - Specifies the date on which the document sent for a review was returned.
Return code - Specifies the current state of the review set by the consolidator for the deliverable item for a specific issue purpose.
When is a deliverable milestone created?
When a deliverable is created as part of a Master Document Register submittal, the software creates a progress deliverable milestone for the deliverable item.
The deliverable milestone stores the planned and receipt dates of the deliverables for the issue purposes along with the review completion information, such
as review status, date returned, and return code.
You can search for planned documents that have been submitted and determine when they should become available to you based on the planned dates and
issue purpose.
Every deliverable item can be issued for multiple issue purposes. Examples of issue purposes include Issued for review (IFR) or Issued for construction (IFC).
Each issue purpose has its own milestone, but only one milestone is created for a deliverable item for a given issue purpose. In addition, planned dates and
actual dates are stored on the deliverable milestone. This allows you to track the progress of contractors by seeing when items are planned and when the
items were actually delivered.
1. In the Documents feature set, select the type of documents to find; for example, Engineering Documents.
2. Select Actions 
 on a deliverable item and then select Show Deliverable Milestone.
The deliverable milestones track the planned date for a given issue purpose and the delivery against that milestone including the revisions issued at that
issue purpose.
Search for documents based on issue purpose
If there are documents you are interested in that were not automatically assigned to you, you can register your interest in reviewing a document by selecting
each type of document issue purpose you require, such as Issued for Design. Each time the document is revised or changed, you are informed that the
document requires a review.
The following table lists the standard set of issue purposes.
Icon
Name
Description
Prelim
Preliminary
The document is issued as the first preliminary draft.
IFR
Issued for Review
The document has been issued for review.
IFD
Issued for Design
The document is a project design document.
IFC
Issued for Construction
The document has been issued for a construction project.
AB
Issued for As-Built
The document has been issued for the accepted as-built project.
Final
Issued for Final
The document has been issued as the final document revision.
 Click Expand 
 to display the issue purpose details, including any rules applied to that issue purpose.
 You can configure additional issue purposes, see Configure issue purposes.
Learn more about creating review plans
The Review Plan feature helps you select the contractor's documents that you want to review. You search for documents to add to your plan based on a range
of criteria, including the design information of the related tags. Then, select documents and related milestones that you are interested in reviewing.
Your organization can configure rules to preselect the items in the plan for you to review. When the company receives a submittal from the contractor, these
items automatically route to you. The rules can be defined to select documents by type, issue purpose, and document discipline. You can also filter the criteria
based on matching the document and tag discipline to the user’s discipline. Conditional logic can be used for specific requirements, such as selecting
documents based on related tag criteria, for example, type.
Document review matrices
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Document Review Matrices
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/656ef4b24a64fe8264ccafc1>
Document review matrices are used to set up recipient lists for reviews in the QA review workflow. The recipients are selected based on the properties or
relationships of the object in the workflow. Each recipient on the matrix has a Reason for Receipt (RFR), which indicates why the recipient is included.
A document review matrix is a table with recipients and document attributes such as discipline, issue purpose, security code, and so on. This matrix can be
used as an alternative to the review plan process where the recipients are specified on the matrix. Each cell in the grid is marked with the reason for receipt for
each recipient and might also include the format in which they will be receiving the document.
What do you want to do?
1. Create a document review matrix.
2. Populate the document review matrix.
 You can import the existing matrices from the Desktop Client, but any changes made in the Web Client will not be visible in the Desktop Client.
3. Select the matrix to be used when creating a project. For more information, see Configure a document review matrix.
Create a document review matrix
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create a document review matrix (modified in an update).
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Create Document Matrix.
2. In the New Create Document Matrix page, type the required information, and select Finish.
A matrix is created with the default column set provided in the class definition. For more information, see Configure SDx document review matrix.
To view the matrix, select Workflow Management feature set > Document Matrices.
Populate the document review matrix
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Populate the document review matrix (modified in an update)
To assign recipients and specify properties:
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Document matrices.
2. Select the matrix and select Actions > Assign Recipients.
3. Select Add Row 
 and specify a value for the property cells in each row.
The attribute columns for the matrix are defined by the system administrator according to project requirements. Typical attributes include Area,
Type, Discipline, and Issue Purpose.
Select  next to the column item to add or remove columns.
You can use the asterisk (*) wildcard character to associate a row with all the values of an attribute.
Selecting a property or relationship as a filter, and entering a wildcard (*) will only consider the objects which have the attribute (property or
relationship) instantiated.
The submittal process is intended to be used by the external organizations, such as vendors, suppliers, and so on, to submit new document
revisions. If certain documents or documents with particular issue purposes, do not require a review, the matrix can be configured to route the
documents around the engineering review. However, the documents are signed off and the deliverable milestones (document planning information)
are updated.
Toggle review mode 
 - The review mode is turned on by default when you add a row. When you turn it off 
,
any existing recipients in that row are removed (see screenshot below).
the documents that satisfy any of the attribute values are not sent for reviews.
The following relationship-related properties allow multiple value selection: Area, Unit, Discipline, and Issue Purpose. The selection of the values in
Area and Unit are mutually dependent. For example, if you select a specific area, the units related to that area are filtered and available for
selection.
The following enumerated list properties allow single value selection: Category, Type, Sub Type, and Security Code. The selection of the values in
Category, Type, and Sub Type are mutually dependent. For example, you have to select a specific document category to select a type related to
that category.
4. To add the recipients, select 
 next to the Reason for Receipt.
Select the recipients cell to activate the edit mode, and to add 
, remove, or highlight the recipients.
To remove the recipients from the SDx document review matrix, you need to select  next to the user, and do the following:
Select Remove to remove the user from the active row.
Select Remove All to remove the user from all the rows of the matrix.
To highlight a user in all the rows of the matrix, you need to select  next to the user, and select Highlight. To turn off the highlight, you need
to select  next to the user, and select Unhighlight. In the user interface:
You can highlight only one user at any given time.
Highlight is turned off when you refresh the view, save or undo any changes.
The default values of the recipients are R (reviewer), I (information), and CON (consolidator).
Reason for receipts related to the SDXDocumentReviewMatrix class definition will be displayed in the system. For more information on how to
configure the reason for receipts, see Configure SDx document review matrix.
 Each user appears in a chip-based interface (users separated by comma is the default view), and you can view the user information by selecting on the
chip.
What do the buttons on the Assign Recipients page do?
Click this
To do this
Add Row
Add an empty row at the beginning of the matrix.
Delete Row
Delete the selected row.
Duplicate Row
Create a copy of the selected row right below the selected row. You need to
select a row to enable this button.
SAVE
Save any changes.
Undo All
Discard any changes.
Compare the users in the matrix
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Compare users within the matrix (modified in an update).
You can compare the recipients within the matrix to know where the recipients are in all rows. This helps you to check if the recipients are correctly assigned
and also fill any missing gaps.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Document matrices.
2. Select the matrix, and select Actions > Assign Recipients.
3. Select 
 next to Recipients. Select 
 to switch to the normal view.
4. Select the users you want to compare.
You can select the users available in the current view only.
You can select a maximum of three users.
The user selection is saved for the current view.
The selected users are displayed in a column view, along with empty rows if any.
You can hide the empty rows by selecting 
, available next to 
.
You can perform all the basic matrix operations in this view, such as copy the assignments of one user to another, add a user, remove a user, add a row,
duplicate a row, and so on.
When you add or remove any of the selected users, the view modifies accordingly.
You can also add or remove a user not in this view.
You can undo any unsaved changes.
Copy user assignments within the matrix
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Copy user assignments within the matrix (modified in an update).
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Document matrices.
2. Select the matrix and select Actions > Assign Recipients.
3. Select  next to the source user, and select Copy Assignments.
4. Select  next to the target user, and select Append Assignments. The target user is added to the corresponding cells in the matrix where the source
user is present, and highlighted.
For example, let's assume User B has review assignment for drawings of unit 100 and information only assignment for datasheets of unit 200. You can copy
User B’s assignments to User A using the Copy Assignments and Append Assignments options. User A then has the same review settings as User B along
with the ones that were already defined.
Filter column headings in a matrix
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Filter column headings in a matrix (modified in an update).
You can use the Filter option to restrict the number of displayed rows in the matrix. You can filter by multiple columns.
1. In the Workflow Management feature set, select Document matrices.
2. Select the matrix and select Actions > Assign Recipients.
3. Select  next to the column header you want to filter, and select Filter 
 to see the filtering options.
4. Type the criteria.
 Select And to combine conditions. Select Or to specify that one condition be met.
The applied column filters will be cleared when you add or duplicate a row.
The currently selected row will be cleared when you apply a filter.
When you close the Assign Recipients window, the filters are not preserved.
With the filter turned on, if you edit the property values and save the changes, the system reloads the matrix rows based on the filter criteria.
Show and hide column headings
1. Select  next to the column header, and select Columns 
 to see the column headings available for display.
2. Select a column heading to show it in the matrix.
3. Clear the column heading to hide it in the matrix.
When you add or remove a column, all the hidden columns will be visible.
You can undo the current changes by selecting RESET. However, you cannot undo the changes after selecting APPLY.
When you close the Assign Recipients window, the hidden columns are not preserved.
Copy the document review matrix
You can copy a document review matrix that is created in the same configuration as well as another configuration.
If you are copying a matrix which is created outside of the active create scope, you must select Copy Across Plant/Project in the Copy Matrix form.
By default, configuration items from the active scope are listed in the Copy Matrix form. Your administrator can modify this to list the configuration items
based on the configuration states.
You can query a document review matrix created in the same configuration or higher configurations.
Copy the structure
1. Select a matrix and select Actions 
 > Copy Matrix Structure.
2. Edit the information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied matrix.
The copied matrix consists of all existing columns from the original matrix.
The filter criteria and assigned users related to the original matrix are not copied.
Copy the content
1. Select a matrix and select Actions 
 > Copy Matrix Content.
2. Edit the information and select Copy.
Select Pins 
 > New Items to view the copied matrix.
The copied matrix consists of all the columns, including the filter criteria and assigned users, from the original matrix.
Review delivered documents
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Introduction to QA Review Workflow
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/6405ef91eac62d273e9a2eff> .
When a document is submitted to the system, you are assigned a review task or a consolidation task based on your review plan or the document review
matrix. Select the task in your To Do List to review the document. If you cannot review the document, you can reassign the task to another user or waive the
task.
 Your system administrator may modify the workflow so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures
described below.
What is a Quality Assurance (QA) Review?
The Quality Assurance (QA) Review allows your company technical experts, engineers, and stakeholders to review documentation deliverables and other
documentation during a project. The aim of the review process is to ensure that the correct information is delivered to the right people so they can efficiently
perform Quality Assurance Reviews. The review results can then be efficiently communicated with the suppliers or contractors.
During a project, the suppliers or contractors deliver documents regularly at various stages, with different revision numbers, and issue purposes. Some
documents are related to the design aspects of the project so are more critical than others, so the review is used to ensure that the most critical documentation
receives the appropriate level of scrutiny.
When document revisions enter the system, they are evaluated to determine if a quality assurance review should be initiated. If so, then a review is triggered
and notifications are sent to each of the reviewers. At the same time the review start date is set, the system calculates the due date based on the contractual
agreement with the contractor.
Each reviewer can make markups, add review comments, add general comments, reassign the review task to another person, or waive a review. Review
comments are observations made during the reviews that require additional follow up. The review comments are attached to a workflow to ensure that they
receive the right level of follow up. Each review functions independently, so they can be performed concurrently without the visibility of other reviews. However,
reviewers may see the comments and markups of all other completed reviews to help minimize duplication and promote discussion.
The number of days needed for the document review process includes the time needed for review, the consolidation of the results by the Consolidator, and to
carry out a Contractual Compliance Review (CCR). Intermediate due dates are set as configurable workflow steps and can be used to shorten the deadline for
document reviews. The overall review is not dependent upon the completion of individual reviews.
The Consolidator opens the documents and creates a consolidated layer that contains all approved comments to the contractor document. The Consolidator
can also create a new consolidated marked up file of the marked up document. A consolidator can know the progress of all the reviews from the QA Document
Review Consolidation page. Reviews that are in progress are shown as In Review and those not started are shown as Review not started against the
corresponding reviewer.
After consolidation, the review is checked for compliance. The Contractual Compliance Review (CCR) is carried out prior to the commented documents being
returned to the originating contractor.
What is the difference between primary and reference documents?
You can distinguish between the different documents that appear in a communication review or response:
Primary documents are the main subject of a review or response and require action, comment, or query.
Reference documents are included for reference or provide additional information to support a review or response, action, comment, or query.
What happens when a revised document is attached through the submittal workflow?
The QA Review Verification workflow handles the document revision attached through the submittal workflow and checks if the document revision has been
reviewed at that issue purpose.
1. If the document revision has been reviewed at the same revision and issue purpose, the system sets the return code on the document to Acknowledged
and exits the workflow.
If the document revision has not been reviewed at the same revision and issue purpose, the system directs this workflow to the next step.
2. The system checks if the issue purposes on the document are related to the current project.
If yes, the system directs this workflow to the QA Review workflow.
If no, the system does not allow the document revision to be submitted.
Perform QA review
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Review Documents and Drawings
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/647843f927e10045fe3cd382> .
1. In your To Do List, select a document review task, and select Actions 
 > Perform QA Review.
 Select WAIVE to decline the document review. You cannot waive a partly completed review.
2. Add markups and actions on the document.
3. On the Reviews tab, add the review comments.
 On the toolbar, select CREATE INTERNAL TECHNICAL QUERY to request more information from an internal resource.
4. Select COMPLETE. The review is marked as complete and is removed from your To Do List.
Main document review steps
The main stages of a document review are:
Name
Description
Assignment
A document is assigned to a reviewer based on the review plan or the document review matrix. The
documents are added to the QA Review workflow, and notifications are sent to the reviewers. A due
date is calculated, and a review task appears in the To Do List of each reviewer.
Review
The reviewer carries out the document review, and can add markups to the review. The reviewer can
also create an outgoing action or an internal technical query within the review activity. When finished,
the reviewer completes the task.
Consolidate
The consolidator collates all the completed document reviews and any related actions. The
consolidator reviews all the comments and markup layers for the document, and selects all applicable
markup layers for consolidation. The consolidator can also reject the actions from the original
document reviewers. The consolidator then sets the return code and completes the task.
Contractual Compliance Review
This is an optional step. The compliance reviewer ensures that the documents comply with the
contractual norms.
Issue
After the consolidation task is complete, the actions are initiated to the contractor. These actions
appear in the contractor's To Do List and follow an independent life cycle to track their acceptance
and closure. The rejected actions are returned to the submitter.
Get to know the QA Document Review user interface
1
The details of the review, where you can add your review and markups, as
well as select individual tabs to add and view actions or comments.
2
Creates an internal technical query in the responder's organization to gather
more information.
3
The document summary includes the name, description, and files, and
displays the responders and contributors selected for the review.
4
Select COMPLETE to finish your review. Select WAIVE to choose to ignore
the review.
Markup layers with reviews in draft or waive status are not visible to
other reviewers or the consolidator.
The system automatically selects all the markup layers of completed
reviews for rendition generation. However, if the same revision is
submitted for a review with a new issue purpose, the system
automatically selects the markup layers created for the current review
and not the layers of previous reviews. A consolidator can manage this
selection. For more information on how to configure this setting, refer to
Navigate client API.
5
The supporting information, where you can attach files and manage any
related items.
6
The files and reference objects, such as actions, added as information to
support the review.
7
Displays the recipients along with the review progress information. Reviews
that are in progress are shown as In Review and those not started are shown
as Review not started. The consolidator and reviewers are distinguished by
distinct icons.
What are the life cycle statuses of document reviews?
The following review statuses are found in the document review user interface.
Review status
Visible to
Description
CLOSED AUTOMATICALLY
Originating organization
The document review is closed automatically
because the review process is overdue or by the
consolidation process.
COMPLETED
Originating organization
The document review is complete and finished.
DRAFT
Creator in originating organization
The document review has been created as a draft,
but it has not been completed.
WAIVED
Originating organization
The document review recipient chooses to ignore
the review.
 Document reviews are not visible to contractors.
Create actions within a QA review
What is the difference between an action, an action pin, and a pin?
Actions are observations made during a review that might require additional follow-up.
Action pins are markup elements you can add to a markup layer to create an action. The action pin is linked to the newly created action and is a graphical
locator for the action.
Pins are selected objects that you add to your Pinboard, so you can quickly access these objects to manage while you are working.
What happens when an action is rejected in the QA Review process?
The QA Action Rejection workflow handles the rejected actions during the document review process. The workflow identifies the originator of the action and
sends a rejection notification.
Add actions from the View and Markup page within a QA review
For all the file types, when you click on the file hyperlink, you can add an action within a markup layer using the View and Markup page. This is a two-part
process:
You place a specific image, called an action pin, on the file and describe the needed action in a form.
SDx creates the action, which will appear in the To Do list of assigned users when the QA Review is completed. SDx then attaches a changemark to the
action pin, so that you can also see the action information on the View and Markup page.
 Action pins cannot be associated with actions created using the Actions tab on the Document Review page.
1. Select on the file hyperlink.
 For information on how to work with markups, see Marking up and comparing your files.
2. Select Markup 
, and select:
New markup to add the action pin in a new markup layer
Open for edit to add the action pin to an existing markup layer
3. From the expanded toolbar, select Image.
4. Select Image 
, and then select Image Options 
 to choose the image your organization uses for action pins.
Even if several images are available, only one can be used as an action pin. Actions are only created when you choose the image that your organization
uses for action pins.
 The default image is GreenActionPin 
. However, your administrator might have changed it using the CreateClassObjAndRelFromMarkup client
API .
5. Select anywhere in the file to place the action pin.
 To move or resize the action pin, click Select 
, and select the action pin.
6. Enter the required information in the New Outgoing Action form.
7. Select Markup 
, and select Save Markup when you are finished. Then select Markup again, and select Close Review.
The action is created, and the changemark containing the action details is added to the markup layer and associated to the action pin.
Why can't I view details of the action pin I just created?
The details of an action associated with an action pin are available in the changemark associated with the action pin. For that changemark to appear after you
create an action pin, you must first save and close the markup layer containing the action pin.
1. Select Markup 
, and select Save Markup.
2. Select Markup again, and select Close Edit.
3. Select Markup once more, and select Open for Edit or Open for Review.
The action pin details are now available.
Why can't I delete an action pin?
You must delete the action pin image and save the markup when you are finished. Deleting the associated changemark does not delete the action pin. For
more information, see Deleting action pins from the View and Markup page.
 You can also add an action interactively from the project communication feature set. For more information, see Create a technical query or an action.
Deleting action pins from the View and Markup page
 Deleting an action pin does not cancel the action. To cancel the action, follow the steps in Cancel a communication object.
You must delete the action pin image. Deleting the associated changemark does not delete the action pin.
1. Select Select 
 on the markup toolbar.
2. Select on the action pin image.
 The default image is GreenActionPin 
. However, your administrator might have changed it using the CreateClassObjAndRelFromMarkup client
API .
3. Press Delete to delete the action pin.
 You can also right-select the action pin image and select Delete.
4. Select Markup 
, and select Save Markup when you are finished.
View an action pin
From the search results list
1. Search for an action.
2. Select Actions 
 > View and Markup 
 .
From the Document Review page
1. Select the Actions tab.
2. Select Options  > View Action Pin.
 Deleting an action pin does not cancel the action. To cancel the action, follow the steps in Cancel a communication object.
Add actions from the Document Review page
1. Select 
 Create Action on the QA Document Review page.
2. Provide other details as needed.
3. Select Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the action.
You can select EDIT to update your action, and add more actions for each review until you are finished, and select COMPLETE.
Attached image files and documents appear as links, which are launched in View and Markup.
You can view the details, manage layers, update, or terminate an action by selecting Options .
Access to object hyperlinks and options can be restricted by system security rules. For more information, contact your administrator.
Add comments and reference information
1. Select EDIT to add your review comment for the document named in the Document Summary.
2. Expand Supporting Information, and select ATTACH FILES 
 to add any files as supporting information for the review.
3. Select MANAGE RELATED ITEMS to relate documents and objects as supporting information for the review.
4. Select SAVE to save your review. You can edit your review until you are finished.
For more information, see Create and edit a relationship.
If you want to create a new comment on the review, select Actions 
 > New Comment. You can add more comments for each review until you are
finished.
If you want to update or terminate a comment on the review, select Options  and select Update or Terminate.
You can add hyperlinks to the Comments box. Access to object hyperlinks and options can be restricted by system security rules. For more information,
contact your administrator.
Comments from reviewers outside of the review process are read-only.
Attached files appear as links so you can view and mark them up using View and Markup.
Consolidate reviews and prepare to issue
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Consolidate reviews and prepare to issue (modified in an update).
Before sending document reviews back to contractors, you consolidate the document reviews.
What is a document review consolidation, and when should it occur?
A consolidator can complete the consolidation step even without any review completed. The consolidator compiles all the document reviews, comments, and
actions as part of the document review consolidation workflow step.
The consolidator responds to each document review, either with approval or by creating a sub-review as an internal technical query asking for further
information. These appear in the document reviewers To Do List for rectification. The consolidator can reject or terminate any duplicate or inappropriate
actions.
The consolidator creates the consolidated review, which contains a marked-up PDF rendition file with all applicable actions. Finally, the consolidator sets the
return code on the document before selecting COMPLETE to finish, which places the consolidated document on a workflow to the recipient contractor.
Consolidate the document reviews
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Consolidate The Review Comments
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64c1401df68938110956dc2e> .
What can a consolidator do?
The consolidator can:
Determine how a review proceeds by either:
Rejecting the review and sending any rejected actions back to the reviewer for rework.
For any rejected actions, the state of the review and actions are set to DRAFT REJECTED. If the rejected actions are associated with a markup
layer and that layer is selected to return to the contractor, warnings are given that the selected layer contains unapproved actions. The consolidator
cannot complete the process to issue the document reviews to the contractor until the action is resolved, deleted, or moved to a markup layer that
is not selected to return to the contractor.
Completing the consolidation of reviews, actions and comments and issuing them to the contractor.
Add one or more users as document reviewers during the review process.
When a consolidator adds a user, who has a document receipt notification step, as a reviewer, the document receipt notification step is
replaced by a review step.
The new review step is assigned the same target date as that of the original step. If the review is already overdue, the added reviewer will
receive an overdue step.
View and edit any reviews, actions, and comments in a DRAFT or RESERVED state, prior to submission for contract compliance review.
View all supporting documentation for every review, action, or comment.
View the markup layers and select which markup layers you want to associate with a specific action.
Make changes to the reviews, actions, or comments and then reconsolidate if a document review is rejected after a contractual compliance review.
Send technical queries if more information is required on an action or comment.
What happens when a consolidator requests additional reviewers for a document that is in review?
The QA Append Reviewer workflow handles the request from a consolidator to add one or more reviewers to a document that is in review. The workflow
creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the user's To Do list.
1. In your To Do List, select a consolidation review task, and select Actions 
 > Perform QA Review Consolidation.
 The consolidator cannot edit any document reviewer's reviews.
a. In your To Do List, select a document review task for Perform QA Review Consolidation step.
b. Select Actions 
 > Add Reviewer.
c. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then select Finish.
2. On the Reviews tab
a. Review all the markups, actions, and comments.
 You can select the markup layers that you want to send with an action, so that only the relevant markup layers are attached to the action.
b. Add consolidation review comments.
3. Accept or reject the review and any actions from the review.
 Additional comments or markups may be available from other contributors outside of the review. Select Show contributor markups to see them.
4. On the toolbar, select Document Return Code for the consolidated review. This notifies the originating company of the return status that the consolidator
has set for the review, such as Acknowledged, Approved, Rejected, Reviewed, or Reviewed with Comments.
 Select CREATE INTERNAL TECHNICAL QUERY to request more information from an internal resource.
5. Select COMPLETE. The consolidated review is marked as complete and is removed from your To Do List.
 The Validate Review Supporting Information step is executed when you complete the consolidation step to check if the recipient organization
has access to all the reference documents. If the validation fails, you have to either remove the reference document or update the collaborating
organization on the document to complete the step.
How to identify review progress as a consolidator?
When a QA review is completed or waived, it reflects in the review progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number
of reviewers to the document. For example, If a document has two reviewers assigned:
Reviewer action
Review progress
Review is started but not completed by any of the reviewers
The review progress is 0.
One of the reviewers completes or waives off the review
The review progress is 50%.
You add a new reviewer
The review progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of reviewers are three and
only one of them has completed the review)
You reassign a reviewer who has already completed the reviews
The review progress is calculated accordingly.
All the reviewers complete the review task
The review progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
What are Contributor markups?
Documents can also be marked up by users who are not assigned to a QA review task. The users who create the markups and comments outside the context
of QA review are called as contributors.
The reason why a contributor might want to create markups outside of a QA review is to provide general observations that are shared as comments/markups
and the markups provided in other context like technical queries or actions context.

1
The details of the document under review, where you can select individual
tabs to see the reviews, actions, and comments to help form a consolidated
review.
2

Select CREATE INTERNAL TECHNICAL QUERY to create an internal
technical query in the consolidator's organization to gather more information.
3
The document summary includes the name, description, revision of the
document being consolidated, and the latest issue purposes, and identifies if
any markups contain rejected actions.
4
The files and comments attached to the consolidated review along with the
PDF renditions, categorized based on the issue purpose, for the
consolidation. Displays the status of reviewers and contributors.
5
Select COMPLETE to finish your consolidated review.
6
The primary and reference actions. Select EDIT to modify an action or select
Options  to manage layers, update, or terminate an action. Warnings
display if an action or action pin in a markup layer is rejected or reserved.
7
Displays the recipients along with the review progress information. Reviews
that are in progress are shown as In Review and those not started are shown
as Review not started. The consolidator and reviewers are distinguished by
distinct icons.
Review the response for contractual compliance
1. In your To Do List, select the technical query to check for compliance against the contract.
2. Select Actions > Perform Compliance Response Review.
3. Review the response for contractual compliance.
 You can select the file hyperlink under ALL FILES tab to open the file in its native format. If you want to view the markups associated with that file:
i. Select  > Manage Response Markup Layers next to the response object.
ii. Select VIEW MARKUP to open the file in the View and Markup Response window.
 If the contractual compliance review is approved, the technical query response state is set to ISSUED. If not approved, the state is set to REJECTED.
Manage markup layers
While preparing actions or responses, any markup layer created is related to the communication object. This relationship is used to identify the context and
preselect the layers created in that context.
For the layers created outside the current scope, the context type does not exist and the layers do not get autoselected.
If a responder or a reviewer waives a review, the association between the markup and the communication object is delinked.
If a consolidator completes a response or review while it is in a draft state, the association between the markup and the communication object is
delinked.
Manage document markup layers
In the document review consolidation, you can manage which markup layers you want to associate with a specific document, so only the relevant markup
layers are attached to the document. This provides you with control of how the consolidated rendition PDF file is sent with the document review.
Familiarize yourself with the Manage Document Markup Layers page
1
The number in the gray panel indicates the total number of layers present in
the context.
The number in the green panel indicates the number of layers selected.
2
The context type from where the layer was created.
3
The action pin. The pin that is being managed is highlighted in blue.
From a Document Review Consolidation
1. Select Documents Summary  > Manage Layers on a document.
2. In Manage Document Markup Layers, select a file name in the left summary pane.
Select this
To do this
Each markup layer
To see more details in the right pane, which displays the context, any text
markups created by the user, any pinned actions, and the rendition
relationships generated by the system.
RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS check box
To enable the actions raised in reviews using the action pins to use the
same rendition as the document. The RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS
check box sets the relation between the actions created from the action
pins and the markup.
Clear the RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS check box
To manage the markup layers on the pinned actions.
 The system remembers the state of the RELATE LAYERS TO ACTIONS checkbox for only the current review, on exiting the Manage Document
Markup Layers dialog box.
You can also manage layers on an individual action to include a subset of all the layers available. Once the review is closed out, the actions are put in a
workflow which generates the renditions for the actions.
If the layer has the context from where the layer is created, the context type is displayed for each markup layer.
If the layer is created from the current review, the context type is highlighted in blue and appears on top of the other layers.
A consolidator can select all the context layers created from the current review by selecting the SELECT LAYERS BY CONTEXT check box.
Manage action markup layers
You can manage which markup layers you want to associate with a specific action, so only the relevant markup layers are attached to the action. This provides
you with control of how the consolidated rendition PDF file is sent with the action.
From an individual action
1. Select the Actions tab.
2. Select 
 > Manage Markup Layers on an action.
3. In the Manage Action Markup Layers dialog box, select a file name in the left summary pane.
 The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet been generated or because the base Hexagon file is
not available. See Configure WebGL renditions for details of how to generate the web file. Contact your administrator if the base file is unavailable.
4. In the middle selection pane, select each markup layer to see the relevant information on the right summary pane. The right pane displays the context,
any text markups created by the user, any pinned actions, and the rendition relationships generated by the system.
If the layer has the context from where the layer is created, the context type is displayed for each markup layer.
If the layer is created from the current review, then the context type is highlighted in blue and appears on top of the other layers.
A consolidator can select all the context layers created from the current review by selecting the SELECT LAYERS BY CONTEXT check box.
Respond to document reviews
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Respond to Document Reviews
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64eb20613232a68262d3d14f>
Once the document reviews are issued, the contractors respond to document renditions specific to their contractor organization. Contractors only see reviews,
actions, and document renditions, including markups, for their organization and do not see other organization or contractors' responses.
If the contractor decides to respond, they make the requested updates or changes from the owner, resubmit the updates, and the document review
process starts again.
If the contractor decides not to respond, they close the document review.
 Renditions are generated when the project communications are issued and are presented to the recipient instead of the original documents.
Renditions contain the markup layers identified for the relevant communication during the consolidation review.
View the status of responses
To view the status of responses to your technical query, select Actions 
 > Communication Management.
 Select 
 next to a document to show the associated files. The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet
been generated or because the base Hexagon file is not available. See Configure WebGL renditions for details of how to generate the web file. Contact your
administrator if the base file is unavailable.
Communicate with stakeholders in your project
You can create outgoing, incoming, and internal queries or actions to communicate with your company and contractor project teams. You can also interactively
assign tasks to team members.
Technical queries are managed communications that provide a consistent path and tracking to ensure that the query and any responses are completed in a
timely manner. For more information about technical queries, see Learn more about technical queries.
Actions are observations made during a review that might require additional followup.
Track your technical queries and actions
How do the technical query processes work?
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Introduction to Technical Queries
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65c3411afc7d6ca3853d90d0>
Technical queries are used by companies and their contractors to ask each other questions or to gather more information.
What does the technical query status mean?
Status
Description
RESERVED
The technical query has been created (draft).
ISSUED
The technical query has been sent to the receiver.
RESPONDED
The technical query has been answered.
REJECTED
The technical query has been rejected.
RESPONSE REJECTED
The technical query's response has been rejected.
RECEIPT ACCEPTED
The technical query has been accepted.
 This status is not applicable for the internal technical query.
INITIATED
The technical query has been submitted, but not yet issued.
 This status is not applicable for the internal technical query.
COMPLETED
The technical query has been closed.
Incoming/Outgoing technical query
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Incoming/outgoing Technical Queries
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/67dd4daf3e379c93c931bfc3>
An incoming technical query is initiated by the contractor to request information from the owner.
An outgoing technical query is initiated by the owner to request information from the contractor.
 Your system administrator may modify the workflow so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures
described below.
Internal technical query
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Internal Technical Queries
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/6633729483ad7fd9d82136e5>
An internal technical query is initiated within an organization to ask questions of other disciplines or departments. You can create an internal technical query
on documents or tags or as part of the Quality Assurance Review process.
 Your system administrator may modify the workflow so that the steps differ from those shown. The steps are still carried out using the procedures
described below.
Learn more about technical queries
Technical queries are managed communications between appropriate stakeholders that provide a consistent path and tracking to ensure that the query and
any responses are completed in a timely manner.
Technical queries between stakeholders:
Require a response within a contractually-specified timeframe
May be related to one or more documents or tags
May have responses from multiple responders
A technical query is a report raised on a document, process, or equipment by an engineer or any other authorized user to report an item for further action, such
as gathering information, notification of a change of design, maintenance, discussion, and so on. The technical query is attached to a defined workflow
definition asynchronously to distribute the query details to contractors and their technical experts or internally to technical experts within the organization.
Each technical query contains all the relevant information, images, or documents for the item or process being reported, which can be opened directly from
within the technical query. When you create a technical query, PDF renditions are generated and issued to the recipient or contractor instead of the original
documents. The technical query can also be configured so that the recipients must respond within a designated time frame to meet any contractual obligations.
How does the action process work?
Actions are used by companies and their contractors to define and track observations made during a review that require additional followup.
What does the action status mean?
The following statuses apply to outgoing and internal actions.
Status
Description
RESERVED
The action has been created (draft).
ISSUED
The action has been sent to the receiver.
RESPONDED
The action has been responded.
REJECTED
The action has been rejected.
RESPONSE REJECTED
The action's response has been rejected.
COMPLETED
The action has been closed.
Internal actions
Internal actions are initiated by a user to other users of disciplines or departments within an organization to perform an action, such as update or validate
engineering documents.
Steps
Description
Create an internal action
Create the internal action from the project communication feature set.
Add content, markup and comments
This step appears in the To Do List of the user that created the action.
Select the step and then select Actions > Prepare Content to complete and issue the action.
Perform action and respond
The selected responders receive the request in their To Do List.
Select Respond to action.
Review the response
The author reviews the response. If all is OK, they close the action. If more information is required, the
action is automatically revised , then the author is asked to prepare and re-issue the action.
Select Review action response.
Outgoing actions
Outgoing actions are initiated by the originating company to the contractor, to perform an action, such as update or validate engineering documents. An
outgoing action can be created as part of the Quality Assurance Review process or interactively from the project communication feature set.
Steps
Description
Create an outgoing action
Create the outgoing action from the project communication feature set.
 You can also create an outgoing action from the Document Review page.
Add content, markup and comments
This step appears in the To Do List of the user that created the action.
Select the step and then select Actions > Prepare Content to complete and issue the action.
Perform compliance review
This is an optional step and is controlled by an option on the Response Control tab when you create
the action.
Select Perform Compliance Review.
You can reject the action during the review and send it back to the author if it requires rework.
You can reassign this step, if necessary.
Perform action and respond
The selected responders receive the request in their To Do List.
Select Respond to action.
Review the response
The author reviews the response. If all is OK, they close the action. If more information is required, the
action is automatically revised , then the author is asked to prepare and re-issue the action.
Select Review action response.
Learn more about actions
Actions are observations made during a review that might require additional follow-up. These actions have a standard workflow which allows users to assign,
manage, and track actions with ease.
What do you want to do?
Create a technical query or an action
Export technical query contents
Add content, markups, and comments
Review for contractual compliance
Initial review (applicable for technical queries only)
Respond to technical queries or actions
Consolidate responses (applicable for technical queries only)
Review the response for contractual compliance (applicable for technical queries only)
Rework the response (applicable for technical queries only)
Review the response
Cancel a communication object
Create a technical query or an action
Create a technical query
What types of technical queries can I send and receive?
The three types of technical queries are:
Incoming Technical Query – Initiated by the contractor to request information from the originating company.
Outgoing Technical Query – Initiated by the originating company to request information from the contractor.
Internal Technical Query – Initiated within the organization, company, or contractor to request information across departments or disciplines.
In a project communication feature set, select the type of technical query you want to send.
1. Select the Contract, To organization, Security Code, and either the To role or To user.
2. Select Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the technical query.
The From CC users get a technical query issue notification step and the To CC users get a technical query receipt notification step. To acknowledge the
step, select Actions 
 > Acknowledge. To know more about how to manage the users displayed in the From CC and the To CC boxes available in the
New Technical Query window, see Create distribution lists.
The Is contractual compliance review required? option (selected by default for outgoing and incoming technical queries) in the Response Control
tab sends the technical query for a compliance review before issuing the technical query to the contractor.
Create an action
What happens when an action is created? 
The action is automatically attached to the Action workflow, and a notification is sent to the recipient's To Do List.
1. Select the required communication type feature set, and click Create Action.
 You can also select an object, and click Actions 
 > Create [Type] Action.
2. Provide other details as needed.
3. Click Next and browse to any external files you want to relate to the action.
The From CC users get an Action issue notification step and the To CC users get an Action receipt notification step. To acknowledge the step, select
Actions 
 > Acknowledge. To know more about how to manage the users displayed in the From CC and the To CC boxes available in the New
Action window, see Create distribution lists.
Selecting the Is contractual compliance review required? option in the Response Control tab sends the outgoing action for a compliance review
before issuing the action to the contractor.
Selecting the Follow up required option in the Response Control tab sends a Review responses step to the originator.
Update a technical query or an action
1. Select Actions 
 > Update.
 From the Prepare Content page, select the Update button.
2. Update the information for the technical query or the action, as required.
You can update a technical query or an action when it is in the RESERVED state.
The due date is defined by the contract on outgoing actions.
Export technical query contents
Additional workflow steps generate a summary report when a technical query is exported and then generate the report as a PDF. This is attached to the
workflow before the technical query is submitted, typically, to contractors who want to share the information outside of the system.
In the Web Client, locate a reserved technical query and use the Technical Query Letter Preview action to view the letter.
In the Web Client, the Export technical query contents command generates a Zip file that includes a similar Microsoft Excel report. This Excel report lists, in
a separate row, each attached document, the attached files, and the location of the separate folders for each document. The separate folders contain the files
attached to the documents.
Add content, markups, and comments
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Add content, markups, and comments (modified in an update).
To add content, markups, and comments, select the Add content, markup and comments step in your To Do List.
Manage related information
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Relationship Builder.
3. Select the Search tab, and search for the document(s) or tags to be attached.
4. Select one or more documents, and select RELATE.
 You can also relate other technical queries or actions, and transmittals to a technical query or an action.
Relate markups
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Manage Markup Layers.
3. In the Manage Technical Query Markup Layers or Manage Action Markup Layers dialog box, select a file name in the left summary pane.
 The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet been generated or because the base Hexagon file is
not available. See Configure WebGL renditions for details of how to generate the web file. Contact your administrator if the base file is unavailable.
4. In the middle selection pane, select each markup layer you want to relate. The right pane displays the context, any text markups created by the user, any
pinned actions, and the rendition relationships generated by the system.
Add comments and issue
1. Select Actions 
 > Prepare Content.
2. In the Prepare Content dialog box, select Update, and add your comments and message for the recipient in the DETAILS section. The issue state
becomes ISSUED.
To add new markups, select on file hyperlink to launch the View and Markup page.
When you issue the item, any documents and attached files along with the selected markup layers are converted into PDF renditions.
If the rendition does not generate correctly, contact your system administrator to fix the problem.
Review for contractual compliance in originating organization
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, seeReview for contractual compliance in originating organization (modified in an update).
What is a contractual compliance review, and what does it involve?
A contractual compliance review can be configured to review any changes to contractual deliverables and actions that extend outside of the company. This
ensures that all communications and requests that are submitted to the contractor are within the scope of the contract terms and can be issued to the supplier
or contractor for implementation.
The consolidator creates a communication with a concise set of actions that can be sent to the contractor, and the contractual compliance reviewer checks the
communication contents, technical queries, and actions for compliance with the contract. The contractual compliance reviewer receives the communication in
their To Do List and reviews each of the related changes, markups and actions. The contractual compliance reviewer can reject any of the communication
contents, technical queries, or actions and may make additional actions and markups identifying any changes.
Any rejected communications, technical queries, or actions are returned to the consolidator using a dedicated rejection workflow. The consolidator can alter or
request alterations to any rejected communications, technical queries or actions before reconsolidating the actions and markups.
1. In your To Do List, select the technical query or the action to check for compliance against the contract before being issued.
2. Select Actions > Perform Compliance Review.
3. Review the documents and files for contractual compliance.
 You can select the file hyperlink under ALL FILES tab to open the file in its native format. If you want to view the markups associated with that file:
i. Select Manage Markup Layers next to the communication object.
ii. Select VIEW MARKUP to open the file in the View and Markup window.
 If the contractual compliance review is approved, the technical query or the action issue state is set to ISSUED. If not approved, the state is set to
REJECTED.
Initial review
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Initial review (modified in an update).
After the technical queries are issued, they appear in the initial recipient's To Do List of the target organization.
As an initial recipient, you can:
Open the technical query and initiate the response.
Identify other responders and a consolidator. However, if you are the only one to respond, you are also the consolidator.
Identify and manage the markup layers of the marked up technical query renditions and any reference documents.
Reject the technical query. The rejected technical query is sent back to the originator.
Perform the initial response
1. Select the Initial review step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions > Perform Initial Response.
Identify additional responders
As the initial recipient of the technical query, select additional responders and a consolidator for the technical query.
Select Identify Responders on the Perform Initial Response page.
 Select Is contractual compliance review required for response? option to send the technical query response for a contractual compliance review,
before sending the technical query back to the originating organization.
Respond to technical queries or actions
Respond to a technical query

with a message
What is the difference between primary and reference documents?
You can distinguish between the different documents that appear in a communication review or response:
Primary documents are the main subject of a review or response and require action, comment, or query.
Reference documents are included for reference or provide additional information to support a review or response, action, comment, or query.
What does a contractual compliance review involve for a technical query response?
A contractual compliance review, when configured, can review the technical query responses that may have changes to contractual deliverables or actions that
extend outside of the company. This ensures that all technical queries are completed within the scope of the contract terms and can be returned to the
originator.
The contractual compliance reviewer receives the technical query in their To Do List and checks the content of the technical query for compliance with the
contract. The contractual compliance reviewer can reject a technical query. Any rejected technical query is returned to the consolidator to make additional edits
or comments to the documentation that require changes to the response.
Get to know the technical query response user interface
1
The details of the technical query, showing the responders, supplier's
organization, current issue status, and due date.
2
The files, objects, and any previous technical queries added as related
information to the technical query.
3
The responders selected to respond to the technical query.
4
The details of one of the responses already completed, including any
markups, actions, and comments.
5
The files and primary reference documents added as information to support
the technical query response.
 Select 
 next to a document to show the associated files. The 
 icon indicates that the file cannot be viewed, either because the web file has not yet
been generated or because the base Hexagon file is not available. See Configure WebGL renditions for details of how to generate the web file. Contact your
administrator if the base file is unavailable.
After the Initial review step:
Responders can comment, update, or mark up document renditions specific to their organization.
If a responder does not want to respond or no longer needs to respond, they can waive the technical query response. For example, a responder might
waive the technical query response if another responder has already made the same comment.
Each responder can save their technical query response and add supporting information as files or documents before completing the response.
The response consolidator reviews the actions, comments, and related files from the responders and submits the consolidated technical query response
containing the renditions back to the originator.
The response consolidator can complete and reject any technical query or response and return back the response to each responder or the originating
organization with a message for rework.
When the originating organization receives the consolidated technical query response, they can either close the technical query or reject the response.
If the originating organization rejects the technical query response, the technical query can be revised with any additional information or renditions and
resubmitted.
If a contractual compliance review is configured, a rejected response is returned to the consolidator.
Renditions are generated when the project communications are issued and are presented to the recipient instead of the original documents. Renditions
contain the markup layers identified for the relevant communication during the consolidation review.
If the rendition does not generate correctly, contact your system administrator to fix the problem.
To add new markups, select on file hyperlink to launch the View and Markup page.
Create a response
As a responder, create a technical query response.
Select 
 > EDIT in the response page.

Select 
 > Manage Markup Layers.
Manage response markup layers
As a consolidator, identify and manage the markup layers of the marked up technical query renditions and any reference documents on the responses. The
consolidated response contains all comments, reference documents, and files from other responders.

2. Select MANAGE RELATED ITEMS to relate documents and objects as supporting information for the review.

Select Actions

All responders can return to the previous object in a results grid by Selecting PARENT SCREEN.
View a brief view of the workflow by Selecting Actions

 Responders can only respond to the technical query by Selecting the LATEST revision.

 > Respond to TQ to complete the responses.

To add new markups, select on file hyperlink to launch the View and Markup page.
For information on how to manage the markup layers and relate documents to the response, see Respond to a technical query above.
You can reject an action but must provide a reason for the rejection. When the originator is notified that the action is rejected, they must decide to either
update the action or cancel it.
Consolidate responses
1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
 > Consolidate Responses.
The consolidator can consolidate all the reference documents and attached files provided by other responders using Add to Consolidate in the
Consolidate Responses dialog box. Any response comments provided by other responders have to be manually copied to the consolidated response
by the consolidator.
The consolidator can remove any reference documents and directly attached files provided by other responders from the consolidated response using
Remove from Consolidate in the Consolidate Responses dialog box.
All the markups created by other responders on the reference documents and directly attached files are selected by default in the consolidated response.
The consolidator has the option to deselect any of these markups from the consolidated response and can also create new markups.
The consolidator can reassign the Consolidate responses step to another user.
Select Actions 
 > Reassign.
The consolidator has to rework the technical query response if the compliance reviewer rejects the consolidated response. For more information, see
Rework the response.
What happens when a consolidator requests additional responders?
The Append Responder workflow handles the request from a consolidator to add one or more responders to the current technical query workflow. The
workflow creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the user's To Do list.
1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Add Responder.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then click Finish.
How to identify step progress as a consolidator?
When a Prepare response step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number
of responders to the technical query. For example, If a technical query has two responders assigned:
Responder action
Step progress
Response is started but not completed by any of the responders
The step progress is 0.
One of the responders completes the response
The step progress is 50%.
You add a new responder
The step progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of responders is
three and only one of them has completed the response)
You reassign the Prepare Response step to a responder who has already completed
the technical query
The step progress of incoming or outgoing technical query is
calculated accordingly.
All the responders complete the prepare response task
The step progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
How to know the status of documents ready to be consolidated?
As a consolidator, when in your To Do List page, you can now check the status of each document and its progress.

3. Review the response for contractual compliance.
 You can select the file hyperlink under ALL FILES tab to open the file in its native format. If you want to view the markups associated with that file:

ii. Select VIEW MARKUP to open the file in the View and Markup Response window.
 If the contractual compliance review is approved, the technical query response state is set to ISSUED. If not approved, the state is set to REJECTED.

1. Select the Consolidate responses step in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Add Responder.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then click Finish.

When a Prepare response step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the number

Cancel a communication object
Cancel a technical query
1. Select Actions 
 > Edit > Cancel Technical Query.
2. Enter the reason for canceling the technical query in the Cancel Object(s) dialog box.
Cancel an action
What happens when an action is canceled?
When you cancel an action object, the action is set to the Canceled state and attached to a cancel workflow. The receiver and sender of the action are notified
that the action is canceled.
1. Click Actions 
 > Edit > Cancel Action.
2. Enter the reason for cancelling the action in the Cancel Object(s) dialog box.
 Any user (except Business Administrator and SDx Administrator) from the originating organization can cancel an action when it is in the ISSUED state.
Terminate an action
When you terminate an action object, the action gets deleted from the database and cannot be queried.
1. Click Actions 
 > Terminate.
2. Enter the reason for terminating the action in the Terminate Object(s) dialog box.
 Any user (except Business Administrator and SDx Administrator) from the originating organization can terminate an action when it is in the
RESERVED or DRAFT REJECTED state.
Create an Internal Review workflow
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Create an Internal Review workflow (modified in an update).
As an engineer working on a document, you can send it to other engineers within the organization for review using the internal review workflow. There can be
one or more reviewers but only one approver. Once the review process is complete, the working documents will be signed off before sending it to other
organizations.
You can create an internal review action from the Internal Communication feature set or from the Actions menu of one or more working documents. Once
the action is created, it gets attached to the Internal Review workflow.
Step
Description
Branch based on condition
In the New Internal Review Action,
Select SAVE AS DRAFT to attach the documents later.
or
Select FINISH to send the documents for review.
Add content, markups, and comments
Select Manage related files or Manage Documents to add files or documents. For more information,
see Managing documents and files.
Select Options  to
Use the EDIT ONLINE option to edit the files online using Office 365 and OneDrive. For more
information, see Edit files online using Microsoft OneDrive.
Use the EDIT FILES option to edit the files on your computer. For more information, see Edit files.
Select the file hyperlink to add markups or comments.
This step is completed by the submitter of the internal review workflow.
 The workflow step response date is calculated based on the due date entered. For example, if the
due date is 5 days from the current date, the system adds 5 days to the current date and sets the result as
the workflow step response date, excluding any non-working days. If the Due date value is blank, then the
current date is set as the workflow step response date.
Set milestone on documents
The system creates a document milestone and sets the milestone status to IN REVIEW.
 When an internal review action is in progress, the Edit Files option is hidden from the document
actions list. This prevents unauthorized modifications to the document while it is under review. Once the
review action is completed, the Edit Files option is available. However, if an internal review is rejected, a
document cannot be edited until the internal review action is revised. In the rejection state, the internal review
action is revised, and the submitter receives a Review Rejection and Re-Issue Required workflow steps. In
a Re-Issue Required workflow step, the documents can be edited once again. You can then make any
necessary changes to the document.
Review the document
The reviewer receives the request in the To Do List. Like in the Add content, markups, and comments step,
the reviewer reviews the document and gives feedback.
Approve the changes
The approver reviews the documents and other review comments. If satisfied, the approver consolidates the
changes, approves this step, and completes the workflow. The system sets the milestone status to REVIEW
COMPLETED.
If the approver rejects this step, a Review Rejection step is sent to the submitter. The system sets the
milestone status to REVIEW REJECTED.
PDF renditions of the documents are generated, including markups. If a PDF rendition fails to generate, the
system directs the workflow to the Correct failure step. For more information on PDF rendition errors, see
Correct PDF rendition generation failures.
Review Rejection
If Reissue is selected, the system revises the review action. Any documents, tags, files, markups on the
existing object are copied on to the new object. Based on the feedback, the submitter then updates the
content as mentioned in the Add content, markups, and comments step above.
If Close is selected, the system exits the workflow.
Complete the workflow
The workflow ends and the working document is signed off. The submitter receives a completion notice
(information step) in the To Do List.
Details of the internal review workflow steps:
1. In the New Internal Review Action:
Type or select the required information.
Navigate to the next page and search for the working documents.
 The current configuration-related documents are returned in the search results, if the Query by selected configurations only option is
enabled.
Select one or more working documents that you want to be reviewed, and select ADD.
 You can edit the value of the issue purpose for each selected document. However, the value cannot be blank.
If the submitter selects SAVE AS DRAFT, the system sends an Add content, markups, and comments step to the submitter's To Do List.
If the submitter selects FINISH, the system sends a review step to the selected recipients.
 The system validates that the submitter(s) are not reviewer(s). However, submitter(s) and reviewer(s) can both be approver(s).
2. When the submitter selects FINISH in the New Internal Review Action or completes the Add content step:
The system sets the recipient to the Reviewer and Approver specified in the create dialog.
The system sets the workflow step target date to the Due date specified in the create dialog.
3. The reviewer gets a step in the To Do List. If the reviewer updates the document, the approver sees those changes in the Approver step.
If there are multiple reviewers, the approver must consolidate the changes and can suppress the comments.
Select WAIVE to decline the document review.
 This is an optional step. Reviewer selection is not mandatory in the internal review workflow process.
4. The approver reviews the document. The approver can see any changes made to the document by the reviewers.
If the approver approves the document, it is signed off and the internal review process ends.
 If the approver completes the step, the system closes any pending review steps in the reviewers' To Do List.
If the approver rejects the step, the system sends a Review Rejection step to the submitter.
5. In the Review Rejection step:
If the submitter selects Reissue, the system sends a Revise Request step to the submitter's To Do List. The workflow continues from step 2.
If the submitter selects Close, the system closes the internal review workflow process.
For more information, see Internal review.
What does the internal review action status mean?
Review status
Icon
Description
RESERVED/DRAFT
Review action has been created as a draft, but it is not
ready for review.
ISSUED
Review action was issued to the internal user.
REJECTED
Review action was rejected by the internal user.
COMPLETED
Review action was completed.
SUPERSEDED
Review action was revised.
See how to create an Internal Review Action
See how the Internal Review Action is reviewed and approved
See how the submitter acknowledges and completes the Internal Review Action
What happens when an approver requests additional reviewers for a document that is in review?
The Append Internal Reviewer workflow handles the request from an approver to add one or more reviewers to a document that is in review. The workflow
creates a review object for the appended reviewer and adds a task in the user's To Do list.
Add an Internal Reviewer
1. In your To Do List, select a document review task for Approve the Review step.
2. Select Actions 
 > Add Internal Reviewer.
3. In the Add Recipients page, select the Role and User, and then select Finish.
How to identify step progress as a Approver?
When a Perform Internal Review step is completed, it reflects in the step progress column as a percentage. The percentage of the progress depends on the
number of reviewers to the internal review action. For example, If an internal review action has two reviewers assigned:
Reviewer action
Step progress
Review is started but not completed by any of the reviewers
The review progress is 0.
One of the reviewers completes or waives off the review
The review progress is 50%.
You add a new reviewer
The review progress is 33.33%. (Now the number of reviewers are three
and only one of them has completed the review)
You reassign a reviewer who has already completed the reviews
The review progress is calculated accordingly.
All the reviewers complete the review task
The review progress is completed and is shown as 100%.
Create an Internal Draft workflow
As an engineer working on a document, you can send it to other engineers within the organization to update the working documents using the Internal Draft
workflow. Once the draft workflow is complete, the document can be signed off manually before loading it into the SDx system through the submittal process or
can be attached to the Internal Review Action workflow where it will be auto-signed off.
Internal Draft workflow is to get the working documents modified by multiple engineers electronically. For example, P&ID drawings developed by engineers can
be sent to the CAD specialists for drafting. With this workflow, traceability and progress of the documents is ensured.
