updated.
Select the document, and then select Actions > Attach Workflow > HEX
Internal Draft Workflow.

comments
Attach any additional files, create markups on the files, and provide
comments.
Select one or more editors and set the due date on the form. Due date is the
time by which an editor should ideally respond.
Select Actions 
 > Document Version > Edit > Manage Files to add the
files.
Select Actions 
 > Document Version > New Comment to add the
comments.
Select Actions 
 > Document Version > 
 to add the markups.
Select Actions 
 > Document Version > Assign Editors.
Submitter or reassigned user
Select Actions 
 > Task > Complete to complete the internal draft
workflow.
Select Actions 
 > Task > Reassign to reassign this step to another user.
Modify the document
Update the document by performing actions similar to the Add content, markups,
and comments step.
 You must claim this step before you can modify the document. Once the
step is claimed, the unclaimed steps in the To Do List of other recipients are
terminated by the system.
One or more editors
Review the changes
Review the response. If satisfied, complete the workflow.
Submitter
Resend
Send the document back to the editor, if additional changes to the document are
required.
Submitter
Reassign
Send the updated document to another user using the Internal Draft workflow.
Submitter
Complete the workflow
End the workflow.
Submitter
Details of the internal draft workflow steps:
The first step of the internal draft workflow sends an Add content, markups, and comments step to the submitter's To Do List.
On completing the Add content step:
The system validates that at least one editor has been selected and they are not the same as the submitter.
The system sets the recipients to the editors specified in the Assign Editors dialog box.
The system sets the workflow step target date to the Due Date specified in the Assign Editors dialog box.
One or more editors get a step in the To Do List. The editor claims the step, updates the document, and sends it back to the submitter.
The submitter reviews the modifications made to the document.
If the submitter accepts the modifications, the internal draft workflow ends.
If the submitter rejects the modifications, the system sends an Add content, markups, and comments step to the submitter's To Do List.
If the submitter reassigns the document to another user, the system routes the document to the selected recipient.
For more information, see Internal draft.
Create distribution lists
As a company or a contractor user, you can define a configuration-controlled distribution list with a list of recipients who are to be assigned a workflow step by
the system or receive notifications. These lists come in handy while creating technical queries, actions, or transmittals.
To create an internal distribution list (From CC users):
1. Select the Internal Communication feature set, and select Create Internal Distribution List.
2. In the New Internal Distribution List screen, type a name and description, and select the users based on the organization.
To create an external distribution list (To CC users):
1. Select the Outgoing Communication feature set, and select Create External Distribution List.
2. In the New External Distribution List screen, type a name and description, and select the users based on the organization.
Any user with a Business Administrator role can view all the distribution lists related to a project.
A user who created the distribution list can update or terminate the list. Any user with a Business Administrator or Global Document Control role can
update or terminate a distribution list created by other users.
Using work packages on a project
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®| Work Packages and Types of Work Packages
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/65e0b3cdf554b733a09813e6>
Work packages help organize, plan, schedule, and manage your work by grouping data, documents, and tags into manageable data sets. Users with a
business administrator or document controller role can create work packages.
Groups of data can be created for different purposes, such as getting an overview of the documents' status, measuring or getting a progress rollup, and more.
For information on how to create a work package, see Using work packages for plant operations.
The following lists some common types of work packages used in a project:
Type of work package
Details
Work Breakdown Structure (WBS) Work Packages
The WBS work package is a
hierarchical grouping of project work
elements that help organize and
subdivide the scope of work in a
project. This grouping is typically
geographically based, while the PBS is
functionally based.
The WBS packages are typically used
for handing over deliverables to the
client or managing engineering
progress.
The WBS packages are created by a
user with the Business Administrator
role.
Cost Breakdown Structure (CBS) Work Packages
The CBS work package is a
hierarchical grouping of the project
work elements that helps organize and
subdivide the scope of work in a
project.
EcoSys is a web-based platform that
provides the deepest capabilities to
support the complete spectrum of
solutions within the project controls
discipline – from project setup, through
budgeting, forecasting, and
performance reporting.
The CBS packages are typically used
for managing the engineering progress
within the SDx-EcoSys interface. The
interface between SDx and EcoSys
allows EcoSys users to better control
the progress based on the progress or
milestone data of the project
documents.
If the Is Progress Item? check box is
selected when creating the CBS
packages, they are referred to as the
progress item work packages. The
documents can be related to the
progress item work packages, and the
progress can be calculated based on
the issue purpose. With the documents
related to the Progress Items (‘Tasks’),
progress of the task can be calculated
based on weight factors (EcoSys) and
document milestones (SDx). EcoSys
users can view the documents as
stored in SDx.
The CBS packages are created and
maintained by EcoSys, and fed into
SDx using the SDx-EcoSys interface.
The CBS packages can also be
created, updated, and terminated by a
user with the SDx Administrator role in
the SDx system.
Front End Engineering Design (FEED) Work Packages
Groups together the documents
created and managed during the FEED
phase of a project. Once the project
scope of work is agreed, the FEED
work package structure is loaded into
the system by a user with the Business
Administrator role. The FEED work
package can be seen as an electronic
version of the FEED design books,
containing all documentation handed
over from contractor to plant owner at
the end of the engineering FEED
phase.
Vendor Work Packages
Groups together vendor deliverables
as they are loaded into the system.
Grouping vendor documents to specific
vendor work packages make
navigation to specific vendor
documents easy and facilitates efficient
vendor document management.
The documents are added to the
vendor packages during the document
submittal process.
General Work Packages
General grouping function to group
deliverables, such as document
revisions, tags, communication items,
and so on for unspecified engineering
reasons. These packages can be
created by any user to group items
together to help manage their work.
For plant operations, work packages support the planning workflow for maintenance or inspection work. For example, SDx makes it easy to find and then
add documents and information that are needed for maintenance work order created in SAP.
The CBS work package is typically cost based, while the WBS work package is geography based, and the PBS work package is function based.
The WBS work package is used often by 3D Design and Construction. The breakdown is based on geographical location. The plant is broken down
into construction areas or work areas. The construction plans/orders are based on this breakdown. The accessibility of the work areas is critical.
The PBS is typically broken down into Plant - Area – Unit, and sometimes extended with Systems and Sub Systems.The PBS is used for the
integration with the schematics design tools as well, and is the basis for many tag naming conventions.
You can perform the following tasks for managing the engineering progress. In the Work Packages feature set:
Run the Document-Progress item Work Packages Report to view the document to progress item work package relation and relate a document
to a progress item using the relationship builder.
Select Progress Item Work Packages to search for the progress item work package and relate that work package to one or more documents
using the relationship builder.
Create a contract transfer request
 You need to have the Global Document Controller or Document Controller role to create a contract transfer request.
You can attach multiple signed-off documents to a contract. As part of the workflow, the documents are revised and the resulting working state documents are
now reassigned to a specific contract.
 The master document of the contract has information which is related to the latest revised document.
1. In the Internal Communication feature set, select Create Contract Transfer Request.
2. In the New Contract Transfer page, specify the contract that you want to associate the documents with.
3. Type or select any other required information.
4. Navigate to the next page and select one or more current documents to attach.
 The listed documents are all signed-off documents.
 When the transfer of the contract is successful, a task is created in your To Do List to review the changes. When this task is completed, the system
closes the contract transfer workflow.
Create and manage handover requests
Project handover is the process of handing over information by the contractor to the client on project phase completion. This marks the change of ownership of
the information and is usually a process in which the client checks for the completeness of the handover. Information is typically handed over in packages or
segments.
Data books support the handover of grouped information and allow for completeness checking by different groups such as Operations, Maintenance, Health,
Safety, and Environment (HSE), and Engineering.
As a contractor user or a company user with a Global Document Control role, you can create a handover request and associate dynamic data book chapters
to it. To create the request, you need to first create a handover data book with dynamic chapters or subchapters containing documents and tags. When a
handover request is created, a handover workflow is attached, and the company user receives the data. For more information on how to create and manage
data books, see Create and manage data books.
 Only the data that is accessible to you can be handed over.
What do the dynamic data book chapter icons mean?
The dynamic data book chapter state is indicated by its icon.
Icon
Data book chapter state
Notes
A dynamic data book
chapter has been created.
A static data book chapter
has been created.
 You cannot
associate a static data book
chapter to a handover
request.
In Progress
The handover process for
this data book chapter is in
progress.
Completed
The handover process for
this data book chapter is
successfully completed.
Rejected
The handover process for
this data book chapter is
rejected and is not yet
assigned to a new
handover request.
What do the handover request icons mean?
The handover request state is indicated by its icon.
Icon
Handover request state
Notes
In Progress
The handover request has
been prepared and has not
yet been approved.
Completed
The handover request has
been approved.
Rejected
The handover request has
been rejected.
Create a handover request
1. In the Data Books feature set, select the handover data book, and then click Actions 
 > Manage Handover Data Book.
2. Click 
 to create a handover request.
3. Type the required information, and then select the dynamic chapters and subchapters from the list.
4. Click FINISH to attach to the handover workflow.
Review a handover request
After reviewing the handover information, you can approve or reject the request. You can also return the request for rework if the shared handover request is
incomplete. For information on how to see the handover details, see View handover details.
 All the documents must be signed off to complete a handover. The system does not allow you to complete a handover request with working
documents, and you must return the request for rework.
1. Select the Initial Handover Review step from your To Do List and click Actions 
 > Review Handover Request.
 You must claim your workflow step when multiple users are assigned to the same user role. To claim a workflow step:
On the Actions 
 menu, select Claim Workflow step, add any comments to the Claim Workflow step dialog box, and then select OK.
2. Select:
Approve if all the data shared looks fine and the handover can proceed.
The documents and tags related to the chapters are moved to as-built.
A completion notice is sent to the submitter.
Reject if a major portion of the data shared is incorrect and not ready for handover.
A rejection notice is sent to the submitter, and the submitter must create a new handover request.
Rework if a minor portion of the data shared is incorrect and can be corrected.
A step is sent to the submitter's To Do List to rework the handover request and send it back to the receiver.
Acknowledge the completion or rejection notice
When a handover request has been approved or rejected by the receiver, the system sends a notice to the submitter.
In your To Do List, select the Completion Notice or Rejection Notice task, followed by Actions 
 > Acknowledge.
Rework the handover request
The submitter reworks the handover request and sends it back to the receiver for another review. For information on how to see the handover details, see View
handover details.
1. Select the Perform Handover Rework step from your To Do List.
2. Select Actions 
 > Rework Handover Request.
3. Click Update to cancel the chapter or subchapter selection and click UPDATE to save the changes.
4. Click COMPLETE.
View handover details
To view the data attached to a handover request, click Actions 
 > Handover Request > View Handover on the handover workflow step.
When the Query by selected configurations only option is disabled, you can view the documents and tags related to the chapters.
When the Query by selected configurations only option is enabled, you can view the documents and tags related to the chapters only if the handover
is in progress.
 The numeric value next to each chapter represents the number of chapters and subchapters related to the handover under that chapter.
 To view the data attached to a handover data book, click Actions 
 > View Handover DataBook on any handover data book.
Create and manage signature requests
SDx interacts with DocuSign, an eSignature tool, to support electronic signatures on communication documents and original documents, and store those
documents with the correct relationships.
 To use DocuSign on SDx, you need to have your SDx deployed on a secure website that can be accessed from the public internet.
What do the signature request status icons mean?
The following icons indicate the signature request state.
Icon
Signature request state
Notes
Draft
The signature request has
been prepared and has not
yet been approved.
Issued
The signature request has
been issued to the signee.
Completed
The signature request has
been completed by the
signee.
Rejected
The signature request has
been rejected by the signee.
Create a signature request
As a user with a Document Controller role, you can create a signature request.
1. In the Internal Communication feature set, select Create Signature Request.
2. Enter the information as required in the New Signature Request page.
3. Navigate to the next page, and add the documents you want to attach to the request.
4. Click FINISH, and the submitter gets a Submit signature request task in their To Do List.
The submitter gets the Update missing documents or files task in their To Do List if any of the attached documents do not have any files. To resolve,
attach one or more files to the documents and retry.
The submitter gets the Update missing/incorrect signers email address task in their To Do List if any of the signers' email address is empty or
incorrect. To resolve, update the signer's email address and retry.
The submitter gets the Resolve file size on signature request task in their To Do List if the cumulative size of the files attached to the signature
request exceeds the size limit specified in the DocuSign connection details. To resolve, replace the files with smaller resized files and retry.
The submitter gets the Signature request correct failure task in their To Do List if the system fails to generate renditions for one or more files. For
more information, see Correct PDF rendition generation failures.
Submit the signature request
1. In your To Do List, select the Submit signature request task.
2. Click Actions 
 > Request for eSignature.
Review the returned request
The submitter of the signature request gets a task in their To Do List to review the signatures.
If all the files are signed by all the signers, the system sends the Review signature request response task to the submitter's To Do List.
1. Select the Review signature request response task in your To Do List.
2. Click Actions 
 > Complete to accept the request or Actions 
 > Reject to reject the request.
If some files are not signed or one or more signers declined to sign, the system sends the Signature request validation failed task to the submitter's To Do
List.
1. Select the Signature request validation failed task in your To Do List.
 On the step details, see Message from the previous step to see the list of unsigned files by the individual signers or the declined signers with the
reason.
2. Click Actions 
 > Complete to accept the request or Actions 
 > Reject to reject the request.
If the submitter accepts the request, a Completion notice is sent to the submitter's To Do List.
If the submitter rejects the request, the documents and files are delinked from the signature request so that they can be attached to another request.
Acknowledge the completion notice
When a signature request has been approved by the submitter, the system sends a notice to the submitter.
In your To Do List, select the Completion notice task, followed by Actions 
 > Acknowledge.
 To view the signed files on a document,
1. Click Actions 
 > Show Documents on the Completion notice task.
2. Select a document, and click Actions 
 > Show Viewable Files.
3. Select a row item, and click Actions 
 > Show Signed File.
Use the Web Client
This section details common tasks available across the Web Client.
Understanding information access
What you see and can do in the Web Client depends on how your administrator configures your system and your security permissions. Administrators use the
security model to do the following:
Create users, access groups, and role assignments.
Assign users to roles and associate roles with access groups, which determine the access to features, commands, plants and projects.
Add plant or projects known as configurations as query and create scopes, which determine what objects and data you can view, create, or modify.
 Configurations are used to manage controlled change to data. The top-level configurations are usually plants with project configurations
underneath at lower levels.
Create and enforce security rules and security codes.
How do I know which roles and configurations I can use?
You can see which roles and configurations you can use two different ways:
You can see which roles and configurations are currently being used in the upper right corner of the Web Client. Select Query, Create, and Role to view
the options available to you.
Select Settings 
, and then Scope to view your configurations, or select Roles to view which roles are available to you.
I can create an object in Plant A, but not in Plant B. Why?
You can only create objects in a single configuration at a time selected as your Create Scope. For example, if you select Plant A as your create scope, you
can only create objects in Plant A. You can query for data and objects in other configurations, such as Plant B, but you cannot create objects in Plant B until
you switch your create scope to Plant B.
Which security features determine what I can see or do in the system?
What you can see or do is determined by which objects you need to see and which actions you need to perform to do your job.
Your administrator can use many different components of the security model to control what you can do or see in the system.
For more information on the security model, see Security model overview.
Security model feature
What it does
Role and role assignment
Determines your level of access to data and
functionality in a specific plant or project configuration.
You can belong to more than one role per configuration.
Roles are associated with related access groups,
domains, and owning groups.
Access group
Determines what actions you can perform in the system
and which functional components you can access in
your system. Users are related to roles, which are
related to access groups.
Security rule
Limits the types of data that you can query in the
system. Your associated access groups are used to
determine which security rules are relevant.
Security code
Sets different security levels for users and data in the
system. Security codes are used by security rules,
which determine your access to documents and data
items that can be secured by these codes. You may be
assigned different sets of security codes in different
plants or projects.
Owning group
Sets up ownership of data, typically by department or
discipline, as well as controls your access to an object
or parts of an object based on its ownership. Objects
can be owned by a user or by an owning group. Owning
groups are associated to roles. The default owning
group is engineering to which everyone has access.
Configuration
You can be assigned different roles in different plants
and projects.
You can create and manipulate data in a single plant or
project. This is also known as the create scope. Objects
created in projects are not visible from parallel projects
or its parent plant.
You can query across multiple plants and projects. This
is also known as the query scope.
Why do I get a restriction exception when I query certain data types?
What you can query for, or how you can navigate to certain data types in the database, is controlled by restrictions related to various user and role types. If you
receive a restriction exception message and believe you should have access to the data you're querying, contact your system administrator. For more
information, your system administrator should see Restriction exception error on query or navigation.
I can’t find a document that I’m supposed to work on. Where is it?
There might be several reasons why you can’t find the document in your system:
A security rule might be applied that limits access to specific organizations.
The document might be in a plant or project that you don’t have access to.
You might not have the appropriate role to view or edit that specific document.
The document might have a security code that you don’t have access to, so specific documents might not be accessible or available.
It’s important to communicate with your administrator to see if you’re allowed to access that document.
Which security codes are delivered?
There are three security codes delivered: Unclassified, Proprietary, and Classified; however, your administrator can configure any security code that meets
your business's needs.
Security codes can be associated with both objects in the system and user roles - setting different security levels and controlling access and visibility of
objects.
Understanding scopes
You access data in your system found within plants or project configurations using scopes. The scopes determine what data you can view, query, create, or
modify in the selected plants or project configurations.
What is the difference between a create scope and a query scope?
The Create Scope is the one plant or project configuration, that you select from a list of query configurations, in which you can create and modify data or
objects.
The Query Scope displays an access-defined set of plants or project configurations that you can search and query for data and objects. When you select the
plant or project configurations, this selection determines what data and objects are returned and displayed after you run a search or query.
When you select multiple plants or project configurations as your query scope, the returned data is displayed in the results grid under separate tabs that
correspond to each configuration. Each tab is shown in alphabetical order.
When you query a project configuration that exists under a plant, the query returns objects found in both the plant and the selected project configuration.
For example, if you set your query scope as PlantA and Project2, your query will return objects that exist in both PlantA and Project 2.
How many plants or projects can I select for each scope?
You can only select one plant or project as your Create Scope.
You can select multiple plants or project configurations as your Query Scope. The returned data is displayed in the results grid under separate tabs that
correspond to each configuration. Each tab is shown in alphabetical order.
When you query a project configuration that exists under a plant, the query returns objects found in both the plant and the selected project configuration. For
example, if you set your query scope as PlantA and Project2, your query will return objects that exist in both PlantA and Project 2.
 The maximum number of scopes you can select as your Query Scope is restricted to ninety nine (99). This is a system limitation. To reduce the
amount of data returned, you should refine your query scopes to a more targeted selection.
How does filtering features by design component work?
You can control the display of specific features in SDx by selecting the integrated design component you require in the Integration Data Filter section when you
configure your scopes. The Consolidated option is selected by default, so you need to select the specific design component to see any of the design
component features, such as objects and methods.
Setting the IDF option to a design component limits your scope to display only the data assigned to the corresponding design component for a specific tool,
such as 3D. This ensures that only the relevant design component features, such as methods, entry points, reports, and forms, are visible to you based on the
IDF you set in the create and query scope.
To control the visibility of each design component feature, relationship definitions have been defined between the type of object you want to control and the
design component.
SPFMethodDesignComponent - Relationship between a method and the design component.
SPFFormDesignComponent - Relationship between a form and the design component
SPFAPIEntryPointDesignComponent - Relationship between an entry point and the design component.
SPFAdhocReportDesignComponent - Relationship between a report and the design component.
Methods
When the SPFMethodDesignComponent relationship definition is configured between a method and a design component, the method is only displayed if you
select the IDF for that design component. The following example image shows that the View Item in Drawing method is associated with both the 3D and the
Consolidated design components using the SPFMethodDesignComponent relationship.
If the selected IDF is both 3D and Consolidated, the View Item in Drawing method is available in both.
If the method is not associated with any specific design component, it is only available in the Consolidated design component.
The same approach applies to actions, shortcut methods, entry points, reports, and forms. The only difference is the relationship definition used to
retrieve the design components..
Forms
When the SPFFormDesignComponent relationship definition is configured between a form and a design component, the form is only displayed if you select
the IDF for that design component.
If a Class Definition is linked to a form that has a relationship with a design component, the form is retrieved if the selected design component matches.
If a Class Definition is linked to a form that is not associated with any design component, the form is retrieved for the default Consolidated design
component.
In cases where both Class Definition and Classification have forms, the most specialized form linked to the classification is selected. The filtering for the
design component is unchanged.
If you select an IDF other than Consolidated, the To Do List icon is hidden in the sidebar.
If the selected IDF is Consolidated, the To Do List displays both Non-SIO tasks and Consolidated tasks through the relationship between the class
definition to the design component.
 For more information on how you can view and set your scopes, see Working with scopes.
Understanding roles
Your user role determines what features or commands you can view in each plant or project. Your business administrator assigns you one or more role within
the plants or projects. You can select multiple roles from the list to access different functionality in different configurations. As you select each role, the different
features or commands are made available, or are removed.
 For more information, see Change your roles.
Which roles do I have access to?
You can check the list of roles assigned to you in the selected plant or project in Settings. As you select each role, different features will become available.
Why do my roles change when I change configuration?
Your business administrator assigns you one or more roles within plants and projects. They can assign the same roles to you across multiple plants or
projects, so different roles can be assigned to you depending on your responsibilities for each plant or project. When different roles are assigned, some
configuration options might not be available to you because of your role permissions.
When the same roles are assigned to you across the multiple plants or projects, you can access the same features in each configuration.
The list of plants or projects available can also change depending on your selected role because some roles might not have access to certain plants or
projects.
The list of roles to which you have access is configured by your system administrator. For more information on your role assignments, contact your
system administrator.
Users assigned a contractor role will not have access to certain features in SDx.
What happens when I select all roles?
When Select All is applied to the Roles list, all your role permissions are applied at the same time.
If you have been assigned a contractor role and the Select All option is selected, some features are hidden and unavailable to you even if another one of your
roles has the necessary permissions to access the features because the limited access permissions for contractor role overrides the other role permissions. To
access the features you would normally have access to, clear the contractor role selection. For more information on your role assignments, contact your
system administrator.
For information about the functionality available to a role, see the HxGN SDx Roles
<https://docs.hexagonppm.com/viewer/document/KV2OpDSbTEp81JHaojREVQ> matrix.
Basic tasks in the Web Client
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Web Client Basic Tasks
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/64c6f28ac7db86033ded193d>
Learn some of the basics of the Web Client to help get you started.
Find your way around the Web Client
After logging into the Web Client, you can quickly and easily navigate to your documents and data through the feature sets and functions on the Home page
and perform your tasks from the sidebar.
Select
To see
What it does
Actions menu
Performs common operations on one or more selected objects as
indicated.
 Select Open in new tab 
 against an action to display the results
in a new browser tab. Keep in mind that logging out in one tab
automatically logs you out in all other open tabs. You will lose any unsaved
changes.
Pins
Provides quick access to items that you have saved, or pinned as
indicated.
Notifications
Alerts you when a change has been made to an object that you have
registered interest in.
Feature sets
Each contains a configured set of functions that you can use to quickly
access data or run reports and commands. You can also open a feature
set and access the functions from the sidebar.
To Do List
Lists all your tasks.
Advanced Search
Performs a complex search using specific search criteria that includes
classifications, their properties, and parameters.
 You can save a complex advanced search query, as well as rename
an existing query or save an edited query with a new name.
Search Bar
Searches for objects in the system based on object types, parameters, or
wildcards that you type in the Search Bar.
Settings
Shows the controls you can use to:
Choose a different configuration or role.
Reassign your workflow tasks to other users.
Change user preferences to customize your experience. For
example, you can change your password.
Search for data and objects in your system
The easiest ways you can search for data and objects in the Web Client are to:
Use the Search Bar for the quick retrieval of information from the system, such as tags, documents, assets, and so on, as described in Search for
objects.
Use the Multiple Item Search option in the Search Bar for the retrieval of specific information that is known by name, as described in Search for
objects.
Run a predefined search function tile found in a feature set configured by your system administrator.
Run a predefined report function tile in a feature set configured by your system administrator.
For more advanced searches, you can use Advanced Search 
 to create more complex queries that you can save for future use, as described in
Querying for data.
All search results are shown in the results grid, see View search results.
For more information on configuring search function tiles, see Relate a method to a feature set.
For more information on running a report, see Run a report.
Search for objects
You can search for specific objects in the search bar.
1. In the toolbar, select Search.
2. In the Search menu, select the data type, and select the search type.
For example, select Documents as the data type, and select All Documents as the search type.
3. In the Search Bar 
, type any defining criteria, parameters, or wildcards, and select Search 
.
 You can control the default search criteria that appears in the Search bar for each object type selected in the Search menu. For more
information, see Configure the default search bar criteria.
How can I improve my search?
You can improve your search by:
Selecting FILTER on the Search menu so you can refine a search to a targeted set of objects using a defined set of column set properties.
 You can choose up to a maximum number of five (5) column set properties from the list. This helps improve your search performance by restricting
the number of properties you can select. If you select more than five (5), a message warns you that too many filters are selected. If you still go ahead
and run the search with six or more filters selected, peek search is disabled, and the search just uses Name as the default or the search filter option
configured at the top.
Selecting a property or relationship as a filter, and entering a wildcard (*) will only return the objects which have the column set item (property or
relationship) instantiated.
Using the search operator Is equal to instead of the default search operator Contains. The Is equal to operator returns more targeted searches for data
that is an exact match and helps improve search performance each time you search.
Changing the default search filters to other items available in the objects column set, such as Description or Classification, so that they are preselected
each time you search. The maximum number you can select as default search filters is five column set items.
Selecting 
 Advanced search to create complex search queries using multiple options and parameters to return a targeted set of objects.
You can select an object category on the Search menu to run searches for specific object types. For more information, see Configure search menu
items.
For more information on Advanced search, see Use the Advanced search.
What is an advanced search?
You can create complex search queries using Advanced Search 
 to return a targeted set of objects. Each query can be saved and run with multiple
options and parameters depending on the object type. For more information, see Use the Advanced search.
The search results are displayed in a results grid. For more information, see View search results.
As you type in the search bar, a peek search returns examples and the number of objects that match the typed characters. You can configure the number
of characters that must be typed before a peek search request is invoked. For more information, see Configure peek search request value.
If your system has been configured to use Full Text Retrieval (FTR), you can select the Full Text Search option to search for objects using the managed
index. For more information, see Full text search option.
Search for multiple items
You can search for multiple items directly in the search bar.
1. In the toolbar, select Search.
2. In the Search Menu, select the data type, and select the search type.
For example, select Tags, Assets and Models as the data type and Tag Types as the search type.
3. In the Search bar 
, select Multi Object Search 
.
4. In the multiple object search dialog, type the name of each search item in a separate row, along with any wildcards. For example:
 You can also copy and paste line entries directly into the multi object search dialog from an existing list, such as from Microsoft Excel. This is
limited to one hundred (100) line entries per search.
5. Click Search 
, and the results are displayed in a Results grid.
Multi object search only supports searching the Name field using the Is equal to search operator, which only returns results that are an exact match.
Multi object search does not support using a peek search to list matching objects.
When you click Search, the contents of the multi object search dialog automatically removes any duplicate entries, empty lines, or spaces from any
search terms. There is no need to use pipe | special character to separate line entries.
If a multi object search request is too large, an error may occur. Reduce the search item size and retry.
Export object data to Excel
After you have run a search for objects, the returned objects and their property column set data is displayed in a results grid. You can then download the
named objects and their column set properties to a Microsoft Excel XLSX file using the EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL command. You can open the downloaded
spreadsheet directly from your browser or you can download it to your designated folder and open it there.
 We do not recommend you export large amounts of data using EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL, as the export may fail if you export more than 10,000
objects. We advise you to run reports that have been specifically developed to handle the export of large amounts of data. Reports can display data in a
variety of formats. For more information on running reports, see Reporting on your data.
The EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL command is also available from opening a pins list to a results grid or when using List Edit, and so on.
Configured reports return and display large amounts of data in a variety of formats. For more information on running reports, see Reporting on your data.
The property columns you see, and the order in which the columns appear in the results grid, are determined by the column set based on the object
classification.
The out put of results using EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL can be controlled using the following Server Manager site settings:
ExportToExcelMaxPageSize - Specifies the maximum number of objects that can be exported in one page.
ExportToExcelTimeout - Specifies a period of time that the server attempts to export a page of data before the server times out.
ExportToExcelExportLimit - Specifies a value to limit the number of objects that can be exported.
 If you set the export limit value higher than the 10,000 default or if the number of objects exported exceeds the default, the export may fail.
 For more information on the export to Excel settings, see Site Settings node properties.
Run a report
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Run a report (modified in an update).
You can run a report by selecting it from the relevant feature set. Configured reports return specific data and display it in various formats, based on the
configuration and selected output style. For more information, see Reporting on your data.
You can use the following options on reports:
Select this
To do this
items per page
Select the number of items to display on a page. When you run a report
using the New Window output style, the results are displayed in a grid view.
You can choose the number of items to display per page from the items per
page list. The available options are 25, 50, 100, 500, and 1000. The default
value is 25.
 The value in the items per page list is reset to the default when
the page is refreshed.
EDIT PARAMETERS
Edit any parameters used in the report.
Reset
Reset the parameters used back to the default settings.
Open in full page
Open the Edit parameters to fill the page.
 You can limit the results to a targeted set of data by selecting the standard parameters supported: =, !=, >=, >, <=, <, and ~. For more information, see
Run a report with a prompt.
Use the Actions menu to manage objects
The Actions menu displays a list of shortcuts to command functions that are specific to the selected object(s). To display the Actions menu command
shortcuts:
Select the object in a row, and select Actions 
 in the row, or select Actions 
 in the sidebar.
If you select multiple objects, the number of objects selected is displayed in the Actions sidebar. Only actions applicable to all of the selected objects are
displayed in the Actions menu.
Some actions are available in the shortcut ribbon when you hover on an object row.
You can also open the Actions menu from the central node on the Info Map and from an object's hotspot bubble in an opened 2D or 3D file.
For more information on adding Actions menu command shortcuts, see Configure Actions menu items.
How can I open action results in a new tab?
Select Open in new tab 
 to open the results in a new browser tab.
 Logging out in one tab automatically logs you out in all other open tabs. You will lose any unsaved changes.
 You can use the Suppress Open in New Tab in Web Client option in a method to hide Open in Web Client 
 icon from display in the Actions
menu. This ensures that the action does not open into a separate browser tab.
Displaying actions as shortcut icons
Some Actions menu functionality can be accessed using shortcut icons that display in a ribbon when you select an object. Only action shortcut icons in the
ribbon relevant to the selected object are displayed. The shortcut icon ribbon displays when:
You hover over an object's description in the Results grid, and the icon ribbon opens beneath the object row.
You open the Actions menu for an object, the icon ribbon displays at the top of the Actions menu.
Apple iPads and Android Tablets
You interact differently with the UI on Apple iPads or Android tablets as there is a variation in functionality for displaying the actions shortcut icons. This is due
to the different devices that can be used to view SDx:
On an Apple iPad or Android tablet, on the object row, select the ribbon option , and the action shortcut ribbon opens beneath the row.
Default ribbon icons
The following action shortcut icons are configured to display in the ribbon by default.
Icon
Action
Description
Show Info Map
Displays the selected object's relationships as clear, organized user-focused
information.
Add to Pinboard
Adds the selected object to a list in the Pinboard.
Show the detail form
Displays the Object Details form showing the current value of the object's
properties.
Share Data URL
Displays the object's URL address path, which can be sent to anyone you are
collaborating with.
Other object shortcut icons
Other action shortcut icons are displayed depending on the type of object selected in the Results grid and how your system is configured. For example, the
following action shortcut icons can be displayed dependent on the document type.
Icon
Action
Description
View and Markup
Displays the selected object in View and Markup.
View drawing
Displays the object as a drawing in a document.
View 3D Model
Displays the object as an item in a three dimensional model.
View laser scan
Displays the object in a laser scan.
Show inbox for object
Displays the To Do List task items for the selected object.
View workflow
Displays the Workflow Viewer, which shows the current status and position
of the object in the attached workflow.
Add objects to the Pinboard
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Using the Pinboard (modified in an update).
You can bookmark your favorite objects by adding them to a list in your Pinboard, where they can be easily managed and recalled for future use.
You access your pinned items by selecting Pins 
 in the sidebar to open the Pins menu, and see all your pinned items in the Pinboard. For more
information, see Using the Pinboard.
 When you create a new object, it is automatically added to New Items in the Pins menu. However, new objects must be added to a list in the
Pinboard 
 to save them, as they are only available in a single session. Save the selected pins to the Default List or a new pins list of your own making.
Review your work in the To Do List
The To Do List 
 displays your workflow tasks that have been assigned to you or your roles that need to be actioned or completed. You can see the number
of tasks available in each selected configuration, the task's details including attached files, and see a brief view of your task step in the workflow.
Select To Do List 
, and select a slice in the pie chart or a task type in the list to view all the tasks in a results table.
Use the Info Map to visually navigate information
Use the Info Map to quickly visualize all the objects related to a single object. The selected object is displayed as a central node surrounded by all the related
objects, such as file types, plants, tags, contracts, suppliers, and so on. Selecting a different node in the view displays that node as the central node with its
related objects expanded.
Select an object in the Results table row, and select Info Map 
.
How can I interact with the Info Map?
Drag and reposition individual object nodes in the window using the mouse or touch screen control.
Zoom and resize objects in the display using the mouse wheel or touch screen control.
You interact with the Info Map and change how objects are presented using the following tool-bar commands:
Name
Icon
Description
Single Mode
Displays a single node surrounded by its related objects and relationships.
Follow Mode
Displays multiple nodes each surrounded by their related objects and
relationships, which allows you to follow the expansion and grow a
relationship tree.
 The follow mode is disabled when you view the Info Map from the
object details form.
Reset
Resets the node positions back to their original position.
Clear History
Clears the Info Map expansion history and returns the display the selected
node and its related objects.
Hide Node Names
Hides the names of each node displayed in the window.
Show Node Names
Shows the names of each node displayed in the window.
Zoom to Fit
Fits all the displayed object nodes into the window.
Undo
Undoes the last selected node expansion.
Redo
Redoes the last selected node expansion.
 Info Map 
 is also displayed in the actions menu or in the object details page.
Change your user settings and personalize your experience
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Personalize your user experience
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/paths/6334884f2917848364e63206/>
You can change your configuration scopes, roles, display, and email preferences in the Settings 
 menu, as well as reassign workflow tasks to other users.
For more information, see Working with scopes and User preferences.
Working with scopes
You can to choose your create and query scopes from the list of available configurations in the Settings page. The selected configurations determine what
objects and data you can retrieve, view, create or modify after a search or query which is then displayed in a results grid.
 For more information, see Understanding information access.
Change the query scope
1. Select Query in the header bar to open the Settings page to set your query scopes.
2. Select the plants or project configurations in which you want to search for data or objects to add them to your query scope. The selected configurations
are displayed in the header bar.
 You can expand each plant or configuration node to reveal other related projects and configurations.
If you query for project configurations that exists under a plant, by default the query returns objects found in both the plant and the project configurations.
For example, if you set your query scope as Project 2 of Plant A, your query returns objects that exist in both Project 2 and Plant A.
The maximum number of configurations you can select as your Query Scope is restricted to ninety nine (99). For more information, see Understanding
scopes.
You can also access the Settings page by selecting Settings 
 in the sidebar.
You can narrow the data returned from a selected set of configurations using the Query by selected configurations only option. For more information,
see Query by selected configurations only option.
Change the create scope
1. Select Create in the header bar to open the Settings page and set your create scope.
 You can also access the Settings page by selecting Settings 
 in the sidebar.
2. In the Query Scopes list, select the Create 
toggle key for the configuration in which you want to create or modify objects.
You can only choose one configuration to use as your Create Scope from the configurations selected in your query scope.
Select the Status Filter option to view the plant and project configurations that match the selected status. For example, select Completed to only display
data from the configurations that have completed in the system.
Scope filter options
You can select 
 additional options to refine the list of displayed configurations.
Select this
To do this
Select All Top Level Configurations
Selects only the top-level configurations or plants as your query scope.
Data from projects below the top-level configurations or plants is not
returned.
Select Sub-Config for Checked Items
Selects only the sub-configurations or projects of your plants as your query
scope. Only data from the projects or sub-configurations below the plants is
returned.
Clear Create Config
Clears the selected create scope configuration.
Clear Query Scope
Clears any selected query configurations.
Query by selected configurations only
By selecting the Query by selected configurations only option, your query only returns data or objects from the selected configurations (projects) and does
not return data from the higher configuration (plant).
For example, if you set your query scope to Project1 and Project 2 of Plant A and select the Query by selected configurations only option, your query only
returns data or objects found in Project1 and Project 2 and returns no data from the higher configuration, Plant A.
Life cycle status filter
You can expand the list of displayed query configurations by selecting other configurations by their lifecycle status. Select the Status Filter list options that
matches the plant or project configurations that you want to view.
The full list of statuses are:
Active
Approved
Approved for As-Built
Cancelled
Completed
Created
Finished
Loader
Merged
Published
Submittal
Design Completed and Design Review are also available for an integrated system.
By default, Active status filter is selected so only configurations with an Active status filter are displayed in the query scope. You can select other
statuses to expand the query for data in configurations with a different life cycle status, such as Completed.
Change your roles
 Your roles are not set until you select your query scope configuration.
1. Select Role in the header bar to open the Settings page.
 You can also select Settings 
 in the sidebar to open the Settings page to select your roles.
2. Select 
 and choose the roles you want to use to access data from the Roles list.
What happens when I select all?
When Select All is applied to the Roles list, all your role permissions are applied at the same time.
If you have been assigned a contractor role and the Select All option is selected, some features are hidden and unavailable to you even if another one of your
roles has the necessary permissions to access the features because the limited access permissions for contractor role overrides the other role permissions. To
access the features you would normally have access to, clear the contractor role selection. For more information on your role assignments, contact your
system administrator.
If you select more than one configuration, the roles you select are combined in the Roles list.
Some roles may not have access to the same functionality in different configurations.
For more information on your roles and access permissions, contact your system administrator.
User preferences
Use the User Preferences section in the Settings to personalize your user settings and viewing experience.
Set the user options
Select Options 
 to customize your display and email notifications to match your workflow requirements.
Option
Details
Picklist Display
Choose how the object details in a list are displayed in the Results table by
selecting Name, Description, or Both.
Nested Reference File Level
When viewing files, this limits how many levels of reference files are displayed
by a master document, as the files may also reference other files. For
example, if a master document was uploaded with four levels of reference files
and the option is set to two, only the first two levels of reference files are
displayed.
 The option level set here is honored by the Desktop Client.
Show All Items On Update References Dialog
You can use this option to choose the display of files in the Update Reference
Relationships window based on their relationship with the master document.
By default, the toggle is turned off, to show only the files with out-of-date
reference relationships with the master document in the Update Reference
Relationships window.
You can turn on the toggle to show all files for a document with reference
relationships such as Always Update, Prompt Update and Always Update
Working Revisions. They are displayed in the Update Reference
Relationships window as active or inactive depending on their reference
relationships with the master document.
Format type for email digest
Select the format for the email digest to be HTML or Text.
 Select HTML to switch on any links in the emails in the email digest.
Email digest duration in hours
Set the number of hours you want to receive notifications in an email digest.
For example, setting 4 means an email digest message is received every 4
hours - the digest combines all notifications from the previous 4 hours.
Notification type
Choose the type of notification you want to receive, Notify, Email, or Notify
and Email.
Notify indicates messages appear as a software notification.
Email indicates messages are delivered using your email.
Group email per project
Indicates that notifications included in an email digest message should be
grouped according to the project with which they are associated.
Info Map node type display name
Sets the object type to show the relationship of an Info Map satellite node with
the main node. Choose how the node type is displayed by selecting Class
display name or Relationship display name.
Use dashboard as default home page
Uses your available home screen dashboards as your default home page
instead of using feature sets.
Open Info Map in a new browser tab
Indicates that the Info Map is displayed in a new browser tab when it is opened
from an Impact Analysis, Conflict Resolution, Data Book Management, or
Communication Management page.
When possible, open to markups view first
View and Markup preferences are designed to view first and then markup as
required. By default, the button is switched OFF allowing you to view first.
You can switch ON the button to open to the markup option first when
possible.
For more information about what file formats are supported or how to markup
your files, see Visualizing your data.
Reassign your tasks
Select Task Reassignment 
 to reassign your workflow tasks to a deputy when you are out of the office for a designated period. You can select individual
projects and choose the roles and assignees to manage the tasks.
For more information, see Reassign work to a different user.
Resize your menu panes
You can resize your menu panes by dragging or double-clicking the menu sizing handle  to alternate between the maximum and minimum pane size.
 Not all menu panes can be resized.
Change your password
You can change your password by:
Selecting Change Password , or selecting your [User Name] > Change Password . After entering your existing password, you can enter your new
password.
By default, your password is updated in both the Web Client and the Desktop Client.
If you use a Single Sign-on cloud environment, you can change your password through an external change password provider, such as Okta. For more
information, see the Change Password URL setting in Web Client Site Settings node. If no URL is set, the change password reverts to the default
behavior.
If your system enforces the password policy, you must choose a strong, secure password when it is changed. This must be a minimum of 8 characters in
length and contain at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and one special character. For more information, contact your system
administrator.
Access to use the Change Password option is managed using the ChangeUserPassword method. This option is disabled by default in a system using
IWA or when a system uses an external change password provider, such as Okta. However, if a change password URL is set in an IWA enabled system,
the Change Password URL method behavior always takes precedence. For more information, see your system administrator.
 Your account will be temporarily locked for a period of time after a number of failed log on attempts. For more information on unlocking your
account, see Unlock a temporarily locked user or contact your system administrator.
Editing object data
To edit object details in the results grid, select Actions 
 > List Editing. For more information, see Using List Edit.
Why can't I edit data in a column?
The columns you see and the order they are displayed is determined by the access to the object's column set. The object values displayed in columns in the
List Edit window can be configured to be viewable, editable, mandatory, or read-only. Some objects may be configured to be only viewable by a specific role
but may be editable by another role. You might not have access to columns due to the column set access set for your role.
 For more information on your role's access to editing values in columns, contact your system administrator.
Monitor for changes to data you are interested in
You can register interest or subscribe to an object event, so every time that event occurs in the software, you receive a notification message in Notifications
. These events can include each time an object is created, copied, updated, or terminated.
How are the different object types displayed?
Each different type of object is clearly indicated by their corresponding color pill. For example:
Icon
Description
A new object has been created
An object has been updated
An object has been copied
An object has been terminated
Other object types, such as reports, are indicated by the report file type. For example:
Icon
Description
An Excel file report
A CSV file report.
A CSV Repeated file report
These types of notifications are created and managed in the Desktop Client. For more information, see Configure notifications.
If you want to receive a notification each time the same object is updated in another configuration, you must register your interest in the object's update
event in each configuration in which the object is found.
Refine the notification list
You can refine the notification list to show only a defined set of notifications by selecting one or more of the following options:
Name
Description
Reports
Refines the notification list to show only notifications on any reports.
Subscriptions
Refines the notification list to show only notifications for object subscriptions.
Report Output
Refines the notification list to show only reports in a specific file type, such as CSV, XHTML, or
CSVREPEATED.
Created
Shows notifications on created reports or object subscriptions.
Copied
Shows notifications on copied reports or object subscriptions.
Updated
Shows notifications on updated reports or object subscriptions.
Terminated
Shows notifications on terminated reports or object subscriptions.
 You can filter the displayed notification list further by typing parameters in the Filter box.
Using an InsightView dashboard
An InsightView dashboard displays real-time data from various sources (including third-party data sources) in a single page within the Web Client. The data
can be related to a selected object, allowing you to easily visualize plant information relevant to that object. There are three types of InsightView dashboard:
Dashboards
These display data related to a selected object, allowing you to easily visualize plant information relevant to that object. For example, a dashboard can
be opened from a tag's Actions 
 menu and can display information related to that tag.
Home Screens
These can be used as a user's default home page instead of the feature sets, providing users with easy access to their tasks and a role-based view of
their assets and critical information. They can also be displayed by selecting 
.
Context Panels
These display data related to a selected object in a split screen view. For example, when you select an object in a Results page, a context panel can
open alongside the results and display its properties and other widget data.
An InsightView dashboard contains several smaller windows (known as widgets), each of which can display data from a different data source. For example, a
dashboard could be configured to show tag, asset, and model information, and recent change requests (from SDx), and active work permits (from an external
permit to work system such as j5) each in a separate widget.
 Your administrator will create various dashboards as required by your organization. The Context Panel dashboards are automatically enabled for all
users. If you require a specific dashboard, please contact your system administrator.
Learn more about an InsightView dashboard
The following is an example of an InsightView dashboard where the dashboard is shown in a pop-up window. It contains three types of widget. All available
widget types are explained below.
 A dashboard can also be configured to open as a page rather than as a pop-up window.
What types of widget are available?
Widget type
Description
IFrame (1)    
An iframe widget displays another web page.
Form (2)    
A form widget displays returned data in a form. In the example above, the
form widget shows details of the selected tag.
Grid (3)    
A grid widget displays returned data in columns similar to the Results page.
In the above example, the grid widget shows work orders related to the tag.
Chart
A chart widget displays returned data in a chart. A chart can be configured so
that clicking on a section of the chart displays the related data in a grid in a
second page.
Count
A count widget displays a count of the number of results returned, with the
details in a grid in a second page.
Spotlight
A spotlight widget displays one item of returned data scaled to fit in the
widget.
To Do List
A To Do List widget displays a summary of your To Do List. You can switch
between displaying tasks by configuration or object type, at the bottom of the
widget. For more information, see Using a To Do List widget.
3D Quick Access
A 3D quick access widget displays a list of the filters that you can access.
Click > View and Markup next to a filter to open the related 3D model.
 This widget is available only for 3D Project As-Built databases.
In a grid widget, you can browse through the pages of results in the same way as in a results page. In a small widget, click on 
, to access the paging
controls.
In grid and count widgets, the Actions 
 menu is available for returned objects if the widget is displayed in a full page dashboard (rather than a pop-up
window), and if configured by your administrator.
In a form widget, you can change the display to show the data in a list or in a tile. You can also sort and filter the data displayed.
Set a home screen dashboard as your home page
Home screen dashboards can be displayed as your default home page instead of the feature sets. This can provide you with a role-based view of your assets
and critical information, together with easy access to your day-to-day tasks.
To use home screen dashboards as your default home page, select the Use dashboard as default home page option in your user options. For more
information, see Set the user options.
When enabled, click on Home 
 to display the home screen dashboards that you can access. You can scroll through your available home screens, or search
for a specific home screen, using the controls at the bottom of the page.
 On a tablet, you can also scroll through your available home screens by swiping left or right.
Can I access the feature sets if a dashboard is my default home page?
Yes. Click on 
 to display the feature set home page when your default home page is set to use home screen dashboards. This does not affect your default
home page, which can be accessed by clicking on 
.
Can I access home screen dashboards without setting them as my home page?
Yes. Click on 
 to display your available home screen dashboards when they aren't set as your default home page. This does not affect your default home
page, which can be accessed by clicking on 
.
Working with widgets
When a widget is displayed in a home screen, its default scope will be your create scope. If it is displayed in a dashboard, its default scope will be the selected
object's scope. The default scope may be overridden by the administrator.
Some widgets are configured to show data for a specified scope which you can then change. To change this scope, click 
 at the top of the widget, and
select the scope required.
 If the Query by selected configurations only setting is set to False, when you select a project or sub-configuration that exists under a plant, the
query returns objects found in both the selected item and the scope above. For example, if you select Project 2 of PlantA, then the widget displays data for
both Project 2 and PlantA. If the Query by selected configurations only setting is set to True, when you select a project or sub-configuration that exists
under a plant, the query returns only objects found in the selected item.
Some widgets can be related to another widget on the dashboard and may not display data until a selection is made in the other widget. These are indicated
by 
 in the bottom right of the widget. Select an object in the linked widget to display related information. When you select a different object in the linked
widget, the related information is updated.
Data displayed in a grid widget can be filtered in the same way as a results grid. For more information, see Filter or sort the results. If the widget is connecting
to a remote system, the remote system may require the filter to be case-sensitive.
You cannot use Is equal to or Is not equal to to filter a column containing both date and time information.
The number of objects displayed in each page of a widget grid may differ from the number of objects displayed in each page of the results grid
depending on how it has been configured by your administrator.
Hover over the widget to display a popup menu:
Select 
 to expand the widget. Select 
 to return it to the original size.
You can also double-click the widget title to expand and collapse it. On an Apple iPad, you can double-tap to expand or collapse the widget and to zoom
in.
Select 
 to reload the data in the widget.
Click 
 to open the iframe widget in a new window.
 On a tablet, the above items are only available when you click 
.
How do I view the status of a widget?
When viewing the dashboard, either from the home screen dashboard or from the Actions 
 menu of the related object, select 
 to see the status of a
widget.
If any of the widgets do not display correctly, possible solutions can be found in InsightView dashboard issues.
You can also preview a widget when creating or updating a dashboard. To do this, hover over the widget and select 
.
The status details returned depend on the type of widget, and can include the entries shown in the following table.
Name
Displays
API
The Web API used to access the data.
Timer
The time taken to return the results.
Records
The number of items returned for the widget.
Status
Whether or not the widget successfully returned data.
Query
The selected scope for the widget.
Query by selected configurations only
Whether this option is set to restrict the scope to just that selected. For more information,
see Query by selected configurations only.
Error
An explanation for the error, if one occurred.
Information on why the widget may not display its contents correctly can be found in the
troubleshooting topic Widget content is not displayed.
 indicates that an error has occurred.
A user name and password might be required to log on to an external web page in an iframe widget. If the widget contents don't display, and you aren't
prompted for these details, select 
 to log on to the external web page in a new window.
Select 
 to expand the widget. Select 
 to return it to the original size.
You can also double-click the widget title to expand and collapse it. On an Apple iPad, you can double-tap to expand or collapse the widget and to zoom
in.
Select 
 to reload the data in the widget.
Why has my widget been disabled?
A widget is disabled if, the last time the dashboard was displayed, the widget request either took too long to return the results or returned too many results. The
amount of time, and number of results allowed, are configured by your administrator.
You can manually enable the widget by clicking 
. However, if the widget continues to run slowly, it will be disabled again, and you should ask your
administrator to look into the issue.
Using a To Do List widget
The To Do List widget displays a summary of your To Do List which contains all of the workflow objects assigned to you or your role which have tasks that you
need to action or complete. For more information on the To Do List, see Using the To Do List.
In the To Do List widget, your tasks are displayed by either configuration or object type. You can switch between these views at the bottom of the widget.
 The To Do List widget ignores the Query by selected configurations only setting. However, it does respect your selected life cycle Status Filters.
For more information, see Working with scopes.
Click on an area in the To Do List chart to open the selected items in the To Do List.
 Only tasks that can be carried out by your currently selected roles are displayed in the To Do List. Therefore, if your To Do List appears empty, or has
fewer tasks than shown in your To Do List widget, change your selected roles as described in Change your roles.
To filter the tasks included in the widget, use the following buttons:
Select this
To include
Tasks that are available to you or your role
Tasks previously claimed by you or explicitly assigned to you for action
Tasks that are overdue and require your immediate action or completion
Tasks that you are eligible to action, but which you must explicitly claim first.
These are tasks assigned to a role or group for which only a subset of people
need to take action
Using a Context Panel dashboard
A Context Panel dashboard displays the properties and widgets when a specific object is selected in a Results grid, the Info Map, or the Details page. To
view an object Context Panel:
Select an object, and click anywhere on the selected objects row or on an Info Map node to expand the related Context Panel.
Context Panels are configured for specific object types and are automatically enabled for all users. For more information, contact your system
administrator.
The Context Panel always appears collapsed by default and displays when an object is selected.
Multiple dashboards with different items and formats can be supported on the Context Panel carousel.
If you select another object in a row, the Context Panel remains open and the panel details are updated with the new object information.
Using the Pinboard
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Using the Pinboard (modified in an update).
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Using the Pinboard
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/6617ed65cd6838249b9283f1>
You can bookmark your favorite items by adding them as pins to your Pinboard, where they can be managed and recalled in pins lists for future use.
You access your pinned items by clicking Pins 
 in the sidebar to open the Pins menu, where all your saved pins are displayed under two columns:
New Items - where any newly created items are displayed. These must be added to a pins list in your Pinboard to be saved for future use, as new items
are only available for a single session.
Pinboard - where any pinned items are saved to a pins list. The pinned items can be easily managed in pins lists and are saved for future use in the
specified configuration.
 If a pinned item has properties that contain a URL, selecting the property opens the URL in a new browser window.
Can I save my pins and manage them in lists?
Yes you can save your items by saving them to a pins list so they can be used anytime in the future. You can also modify the lists you create and copy or move
saved items between different lists. For more information, see Modify the pins lists and Move or copy pins.
What happens if you create new items?
Items newly created in SDx are automatically added to the New Items column in the Pins menu. These can then be added to any list in the Pinboard so they
are saved for future use, as new items are only available for a single session.
Can I create objects directly from a saved pin?
Yes you can create new objects directly related to your selected pinned item using the appropriate create form for the object type. For more information, see
Create an object related to a pin.
Adding items to your Pinboard
You can add items to a pins list in your Pinboard in the following ways:
Hover over an item in a results grid, select Pin 
 from the shortcut menu ribbon. The item is added to the pins list selected.
Select items in the results grid, and click the Pin to 
 command to add the pins to a list in the 
 drop-down menu.
Select the items, click Actions 
 > Pin 
 to add the pins to a list in the 
 drop-down menu.
Can you add a pin from an image or drawing?
You can add a pin to the Pinboard directly from an object's open image or drawing file:
Select Pin 
 on the item's hotspot bubble to add the item to a list in the 
 drop-down menu.
 You can only unpin item from a list using the Pins menu unpin action. See Pins menu shortcut icons.
Group your pins in lists
All your pinned items are saved in to a pins list so they can be managed in groups and saved for future use. Each pins list is displayed in the list 
 drop-down
menu. You can add, modify, copy , and move your pins in each list. For more information, see Pins menu shortcut icons.
 You can create a new pins list by selecting NEW LIST in the list 
 drop-down menu.
Every pins list is displayed in alphabetical order and under tabs for each configuration to which you have access.
If a pins list does not exist in your system, your pins are saved to the Default List. When you first use the Pinboard, any pinned items are added to the
default list until you create and select a new pins list.
The Default List contains all of your existing pins created in a previous version of Web Client before upgrade. This way you do not lose any saved pins
when upgrading from the previous version, and can group them in a new pins list.
Pins menu shortcut icons
The Pins menu displays the following action shortcut icons in a ribbon when you select pins.
Icon
Action
Description
View In Grid
Displays the pinned items in a new results grid.
Unpin
Unpins the items from the selected pins list.
Move or copy selected pins
Moves or copies the selected pinned items to another target list.
Create an object from the selected pins
Creates a new object related to the pinned items type, such as a change
request.
 Unpinned items are only removed from the selected pins list, and remain in other lists if present.
Pinned item actions
Additional action shortcut icons display when you select a single pinned item in a list, and click More Actions .
Icon
Action
Description
UNPIN
Unpins the item from the selected pins list.
ACTIONS
Opens the Actions menu for the pinned item.
SHOW INFO MAP
Displays the pinned item's relationships as clear, organized user-focused
information.
SHOW THE DETAIL FORM
Displays the Object Details form for the pinned item showing the current
value of its properties.
MOVE OR COPY PIN
Moves or copies the selected pinned item to another target list.
Modify the pins lists
You can modify a pins list in the Pinboard:
Select the pins list from the 
 list drop-down menu in the Pinboard, and click MODIFY LISTS to add, rename, or delete a pins list.
 The Default List cannot be modified.
Move or copy pins
You can easily move or copy selected pins to another pins list.
1. Select the Move or copy selected pins 
 command.
2. Select the pins list from the 
 drop-down menu to which you want to add the items.
 You can also create a new pins list in the current configuration to which you can move or copy pins by selecting Enter a name for the new list.
You can use the following options:
Retain pins in current list - the selected pins are left in the current pins list and copied to the target pins list.
Open target list after move - the target pins list opens so you can verify that the pins have been moved or copied.
