1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Select the Open panel arrow to open the Visual Intelligence panel.
3. Expand the Search collapsible menu.
4. Add any defining parameters or wildcards in the Search Model or Drawing box, and select Search.
 You must enter at least three characters to get search suggestions.
If more than 300 results are returned, you are asked if you want to continue. Select Ok to continue or Cancel to refine your search.
How can I improve my search for tags?
You can improve your search by:
Selecting Use 'Is equal to' Operator below the search bar to refine your search to a targeted set of parameters.
Using the pie chart to navigate your search results.
Toggling search results on or off.
Organizing search results by color.
Hiding non-matching results from your view.
How can I share my search results?
To share your search results:
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Run a visual intelligence search.
3. Select  and Generate Share URL.
4. Select Close on the Share URL dialog box.
The URL to launch a view of your search results is automatically copied to your clipboard. You can send this URL to another user.
Run a visual intelligence report
Visual intelligence reports allow you to group and assign colors to graphical objects based on certain property values stored on the object itself or on related
objects. This is a very flexible and visual way to analyze large amounts of data within a single view.
Some reports are delivered with the software. To create or extend reports, you must configure them in the Desktop Client. For more information, see
Configure visual intelligence reports.
The visual intelligence report functionality is available only for data loaded using Data Capture with HxGN SDx and requires both tags and representation
object relationships for the model or drawing.
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Select the Open panel arrow to open the Visual Intelligence panel.
3. Select a report name under the Reports collapsible menu to run that report.
4. Select a group name to view tags in that group.
5. Select one or more tag numbers to access Fit selected items 
.
6. To close all reports and reset the display, expand the Search collapsible menu, and Select Reset to default on the  menu.
How can I share my report?
To share your search results:
1. Open a supported file type using View and Markup.
2. Run a visual intelligence report.
3. Select  > Generate Share URL.
4. Select Close on the Share URL dialog box.
The URL to launch a view of your report is automatically copied to your clipboard. You can send this URL to another user.
What visual intelligence reports are delivered with the software?
The following reports are available for both 2D drawings and 3D models:
Change Request Name
Change Request Priority
Change Request Status
Work Package Name
Work Package Status
The following report is only available for 2D Project As-Built databases:
Tags by Class
 This report is only available when viewing Smart Interop Objects (SIOs) in 2D drawings.
The following reports are only available for 3D Project As-Built databases:
Items by Design Status in Project
 This report is available when the create configuration scope is set to a project.
SIO Pipelines by Fluid Code
SIO Pipelines by Fluid Requirement
Items Under Change
Items Under Change by Status
Tags by Change Request Name
Tags by Change Request Priority
Tags by Change Request Status
Tags by Configs Claimed to
Tags by Work Package Name
Tags by Work Package Status
Visual intelligence options
These visual intelligence options help you navigate data in a Hexagon model opened in View and Markup.
Click this
To do this
Search
Searches for tags in a model or a hotspotted drawing and groups the search
results by color.
Toggle display of listed results
Turns the color of selected items on or off.
Change color
Changes the color of the item in the displayed file.
 If more than 30 groups are displayed, the same color is used for group
30 and all subsequent groups.
Show chart
Shows a pie chart divided into sectors representing selected items.
Sort
Sorts groups by frequency or by name in ascending or descending order.
By default, groups sort by descending frequency.
Fit selected items
Fits the display to the selected items.
Show repeated results
Shows groups of tags belonging to more than one change request or work
package. This option is only available when repeated results exist.
 This is only supported for published Hexagon models that are
transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Validator.
 and ...
Hide/Show non-matching items
Hides non-selected items from the view or shows non-selected items with low
opaqueness in a model.
Reset to default
Resets the displayed file, refreshes the list of reports, and deletes search
results.
Generate Share URL
Copies the shareable URL to your clipboard. If the user has the necessary
permissions, the URL navigates straight to the visual intelligence report or
search results.
Printing your files
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx®, HxGN SDx2 | Visualization: Printing your Files
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/666b24603ec4ed0d925e5406>
 Print options depend on the viewer and browser you are using. For more information about these options, please see the viewer's or browser's Help.
You can print a file you are currently viewing. How you print depends on the file type:
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Print files using the Brava! HTML Viewer (retired)..
To print a non-Hexagon file, see Print a non-Hexagon file format.
To print Hexagon files, use your browser.
You can print a file without viewing it. There are four ways to do this:
Navigate to the file, and click Actions 
 > Print File 
.
Hover the cursor over the file, and choose Print File 
.
Navigate to the document, and click Actions 
 > Print File 
. Select the file from the list.
Navigate to the document, hover the cursor over the document, and choose Print File 
. Select the file from the list.
 When printing files through documents, the list does not appear if there is one file in the document.
You can combine two or more files into a single PDF for printing. There are three ways to do this:
Select a document with two or more files attached in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Files > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
Select two or more documents with files attached in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Files > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
Select two or more files in the results grid and select Actions 
 > Generate Merged Rendition For Files.
When done, select the notification for PDF generation. You can click DOWNLOAD PDF 
 and print it locally, or click PRINT PDF 
.
Why is printing not working like I expect it to?
Here are some reasons that printing might not work like you expect it to:
You cannot change the color settings if printing to PDF.
All displayed markups are automatically printed.
Watermarks and labels (banners) configured in the Desktop Client are not printed.
If you print a master file, all of its reference files print too.
When using a tablet or other mobile device, printing might not work.
If you are printing without viewing the file and an alternate rendition exists, the alternate rendition is printed. You might need to regenerate the alternate
rendition to get the latest markups included.
Quick View
PDF renditions of files supported by the view and markup control can be viewed directly in a new browser tab through the following steps:
1. In the Documents feature set, select All Documents.
2. Select the required documents from the All Documents list and select Actions 
 > Quick View 
.
3. In the Select Files to Quick View window, select the required files.
 If only one file is linked to the document, that file will be selected by default.
4. Select OK.
The file will be opened in the browser's default PDF viewer.
Ensure that your browser's pop-up blocker has been disabled to allow files to be opened in new tabs.
Visualization troubleshooting
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with viewing, printing, marking up, or navigating (VPMN) files.
 Visualization is controlled by two view controls, the Graphic View Control (GVC) and the view and markup control. The following topics are divided by
view control.
Troubleshooting the Graphic View Control (GVC)
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with the Graphic View Control (GVC).
An error occurred during display
Problem
The viewer displays the following message and navigates to the previous page.
An error occurred during display. See console for additional information.
Causes
This might occur if:
The Visualization Data Service (VDS) is unavailable.
An authentication issue exists.
Solution
Contact your system administrator.
Application has run out of memory
Problem
You get a browser error message saying the application has run out of memory.
Solution
Refresh your browser or reload the web page.
Cannot load Hexagon 3D models
Problem
You cannot view a Hexagon model in the viewer.
You might see one of the following errors:
Popups must be enabled. Please see Graphic Data Service troubleshooting guide for help.
Ensure GDS is configured and running. Please see Graphic Data Service troubleshooting for help.
Causes
Accessibility settings must be enabled.
Pop-up windows are blocked.
The Graphic Data Service is not running or it is not configured properly.
Solutions
To enable accessibility settings:
1. Turn on the Force GPU rendering option, typically available under Developer options.
 The location and instructions for turning the Force GPU rendering option on and off can vary depending on your device. Refer to the Help
provided with your device for specific information.
2. If the browser is Google Chrome, make sure that the Use hardware acceleration when available option in Settings is enabled:
3. Reload the 3D model.
Enable pop-up windows in your browser settings and reload the application.
To check your Graphic Data Service configuration, contact your system administrator.
Cannot start the viewer
Problem
You cannot start the viewer using Google Chrome.
Solution
You must disable the Override software rendering list:
1. Open the Chrome browser, and then type Chrome://flags in the URL text box.
2. When the Chrome flags section opens, select Disable for Override software rendering list.
3. Restart the Chrome browser.
Viewer is unresponsive to touch gestures
Problem
The viewer does not respond to touch gestures.
Cause
Touch events are disabled on your device.
Solution
If you are using Google Chrome on a Windows 10 device, you must enable touch events.
1. Open Chrome browser and type in Chrome://flags in the URL text box.
2. When the Chrome flags section opens, set Enable touch events to Enabled.
 On the latest versions of Chrome versions (70 .0.3538.77 and later), the event name has been changed to Touch Events API.
Slow performance when loading Hexagon 3D models
Problem
You experience slow performance when loading Hexagon 3D models.
Causes
Here are some common causes for slower performance:
A cached file does not exist.
Browser settings are limiting performance.
Slow network performance and latency.
Not enough memory is available on the host device.
The Windows 10 operating system defaults to a lower power GPU card if the system power profile is set to conserve battery life (Battery Saver mode).
Viewing models with a large number of graphics.
Solutions
For cached file:
You can expect slower performance if this is the first time the model has been opened for viewing or if you are refreshing the model. This is due to required
processes on the server. A cached copy of the file will be stored for faster subsequent views.
To troubleshoot browser settings:
Browser settings can impact viewing performance. Check for these settings:
The cache size is set to the maximum allowable size.
HTTP compression is disabled.
Optimal browser caching policies in both client requests and server responses.
To troubleshoot memory, network performance, and latency issues:
Monitor the CPU usage of the host device. Various platforms have their own proprietary tools for monitoring systems. Consult your infrastructure
documentation for detailed information on how to monitor CPU, memory, storage utilization, and network traffic.
To troubleshoot the GPU card:
To determine the GPU card is using, select Settings > Information > GL Renderer, and then check GL Settings.
If your computer is defaulting to a lower power card, you can set to always run on the higher performance card using that card’s control panel. See your
graphics card manufacturer documentation on how to assign an application to a GPU.
When running in a web browser, the browser application you are using must be set to run on the highest performing card. See your graphics card
manufacturer documentation on how to assign an application to a GPU card.
See HxGN SDx Web Client platform recommendations for specific information and instructions for your GPU card.
To optimize Rendering Settings:
Rendering Settings are set of variables that you can use to refine how the Graphic View Control (GVC) loads models. To create Rendering Settings, have
your system administrator contact support for assistance.
Once the Rendering Settings have been created, the system administrator can then apply Rendering Settings to specific documents containing 3D models
or to user accounts to maximize a models viewing performance.
How do I apply Rendering Settings to a 3D model?
1. Depending on the dataset your environment uses, log on to HxGN SDx as one of the following:
Data Capture Administrator
SDx Administrator
System Administrator belonging to the PABSystemAdmin or PABPlantAdmin access groups
2. Navigate to the System Administration feature set, and select Rendering Settings.
3. Select a Rendering Setting in the results grid, and click Actions 
 > Relationship Builder.
4. In the Relationship Items section, add one or more specific documents containing 3D models to relate to the selected Rendering Settings from the
following tabs:
Pins - shows documents previously saved as pins in your Pinboard or newly created documents in New Items.
Existing - shows documents that are already related to the selected Rendering Settings.
Search - search for documents in your system using specific search criteria and Filter options.
5. Select RELATE.
 For more information about the Relationship Builder, see Create and edit a relationship
How do I apply Rendering Settings to a user?
 When Rendering Settings are applied to a user account, it will supersede any other Rendering Settings applied to individual models.
1. Depending on the dataset your environment uses, log on to HxGN SDx as one of the following:
Data Capture Administrator
SDx Administrator
System Administrator belonging to the PABSystemAdmin or PABPlantAdmin access groups
2. In the Business Administration feature set, select User Management.
3. In the User tab, find the required user, and select 
 > UPDATE.
4. In the MAIN DETAILS tab, choose one of the Rendering settings.
5. Select UPDATE.
PDF files not supported for split-screen mode
Problem
The following message displays after selecting to view a PDF file format using split-screen mode.
PDF files not supported for split-screen mode.
Cause
Only file formats supported in the Graphic View Control (GVC) can be viewed during split-screen mode. PDF file formats are not supported in the GVC.
Solution
1. In the Search Bar, type any defining criteria for the PDF file, and click Search 
.
2. Open the PDF file using View and Markup.
For more information, see Visualizing your data.
Unable to interact with the GVC on an iPad
Problem
When using Safari to access HxGN SDx on an iPad, attempts to interact with a model or drawing in the GVC by zooming or selecting objects have unexpected
results such as:
The entire browser window zooming.
The object cannot be selected.
Cause
Safari sometimes displays the full desktop version of a website in a mobile environment, thereby simplifying the user interaction. The simplified user interaction
limits certain GVC functionalities. The full desktop version of HxGN SDx offers full GVC functionalities.
Solution
In the Safari settings, select Request Desktop Website in the Settings for Websites section, and enable the All Websites toggle.
Troubleshooting the view and markup control
The following topics will help you troubleshoot issues with the view and markup control.
Unable to download a file
Error
The following message displays when trying to download a file:
This file is over the maximum size for downloading. Please consult our documentation for workarounds.
Cause
The file is too large for your browser to download.
Solutions
To download the file without markups:
1. Navigate back to the file or document in the results list.
2. Select Actions 
 > Save Target As.
3. In the Select files to download page, select the files you want to download. The files are displayed in pages based on the total number of files returned.
 Non-viewable files are also displayed, which you can also download as required.
To download the file with markups:
1. Navigate back to the file or document in the results list.
2. Select Actions 
 > Save Alternate Rendition.
An alternate rendition must exist for the file.
Your administrator must configure the method on the GenerateAlternateRendition client API to include markups.
Unable to retrieve the markup rendition
Errors
The following messages display when trying to view a file or compare files:
Unable to retrieve the markup rendition
Created scheduled task to create markup rendition
Cause
You might encounter this problem if a markup rendition does not exist.
Solution
Users can view a file or compare files after the scheduled task completes and a markup rendition is created. When you first try to view a file without a markup
rendition you get:
"Unable to retrieve the markup rendition".
When the call to generate the markup rendition completes you either get:
"Generated markup rendition" if successful, or:
"Could not generate markup rendition" if the call fails.
The main scheduler does not need to be enabled to create the markup rendition. If a markup rendition is not found, it will be created asynchronously, without
the use of the scheduled task, when the user attempts to view the file.
If the main scheduler is running, a scheduled task is created when the file is attached to the document, and users can check its status. But when users try to
view and markup the file, no scheduled task is created, and users will need to check the server log to view the status.
Annotations change to a draft image when rotated in Adobe Acrobat
Problem
Annotations change to a draft image if you download a file containing stamp, symbol, or image annotations into Adobe Acrobat with Markups as comments
selected and you rotate the annotations.
Cause
This is an Adobe Acrobat limitation.
Solution
Rotate the annotations using the viewer before you download the file with markups into Adobe Acrobat.
Rendering limitations
Problem
Content flashes when zooming in and out of a PDF file in the viewer. Data in the file might also change. Output might be incorrect when the file is printed or
downloaded.
Cause
If a customer covers old data in a file using a dynamic input box or text box (such as the Adobe Text Box tool), the element is rendered as an HTML element
and is not part of the file. As the file is rendered in the viewer, the HTML element might disappear and reappear. This causes flashing and the appearance of
old data under the element.
Solution
Remove any old data in the file without the use of a dynamic input box or text box.
Conversion fails for markup layers
Problem
Markup layers do not convert to the supported file format when viewing a file or when generating an alternate rendition of the file. The following error message
appears in the console:
Failed to convert deprecated markups - Failed to generate commented pdf using Queue server.
Cause
You might encounter this problem if the queue server is not running.
Solution
Ensure that the queue server is running on the application server.
1. Log on to the server as the administrator running Blazon services.
2. Click Start > Services.
3. Verify that the Blazon Queue Server is running and set to Automatic.
Viewer does not load correctly
Error
When trying to view a file, the viewer is grayed out and the following error appears:
Failed to find PDF worker files. This project is not set up to work with PDF files.
The following error messages appear in the console:
Uncaught DOMException: Failed to execute 'importScripts' on 'WorkerGlobalScope': The script at
'blob:https://[webclient]/[objectObid]' failed to load.
Refused to load the script 'blob:https://[webclient]/[objectObid]' because it violates the following Content Security
Policy directive: "default-src https: data: 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval'". Note that 'script-src-elem' was not
explicitly set, so 'default-src' is used as a fallback.
Failed to find PDF worker files. This project is not set up to work with PDF files.
Cause
You might encounter this problem if additional HTTP headers are present, specifically the Content Security Policy header.
Solution
1. Log on to the application server.
2. Start the Internet Information Services (IIS) Manager.
3. Click Default Web Site > HTTP Response Headers.
4. Select Content Security Policy and click Edit.
5. Enter default-src https: data: 'unsafe-inline' 'unsafe-eval' script-src: 'self' blob:; for the Value.
6. Any end-users using the client when these changes are made should refresh their browser.
Managing CAD custom fonts
Problem
While converting DWG and DGN files to PDF, fonts are not generated properly in the PDF rendition.
Solution
You can use the viewer's font support.
1. Place the fonts (for example: SHX) in a specific folder.
2. In the Server Manager, go to the File Service settings for the SDx site and type the path to the custom fonts folder for the DWG and DGN files in the
CustomFontsPath property.
3. Perform an IIS reset.
Existing markup annotations shift when opening a markup snapshot file for viewing
Problem
Existing annotations on markup snapshot files shift when the markup snapshot file is opened again for View and Markup.
Solutions
Starting with Update 50, the software creates a markup rendition (.pdf) of the markup snapshot file. This prevents the shifting problem.
If you are using a version prior to this update, you will need to readjust the annotations after viewing a second time.
Troubleshooting display items values in view and markup
Display item attribute values do not display in view and markup
A display item for an object method create form does not display the attribute values in view and markup, such as read only. In addition, the parent or
dependent display items also do not display the attribute values. For example, this can happen when you create a change request in the Actions menu for a
document in view and markup.
Cause
Your users might encounter this problem in view and markup if the CreateObjectFromSelectedObjects client API is used to make the method for the object
create form. The display items in the form do not display the attribute values.
Solution
You must create the method for the object create form for view and markup using the SDACreateClassObjAndRelFromMarkup client API. This client API
allows the display item attribute values to be displayed in the object's create form. For example, read only display item values are displayed in view and
markup for a change request form.
Querying for data and reporting on your data
With HxGN SDx, you can:
Search for tags and navigate the 3D model using those tags.
Use advanced search capabilities to find the data you need
Create reports, including summary, or business intelligence, reports
Querying for data
In addition to using the search bar, you can create more complex search queries using 
 Advanced Search, which uses object criteria combined with
multiple search operators to locate objects in a single query.
The benefit that an Advanced Search provides over the search bar is that you can create specific search queries that you can save and run again and again.
In this way, you can easily find important objects that are used multiple times throughout the life of the plant or a project.
For more information, see Use the Advanced search.
 You can also view information across a structure of data by running predefined reports, see Reporting on your data.
Use the Advanced search
You can create complex search queries using 
 Advanced Search to search for objects using selected criteria. Each query can then be saved so that the it
can run again and the objects quickly found.
Each search query can be built with specific object types, classifications, and criteria that you combine with Boolean search operators to return a targeted set
of objects. After you run the query, the items are returned and displayed in the Results grid.
 You can easily add the returned objects to a Pins list. They are then available for other functionality, such as building relationships to other objects. For
more information on Pins, see Using the Pinboard.
Create a query
1. Go to 
 Advanced Search in the side toolbar, and select + New Query.
2. Select the TYPES, CLASSIFICATION, and add search CRITERIA to build the query.
 You can add multiple criteria selections using the AND and OR statements to build the query using Boolean operators, parameters, and terms.
For example, the following query is to locate specific process engineering flow scheme documents that have names that start with the prefix, 1011-ENG-
PID:
3. Select SEARCH to run the query.
 You can save the query for future use or once the query has returned the correct data. This is especially useful if the query is complex and you need to
refine the query to return specific objects. For more information, see Save a query.
Save a query
1. Create a query, and select SAVE in the main toolbar.
2. In the Save dialog box, add the name and description for the query, and select SAVE to save the query in the Saved Queries menu.
If you run a query without saving it, select ß Back in the side toolbar to reopen the query details, and then select SAVE.
You can select SAVE AS in the toolbar to save the query as a new query that you can refine.
You can select RENAME to rename an existing query.
Filter the saved queries
1. You can filter the saved queries in the list by selecting 
 Filter in the Saved Queries menu.
2. In the filter box, enter the name or part of the name of the query. For example, Vessel. Only queries containing the name Vessel are displayed in the list.
The Filter icon changes to 
 to indicate that the list has been filtered.
The filtering process only uses the Contains search operator for searching the query list.
Sort the saved queries
You can sort the list of queries in the Saved Queries menu by selecting the 
 Sort by option, and selecting one of the following:
Select this
To do this
 A TO Z
Sort the queries in ascending A to Z order.
 Z TO A
Sort the queries in descending Z to A order.
 OLD TO NEW
Sort the queries by the latest updated date with oldest queries first.
 NEW TO OLD
Sort the queries by the latest updated date with newest queries first.
Edit a query
1. In the Saved Queries menu, select a query to edit, and select 
 More actions.
2. Select 
 EDIT QUERY, and edit the query parameters as required.
 You can select SAVE AS to save the edited query as a new query or select RENAME to rename the existing query with a new name and description.
Delete a query
You can delete a single query or multiple queries from the Saved Queries menu.
1. In the Saved Queries menu, select a single query or select ALL to select multiple queries.
2. Select 
 Delete Queries.
 You can also go to 
 More actions on a single query, and select 
 DELETE QUERY.
View search results
All search and query results are displayed in rows in the Results grid. When you select a row, it is highlighted to keep it in focus. Select the object name to
open the Object Details page.
The search results do not contain what I'm looking for. Why?
If the search results do not contain what you are searching for, it might be because of one of the following reasons:
You do not have permission to view the items. Change your user role to view the items.
The items do not exist in the current scope. Change your query scope to match the scope that contains the objects.
The wildcards used are not supported. Contact your administrator for further information.
 If a pinned item has properties that contain a URL, selecting the property opens the URL in a new browser window.
If the query scope includes more than one configuration, the results are shown on separate configuration tabs arranged in alphabetical order. The create
scope configuration tab results always appear on the first tab.
Select an object row Select this item box to open the Actions menu. If you select multiple object rows and open the Actions menu, only actions
applicable to all the objects are displayed.
The property columns and the order in which they are displayed are determined by the property column set and the column items. However, a column
set’s column items can be configured to conditionally display on the same form when selected from different plants.
Some classified objects return the property column set defined by the class definition as Advanced Search does not use a client API configured column
set.
If the object names are configured to open a specific view method, when you select the hyperlink any attached files are displayed using the
corresponding application.
Results table options
The following commands are available in the results table.
Select this
To do this
ADD TO PINBOARD
Add the selected object(s) to Pins.
EXPORT ALL TO EXCEL
Export all the results to a Microsoft Excel spreadsheet.
 The results open directly as a Microsoft Excel XLSX file, or you can
open the results from your designated save folder.
ALL
Select all the objects displayed in a single page in the results table.
Open in new tab 
Open the action results in a new browser tab.
 Logging out in one tab automatically logs you out in all other
open tabs. You will lose any unsaved changes.
Refresh 
Refresh the Results table with updated items, such as your To Do List
tasks.
We do not recommend you export large data sets using the Export All to Excel command. You should instead use reports as they are specifically
developed to handle large amounts of complex data.
Only the action shortcuts applicable to a specific object are displayed in the Actions menu.
The order and type of property columns displayed is determined by the property column set.
Filter or sort the results
At the top of the required column, select Column Settings 
, and then select one of the following:
Select this
To do this
Sort Ascending
Sort the results in ascending order.
Sort Descending
Sort the results in descending order.
Columns
Select the columns displayed in the table.
Filter
Filter the table using standard parameters, such as Contains.
How do you use the Filter settings?
You can select different Filter options in each column to refine the results to a specific set of objects. Filtered columns display a filter flag 
.
Select the Filter option, and enter the required value to filter the column results by that value.
The following table shows the main filter options:
Filter option
Description
Is equal to
Use the Is equal to operator to filter for objects that equal a value in the
column item.
For example, you filter the State column to see only objects where State is
set to Current.
Contains
Use the Contains operator to filter for objects that contain a specific value in
the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects that contain the value
Gas.
Starts with
Use the Starts with operator to filter for objects that start with a value found
in the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column so only objects where the title starts
with Gas.
Ends with
Use the Ends with operator to filter for objects that end with a value in the
column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects where the title ends with
Sheet.
Is empty
Use the Is empty operator to filter for objects that have an empty value in
the column item.
For example, you filter the Title column for objects that have an empty value
set in the title.
 The Is not empty operator filters for objects that don't have an
empty value in the column item.
Is null
Use the Is null operator to filter for objects that have a NULL value in the
column item.
For example, you filter the Criticality Rating column for objects that have a
NULL value in the column.
 The Is not null operator filters for objects that don't have a NULL
value in the column item.
Has no value
Use the Has no value operator to filter for objects that have either an empty
value or a NULL value in the column item.
For example, you filter the Criticality Rating column for objects that either
have no value set or have a NULL value in the column.
 The Has value operator filters for objects that don't have an empty
value or a NULL value in the column item.
Is greater than
Use the Is greater than operator to filter for objects that have a value
greater than the value provided.
For example, you filter the Version column for objects that have a value
greater than 1 set as their version.
Is less than
Use the Is less than operator to filter for objects that have a value less than
the value provided.
For example, you filter the Version column for objects that have a value less
than 3 set as their version.
The list of filter options depends on the configuration of the column set. Not every filter is available for each column item in the list.
Depending on the column item selected, some filter options can show a list of existing values in the Select Value list.
You can use the And or Or syntax to narrow results to include a range of values, especially when there are multiple values in the same column item. For
example, by setting Starts with to 100 and Ends with 150, only objects between those values are displayed.
You can combine multiple filters to display a specific set of data. For example, using the Contains and Ends with filters, you can filter the Name column
using Contains 1011, an AND, and Ends with HSE. Only objects with names that contain 1011 and end with HSE are displayed.
You can use wildcards to further target results. For example, if you use *(all) with 01 to filter for objects, (01*), you will get every object with 01, such as
01, 01A, 01B, 01C, and so on
Full text search option
The Full Text Search option in the Search Bar 
 allows you to search for text contained in objects or associated with attached files that have been indexed
and managed using Full Text Retrieval (FTR).
 If you want to use Full Text Retrieval (FTR), it must be configured in your system using the Desktop Client. For more information, see Configure Full
Text Retrieval (FTR) . The Web Client can access a system that uses Full Text Retrieval (FTR) without any further configuration.
The FTR process indexes the object metadata and file content for faster retrieval of information. When you use the Full Text Search option, the managed
index is used to list the objects by the individual words with their locations in the files.
You can use search string operators in the search bar, such as * Wildcard, And, Or, and so on. You can also use the following options to target the search
content.
Select this option
To do this
PROPERTIES
Search for the text only in the properties of objects.
CONTENT
Search the content of attached files.
FULL
Search the content including the properties of objects.
The search results are returned in the FTR Search window displayed under separate tabs for each classification type, such as Documents, Tags, and so on.
The search result can include documents with attached files that contain text that match the search criteria. If a document contains multiple attached
files, you must expand each file to find the text you searched for.
You can select a tab and export the search results on the current page to Excel using the ExportToExcel command. You can also export the search
results on all the pages to Excel for the currently selected tab using the ExportAllToExcel command.
Reporting on your data
 eLearning is available for this topic. Check it out here: HxGN SDx® | Introduction and Working with Reports
<https://universityhexagonali.360learning.com/course/play/652bdf04c49e6ec61e9855d3>
Reports are located in the relevant feature sets. For example, a document revision report is available in the Documents feature set.
You can use the following types of reports in the Web Client:
Saved reports to Excel, ListView, and more from the Reports feature set.
Summary report charts from tiles in the relevant feature sets.
Formatted Word reports on objects.
Visual intelligence reports on published models (that are transferred to the Facilities Data Warehouse using Data Validator) or models streamed from the
Visualization Data Service (VDS) within View and Markup.
The following output formats are supported by Adhoc reports in the Web Client:
XHTML - The results are displayed in a new browser tab.
ListView - The results are displayed in a list format in a new window.
CSV - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file, one row per data item.
CSV Repeated - The results can be downloaded as a CSV formatted text file with rows repeated for each primary and related object.
Excel (OpenXML) - The results can be downloaded as an Excel file. There is an option to use a template Excel file that can contain a macro to run to
further manipulate the data as required. The macro must be specified to run when the file is opened, as Open XML does not support the running of
macros.
Custom Report - The results can be sent to third-party applications, such as Crystal Reports or Adobe Acrobat.
What are the report formats supported in the Desktop Client and the Web Client?
The following table contains all the report formats that are supported in the Desktop Client and the Web Client.

CSV

 Running the reports using query scopes returns the data based on the query scopes set. The results are displayed in tabs in an alphabetical order
that corresponds to each selected configuration. For more information on query scopes, see Understanding scopes.
Run saved reports
Reports provide a quick way to view project or plant information, perform complex queries, and view information across a structure of data. When you select a
report, it either opens the data in a grid view or exports the data in different formats, depending on the configuration. Based on the configuration, some reports
allow you to add additional filters to get specific data in the results, and this type of report is commonly referred to as a report with a prompt.
In the relevant feature set, select the required report. The report runs and displays in the selected format.
Reports with the New Window output style appear in a grid view.
Reports with the XHTML output style open in a new browser tab.
Reports with the CSV, CSV Repeated, or Excel (Open XML) output style are downloaded to your computer.
All the reports displayed in the Web Client are created in the Desktop Client using report definitions. For more information on managing the reports, see
Configure reports.
The user role and the scope selected restrict the availability of the reports. For more information, see Understanding scopes.
Each report configured to be displayed appears with an icon that indicates how the results are returned.
Icons
Report execution and accessing results
Run -
Grid
view
The report data is displayed in a grid view. You can save the report to a file using the EXPORT TO EXCEL button.
Downloa
d
The report is executed and downloaded to your computer as a CSV, CSV Repeated, or Excel (Open XML) file, depending on the format
configured in the report.
Prompt
The report provides the ability to add run-time criteria on top of the configured report criteria.
Launch -
New tab
When you select the XHTML report format, the report is executed and displayed in a new browser tab.
Backgro
und
The report is executed in the background, and a notification message is generated. When the report execution is complete, you can
download the report result from Notifications 
.
The report is configured to display the result in a file. The supported file formats are CSV, CSV Repeated, and Excel (Open XML).
File
downloa
d
Along with the report result in a file, this report also downloads files attached to objects in the report based on configuration.
The report is downloaded to your computer as a CSV, CSV Repeated, or Excel (Open XML) file, depending on the format configured in
the report.
Ignore
Config
The report gathers all the data configured for the report at the site level without considering the configurations, such as plants or
projects, and the report result is downloaded to your computer in a single file.
The supported file formats are CSV and CSV Repeated.
Run a report with a prompt (Query)
You can provide search criteria for a few reports before they run, which allows you to view different data in a report without creating multiple reports. For more
information about defining a report that uses criteria to filter the results of a report, see Define filter criteria for an Adhoc report and Define criteria and prompts.
 Reports are made available in the Web Client by an administrator, who relates relevant reports to the appropriate feature sets. For more information,
see Expose Adhoc reports in the Web Client and Expose summary reports in Web Client feature sets.
What do the Criteria and Prompt options do?
Criteria - It allows you to add filter criteria to a report. An administrator can only add it during the report configuration process and modify it later when
required.
Prompt - It allows you to add run-time filter criteria to the report output using properties. To use this functionality, an administrator must enable
the Prompt option in the report property column.
1. Search for the report that you want to run, and select it.
2. Provide criteria by entering the appropriate parameters and values in the Operator and Value boxes to return a targeted set of data in the result.
What are the operators I can use to modify the query?
The OPERATOR option allows you to specify search operators to refine your search to a specific set of objects. There are two sets of operators you can
use, equals and like, depending on the property type selected.
If the property is a value in a list available by default or if you use the words Null or Blank as the value, only the equals operator is available. The
operator automatically changes to equals when you enter Null or Blank.
You can use the wildcards to define search criteria for a string property that matches one or more characters using the like or equals operator.
The following table shows the operators for strings, doubles, integers, small integers, and select lists:
Parameter
Function
equals 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in
the VALUE box, including wildcards.
not equal to 
Includes items where the value of this property does not match what
you entered in the VALUE box.
less than 
Includes items where this property value is less than what you entered
in the VALUE box.
greater than 
Includes items where this property value is greater than what you
entered in the VALUE box.
less than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or less than what
you entered in the VALUE box.
greater than or equal to 
Includes items where this property value is equal to or greater than
what you entered in the VALUE box.
like 
Includes items where this property value matches what you entered in
the VALUE box, including wildcards.
 The behavior of the equals and like operators is identical. You can use either of these when you are defining the search criteria using wildcards.
An asterisk (*) matches zero or more characters in your search. For example, if you specify pa*, the software returns objects where that property
value contains words that start with the characters pa, such as pan, park, part, and so on.
You can use an underscore (_) or a question mark (?) to match a single character. For example, if you specify pi_ or pi?, the software returns pid,
pit, and pip, but not pipe.
To search for objects where the property value includes an underscore, question mark, or asterisk character, enclose them in square brackets. For
example, use 100[*] to return 100*, but not 100abc.
You can search for objects with a property that contains a single character in a specific range of characters by enclosing them in square brackets.
For example, using [a-f] returns only objects where the value of the property includes a to f in a set of characters. So, if you specify pi[a-p], the
software returns objects where the property value is pid and pip, but not pit, as 't' is outside the range.
You can use a caret (^) character to search for objects where a property value does not include a character in a range or set. For example, if you
specify pi[^a-p], the software returns objects where the property value is pit, but not pid or pip, as 'd' and 'p' are in the range a-p.
3. Select RUN REPORT to run the report.
Select 
 to add a row to provide multiple criteria for a property.
Select AND or OR when you want to provide multiple criteria for a property in a report.
You can run a report without providing any input, if you do not want to filter the result.
For example, you can filter the report result by entering parameters for the following properties, as shown in the image below:
Run a background report
Administrators can configure reports to run in the background. Background reports allow you to continue working while the report is being generated.
1. In the relevant feature set, select the required report. A notification message is displayed.
2. After the report runs successfully, download it from Notifications 
. For more information, see View notifications of reports.
View notifications of reports
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see View notifications of reports (modified in an update).
You can view, download, and delete the background Adhoc report results from Notifications.
1. In the Web Client, select Notifications 
.
2. Select VIEW NOTIFICATION 
 to view the complete details of a report notification.
A notification is created in the Web Client for a report selected to run in the background.
Select DOWNLOAD REPORT 
 to download the report.
Only reports with Excel (Open XML), CSV, and CSV Repeated formats are supported to download from Notifications.
Select DELETE NOTIFICATION 
 to delete the report notification.
Run formatted Word reports
You can download a formatted Word report on data structures such as Technical Queries and Transmittals. For more information on configuring formatted
Word reports, see Configure formatted Word reports.
Summary Reports
The Summary Reports, or business intelligence reports, provide a convenient way to view key business metrics and engineering data in the form of charts. For
example, you can generate a report to view documents that have a status related to a specific owner. You configure Summary Reports in the Desktop Client
for use in the Web Client. For more information, see Expose summary reports in Web Client feature sets.
Summary reports are available as tiles and are located in the relevant feature sets. They are identified by the 
 icon. For more information on customizing
feature sets to display Summary Reports, see Expose summary reports in Web Client feature sets.
Searching for customer modifications
You can search and query for any customized schema or administration objects in your system that have been updated or modified as part of a project. In this
way, you can identify which objects have been modified from the delivered out-of-the-box (OOTB) as-built configuration. You can also identify in which
configuration and version the objects were modified and from what product source the modification came from, such as SDx or Data Validator, and so on.
The types of modifications that are supported include:
Modifications made by a single specific product, such as Data Validator.
Modifications made by the customer, such as objects or relationships that are created, added, modified, or terminated.
Configurations modified by the customer and subsequently updated by a product update.
Modifications made over a timescale, so you can find objects using differently dated queries and compare the results.
Using ENS with documents and objects
The Engineering Numbering System (ENS) provides automated object identification numbering that allows you to apply a specific identification format section
to create forms as you create objects and documents in the system. You can also reserve a sequence of ENS numbers that can be allocated to objects in the
future for a specific project. The ENS identifying format that you can use is defined in an ENS definition.
Can you make ENS display items mandatory on forms?
The ENS fields in display items for ENS Definitions are mandatory by default on the create or copy forms to ensure that the ENS is correctly applied to data as
it is created. However, for some ENS definitions, you can stop this by clearing the Mandatory on the form option as you create an ENS definition.
You can preview the ENS items added to the ENS definition in the ENS summary. This includes any ENS items that contain a classification. For more
information, see ENS preview summary.
You can control the display of ENS in the object create forms using the Relationship Builder. For more information, see Control ENS display in create
forms.
For more information on configuring an ENS definition, see Configure the Engineering Numbering System (ENS).
Create multiple documents or objects with ENS
When the ENS definition has been configured, you can create multiple objects or documents using the ENS fields displayed in a dynamic Main details section
in the create or copy forms. You enter the number of documents or objects you want to create in the following fields:
ENS Start Sequence - allows you to select the ENS number from which you want to start numbering.
Number to create - indicates the number of ENS numbers to create.
 As you add the details in the create or copy form, the ENS definition identifiers are displayed in the dynamic section.
For example, the following image displays a ENS definition configured with four ENS items for the Create Data Book form. The first ENS item is configured
with the Name property set as a Constant, the second and third ENS items are configured with Related Items set to different classification relationship
definition UIDs, and the last ENS item is configured with Function set to one as the ENS Sequence start number.
When you have completed the ENS object details in the create or copy form wizard, you can preview the sequence of any created ENS numbers in the ENS
Summary page. For more information, see ENS preview summary.
If you creating more than one document, you cannot upload any file attachments in this process.
Only numbers are supported as pad characters for sequence type ENS items.
ENS preview summary
You can preview the created ENS definition numbered objects in the Summary page, which shows:
The total number of ENS objects created, starting with the first identifier in the ENS sequence.
The total number of ENS objects that cannot be created due to conflicts.
The current status of the ENS objects.
This following image shows the ENS for five data books with related ENS items set with the relationship definition UIDs for two different classifications.
Every object with a conflicted ENS number is listed with the reason why it was not created, such as the number already exists, it is locked, or the number
was previously reserved.
If a start number is not selected, the sequence will start at the first available number in the system.
If the sequence includes a section of numbers already allocated, the summary will indicate those and continue after those allocated numbers.
When creating documents or objects related to ENS, the preview summary displays only the primary ENS definitions. Documents or objects linked from
primary ENS definitions to secondary ENS definitions are not displayed in the preview summary, and they do not follow the rules defined in the
secondary ENS definitions.
For more information on configuring an ENS definition, see Configure the Engineering Numbering System (ENS).
Using the Info Map
You can use the Info Map to dynamically visualize objects and their relationships to other objects, all displayed in an easy to view graphical representation.
When you select an object, the Info Map displays it as a central node surrounded by any related objects displayed as separate nodes. When you select
another object node, you make that object the focus of the Info Map so it is now displayed as the central node surrounded by its own related objects.
How can I interact with the Info Map?
Drag and reposition individual object nodes in the window using the mouse or touch screen control.
Zoom and resize objects in the display using the mouse wheel or touch screen control.
You interact with the Info Map and change how objects are presented using the following tool-bar commands:
Name
Icon
Description
Single Mode
Displays a single node surrounded by its related objects and relationships.
Follow Mode
Displays multiple nodes each surrounded by their related objects and
relationships, which allows you to follow the expansion and grow a relationship
tree.
 The follow mode is disabled when you view the Info Map from the
object details form.
Reset
Resets the node positions back to their original position.
Clear History
Clears the Info Map expansion history and returns the display the selected node
and its related objects.
Hide Node Names
Hides the names of each node displayed in the window.
Show Node Names
Shows the names of each node displayed in the window.
Zoom to Fit
Fits all the displayed object nodes into the window.
Undo
Undoes the last selected node expansion.
Redo
Redoes the last selected node expansion.
What are the node commands?
When you hover over any object node, the following commands display around the selected node:
Name
Icon
Description
Actions
Opens the Actions menu for the selected object node.
Details
Displays the Object Details form for the selected object node.
View and Markup
Displays any files attached to the selected object node.
 Due to a limitation in Microsoft Internet Explorer, these commands can become hidden behind other nodes. Drag the node clear of any nodes to see
them.
 A number count value on a node indicates that the selected node has multiple related objects. By default, when a node displays a count value greater
than three (>3), selecting the node displays the related objects in a results grid. The value to trigger this action is not configurable.
Create a relationship tree
Select Follow Mode to expand multiple nodes and their related objects to create a relationship tree.
Each time you select another object node, its related objects and relationships are also expanded out in addition to the previous expanded nodes. In this way,
you can create a relationship tree, and expand a visual representation of relationships from one object to another.
Limitations using Info Map
There are variations in functionality using Info Map due to differences in the operating software and the device being used, such as Apple iPads or Android
tablets. This means you interact differently with the UI depending on the device you use. For example:
Apple devices require two clicks to select a node, a click to open the node options, and a click to select an option.
Android devices require you to select and hold a node to open the node options, and a click to select an option.
Node commands can be obscured if you use Microsoft Internet Explorer (IE). This is due to a technical limitation imposed by Microsoft Internet Explorer.
Drag the selected node clear of other elements to see the node commands.
Using workflows
Workflows in SDx are ready for you to use. Each workflow is a set of steps that represents business processes to help you manage tasks that must be
completed and focus on workflow steps assigned to you.
Make workflows work for you
Workflows are ready for you to use straight away.
Configure automatic reassignment of workflow steps for out-of-office or disabled users
You can configure the automatic reassignment of workflow steps to take place when the user is away from the organization, temporarily or permanently, by
assigning a deputy for the user.
When a user is away for one or more days, temporary automatic reassignment of workflow steps and review objects takes place. For a disabled user,
permanent automatic reassignment of workflow steps and review objects takes place.
In both cases, a scheduler task processes the pending workflow steps and the corresponding review objects, and then reassigns these to the selected deputy.
All the supporting information, comments, and actions related to review objects are also reassigned to the selected deputy.
 The same process is applicable for consolidation steps.
Reassignment of workflow steps to an existing reviewer
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has not completed their review, the user becomes the deputy, but
continues working through their original review. Any actions or review comments from the user that is out-of-office or disabled are transferred to the deputy.
The workflow step for the out-of-office or disabled user is terminated, and the review comments and actions of the user will not appear in any reports.
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has completed their review, then the workflow step from the out-of-office
user is not transferred to the deputy, and actions and comments are not transferred.
Reassignment of workflow steps to the consolidator
If a workflow step is reassigned to a user who is the consolidator, the consolidation step takes precedence over the review step. Any actions or review
comments from the user that is out of office or disabled are transferred to the consolidator so that they can review them and act upon them. The workflow step
for the out-of-office or disabled user is terminated, and the review comments and actions of the user will not appear in any reports.
Reassignment of a consolidation step to a reviewer
If a consolidation workflow step is reassigned to a user who is already a reviewer and that reviewer has not completed their review, all actions and review
comments from reviewers are transferred and the deputy becomes the consolidator of all actions and comments from other reviewers. If the reviewer has
completed their review, his review is left intact, and he performs the consolidation of all actions and review comments.
For more information, see Allow reassignment of workflow steps.
Allow reassignment of workflow steps
The Allow workflow step auto reassignment option in the system options enables workflow steps to be reassigned to deputies. Scheduler tasks process the
steps and move them to the deputy's inbox.
In the system options, in the Workflow options details section, select the Allow workflow step auto reassignment option..
 When the option is cleared, workflow steps cannot be reassigned automatically to the specific deputies.
In the system options, in the Workflow options details section, select the Allow workflow step auto reassignment option.
For more information on the system options, see Configure the system options.
Business administrators can transfer items to a new owner either permanently or temporarily. For more information, see Reassign tasks owned by one
user to another user.
Understand role criteria for filtering deputies
The following role criteria are used to filter the deputies that can be selected when a workflow step is reassigned. The options are SAMEROLE,
MANAGEDBYROLE, MANAGEROLE, and ANY. You can select multiple criteria, for example, SAMEROLE and MANAGEROLE, to filter available deputies.
SAMEROLE
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on those users who have the same roles or more roles. User2 is a valid deputy because it has the same
roles plus more than User1. User3 is not a valid deputy because it only matches fewer roles than User1.
MANAGEDBYROLE
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on those users who manage the user's role. User1 is related to Role1 which is managed, through a
relationship, by Role3. User2 is not directly managed by Role3, while User3 is directly related to Role3. User3 is a valid deputy because it has Role3, which
manages Role1. User2 is not a valid deputy because it is not directly managed by Role3, nor is its Role1 managed by Role3.
MANAGEROLE
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on those users who are directly managed by the same role. User1 is related to Role2 which manages,
through a relationship, Role4. User2 is directly related to Role4. User2 is a valid deputy because it is directly managed by the same role. User3 is not a valid
deputy because it has no relationship to Role4.
ANY
In this scenario, the software filters deputies based on users regardless of role. All users are valid deputies.
Manage your workflow steps
Workflow steps can be performed in sequence or, when multiple steps can happen at the same time, in parallel.
When a workflow step requires action from you, it appears in the your To Do List. The To Do List provides a place for you to view all workflow steps that have
been assigned to you and their statuses.
Workflow steps manage each stage of the process including validation, approval, distribution, and notification. Each workflow step has a target completion
date that determines when overdue notifications are sent and workflows expire.
 By default, the duration of each workflow step is set to zero. This means it is expected that a step will be closed on the same day it is started. For
information on where the step duration is configured, see Create a workflow step.
Understand how workflow steps behave
The following actions are available specifically for workflow steps in your To Do List. You see these actions only when they are applicable to the selected
workflow item:
Action
When to choose
What happens next
Add recipients
You want to add one or more users to the original workflow
step that is already initiated.
The workflow remains at this step, and the recipients get this step in
their To Do List.
 The target date of the recipient step does not change. For
information on how to configure this setting, see Configure the system
options.
Claim
Because multiple users can have the same role, you must
claim a step to do the work required when it was assigned
to a role rather than individual users.
The workflow remains at this step until you take the necessary action
to move it forward.
Acknowledge
You need to confirm that work was done in previous steps.
The workflow moves to the next step.
Approve
You do not find any problems with the work done in
previous steps.
The workflow moves to the next step.
Reassign
You want to reassign the step to another user.
The workflow moves the step to the reassigned user.
 The target date of the step does not change. For information
on how to configure this setting, see Configure the system options.
Reject
You find a problem with the work done in previous steps.
The workflow moves to the defined rejection path, which can be
another workflow or another step in the same workflow.
Retry
You fixed an issue and want to execute the workflow step
again.
The workflow step gets executed and moves to the defined path,
which can be another workflow or another step in the same workflow.
Cancel
You cannot perform the work for the step.
The workflow moves to the defined cancelation path, which can be
another workflow or another step in the same workflow.
Complete
You have finished the work for the step.
The workflow moves to the accept step.
Unable to Complete
You cannot complete the step.
The workflow moves to the rejection step.
Document Review
You can conduct a document review.
The document review page opens so you can review the document.
Manage Review Plan
You want to update your review plans.
The review plan page opens so you can manage your review plans.
 Workflow steps can be configured to prompt you to re-enter your password as you try to submit a workflow step before the workflow can continue. For
more information, see Configure workflow step re-authentication.
Acknowledge an information step
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Acknowledge.
2. Type any details in the Acknowledge box, and select OK.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
Approve a workflow step
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Approve.
2. Type any details in the Approve box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
Approve or reject a transmittal
1. In the To Do List, select one or more transmittals, and either approve or reject them.
2. Type any details for the recipient of the next step.
 To complete the approve step, the To organization must be set to the same originating or collaborating organization on the document.
Cancel a workflow step
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Cancel.
2. Type any details in the Cancel box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
Claim a workflow step
Steps can be set up so that one user can respond for all recipients or respond for all recipients that share the same role. If the step is set up in either way, you
must claim the step before you can complete the action it requires.
1. In the To Do List, select a step, and select Actions 
 > Claim.
2. Type any details in the Claim box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
Complete a workflow step
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Complete.
2. Type any details in the Complete box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
3. Select Completed.
Reject a workflow step
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Reject.
2. Type any details in the Reject box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
Indicate a workflow step cannot be completed
What happens if a workflow step cannot be completed?
If you indicate that a step cannot be completed, the step disappears from your To Do List, and the workflow moves through the defined rejection path, which
can be another workflow or another step in the same workflow.
1. In the To Do List, select one or more steps, and select Actions 
 > Unable to Complete.
2. Type any details in the Unable to Complete box.
Comments are saved with the object and shared with the recipient of the next step.
Messages to the next step are saved with this step and shared with the recipients of the next step.
3. Select Unable to Complete.
Attach a workflow to an object
When a submittal, action, transmittal, or technical query is created, a workflow is automatically attached. However, for documents or folders, you must
manually attach a workflow after creating them.
1. Select an object from the list, and select Actions 
 > Workflow > Attach Workflow.
2. In the Attach Workflow dialog, select a workflow from the list, and then select OK.
 Workflows are displayed based on the interfaces you selected when creating workflows.
3. When the attach process is complete, refresh all views to see the updated data for the objects and their related workflow.
Delete an attached workflow
You can delete a workflow from a selected workflow item using the Delete Workflow action.
Select a workflow object, and click Actions 
 > Delete Workflow.
After you delete an attached workflow from a workflow object, the following changes are made:
The relationship between the object and the workflow is removed and the workflow status of the object is cleared.
All the workflow history for the object is removed from the workflow.
Remaining workflow steps for the object are removed from each user’s To Do List.
Terminate an attached workflow
You can terminate an attached workflow for a specific workflow item using the Terminate Workflow action.
1. Select a workflow item, and click Actions 
 > Terminate Workflow.
2. Click Yes in the Terminate Workflow dialog, and type a reason for the workflow termination.
 This only terminates the relationship, it does not terminate the object or the workflow.
After you terminate an attached workflow, the following changes are made:
The relationship between the object and the workflow is terminated.
Workflow history records the termination and the point in the workflow where it happened.
All historical information regarding steps processed prior to termination are saved.
All uncompleted steps for the object in the workflow are terminated and removed from the To Do List for all users.
View a workflow details report for an object
A workflow details report provides information about each step of the workflow attached to the selected object. It helps in analyzing the workflow and deciding
if any changes are required.
1. Select an object from the list, and then select Actions 
.
2. In the Actions menu, select Workflow > Workflow Details Report.
 The report is displayed in a new browser tab.
Correct PDF rendition generation failures
This functionality was modified in an update. For more information, see Correct PDF rendition generation failures (modified in an update).
When renditions do not generate correctly, the system directs the workflow to a step to correct the failure. The submitter is notified and can contact a system
administrator to determine the issue and fix it.
When the problem is fixed, the workflow progresses to the next step.
Reassign work to a different user
When you are unavailable or out of the office, you can reassign your own work to a different user. However, your business administrator can also configure
steps in a workflow so that they can be reassigned to another user.
 Your logged on user details are also displayed in the reassign window so you can assign tasks back to yourself. Administrator users may also want to
help the workflow by unblocking other users.
Reassign your tasks temporarily
You can reassign your workflow tasks to another user for a designated period of time.
When should tasks be reassigned and by whom?
If you are out of the office, you or a business administrator can temporarily reassign tasks to another user. During the dates you are away, any tasks
assigned to you are automatically reassigned to your designated user. The task is removed from your To Do List, and added to the To Do List of the
designated user. Tasks the designated user does not complete can be manually reassigned back to you when you return.
When you cannot complete tasks, you can reassign them to other users who have similar roles.
1. Select Settings 
 > Task Reassignment 
.
2. Select the From Date and To Date during which your tasks will be assigned to another user.
3. In Assignee Filters, select the role criteria, Same Role, Managed by Role, Manage Role, or Any Role, to filter the list of users to those that can be
assigned the tasks.
4. In Assignee Selection, search for and select the user to which you want to reassign the items using one of the following:
Select a Project, and select a user from the Assignee list in the same row. This user is assigned the workflow tasks only for that project. You can
select different users for each project.
Select Assignee  options, and select a Common Assignee. A Common Assignee is a user that has a common role across a single project or
multiple projects and can be assigned your tasks for projects based on that common role.
5. Select FINISH.
 When you return to the office, you can run the My active workflow reassignments or My Document Review Assignments reports to see the current
status of any tasks that were reassigned to your deputy. You can ask your deputy to manually reassign any uncompleted tasks back to yourself.
Reassign a workflow step
1. Select a workflow step, and click Actions 
 > Reassign.
2. Select a user from a selected role to reassign the workflow step to that designated user.
You can only reassign workflow steps to a single user, not multiple users.
You can filter both the roles and users to narrow the list to a specific user.
When reassigning a workflow step:
A consolidate step takes precedence over the review step or document receipt notification step.
A review step takes precedence over the document receipt notification step.
You cannot reassign any notification steps.
What happens when I reassign a workflow step in a QA Review workflow?
The following table details the outcome when you reassign a workflow step to another user in a QA Review workflow:
User 1
Reassigns to User 2 who has

The consolidate step is
reassigned to User 2.

The review step is terminated,
and the consolidate step is
reassigned to User 2.
Consolidator

The document receipt
notification step is terminated,
and the consolidate step is
reassigned to User 2.
Reviewer
No workflow step
The review step is reassigned to
User 2.
Reviewer

The review step is terminated
for User 1.
Reviewer

The document receipt
notification step is terminated,
and the review step is
reassigned to User 2.
These priorities are delivered by default and can be configured by your administrator.

The following table details the outcome when you reassign a workflow step to another user in a project communication workflow:
