Glossary
3D reader
Validates tags, creates and cross-references the tags to the documents, and extracts visual file and tag data from the 3D models.
access token
A system object that contains client and user information used by APIs to authorize data access. An access token can be validated by a resource. Clients
request access tokens and forward them to an API.
action pins
Markup elements you can add to a markup layer to create a graphical locator that is linked to a newly created action.
actions
Common operations on one or more selected objects. Actions are specific to an object type and contain commands, such as create, update, view, and manage
objects.
In the Web Client, they are grouped for display in the actions menu and grouped by type.
In Data Validator, the import, validation and load processes can run m such as create, update, and delete on the object data. These operations can vary
depending on what the column header is mapped to, such as an object, a property, or a relationship.
active scope
A configuration in which you may view, create, modify, and delete information in the software.
alias tag
A tag that is named differently, but represents the master tag in the system.
API
The Application Programming Interface.
API Services
A configurable interface that provides access to data within a site through an Application Programming Interface (API), which uses RESTful APIs provided in a
standardized format using the Open Data Protocol (OData).
as-built
The set of data that describes the existing conditions of a plant or site; the completed and approved state of a project.
asset
A physical object which is the physical part located in the facility, with its own serial number.
attribute
An object characteristic or property.
authorization token
A system object that contains enough information for the process to achieve sufficient confidence in the binding to request that the user can be authenticated
by the Authorization Server.
auto-generate
An option to automatically generate validation rule definitions, export mappings, and rules, based on actions. The validation rules are used to validate imported
and exported data and the export mappings ensure that the column headers match the objects and properties found in the target system.
brownfield
An existing project or area that has constraints imposed due to prior work and contains existing data.
business administrator
A user that is responsible for the project infrastructure and configuration, as well as managing contracts, disciplines, users, the WBS, and the PBS.
business objects
Complex objects representing more than one class definition.
cache
Memory that stores recently accessed data so that subsequent requests to access the same data can be processed quickly.
cardinality
A setting on a relationship definition that specifies how many instances of a relationship are valid for the objects at the end of the relationship. For example, a
tag cannot exist without a primary classification relationship and also cannot have more than one primary classification related to it.
check in
The process of moving a file from a user location to a storage location and recording that location in the database.
client API
A .dll (dynamic link library) that performs a particular function in the software. Client APIs are processed by a specific section of code in a component.
column headers
They are used in the software as the basis for mapping new data in columns to match an existing structured database.
common assignee
A user that has a common role across a single project or multiple projects and can be assigned workflow tasks for projects based on that common role.
condition
An object that can restrict access to a method, workflow, or relationship based on specified criteria.
configuration
A sub-set of a database system that acts as a suppository for managing configuration items as well as relationships to the assets for that specific part or plant.
A database system can be made up of multiple configurations.
configuration tree
A representation in a tree list, which may include plant, areas, units, and projects, that indicates the structure in which the data is stored.
configurationTop
ConfigurationTop refers to the top-level configuration context within SDx. It serves as the foundational layer for organizing and accessing documents and
relationships that are not scoped to specific plant or project configurations.
content discovery task
Extracts content from the master file, relates master and alias tags to the documents, and relates documents to the organizational items.
contract
A group of documents that are collected and issued for bid, construction, review, and so on.
contractor
A company or industry that undertakes a contract to supply a product, materials, or a service for a specific project, such as the construction of a plant.
CORS
Cross-origin resource sharing (CORS) is a mechanism that allows restricted resources found in a web site, such as Fonts, JavaScript and so on, to be
requested from a domain outside of the originating domain.
create scope
A configuration for data creation, modification, and termination in the software.
CSV
A comma-separated value (CSV) file is a file format used to store tabular data as a spreadsheet or datasheet, which can be imported or exported from a
program.
current document
A document that has been signed off. Current documents can be revised in the software, but not checked out or in.
data files
A job can process multiple input files and therefore use multiple input mappings. Import mappings are defined on the job definition.
Data Validator
Data Validator, the software used for importing data into the staging area, validating the data, and/or exporting data to a target system.
database
Collection of files of comprehensive information that have predefined structure and organization; a specific program can communicate, interpret, or process
these files.
database domain discovery task
A process that uses a defined database reader pattern to connect to a database and extract documents and tags along with their properties as defined within
the pattern.
database reader
Reads data directly from a database. Administrators can define specific tables and fields to be transferred so that they can be used for data comparisons.
database reader pattern
Defines the database tables and properties that need to be extracted and mapped to classes and properties within the software database.
delimiter
A character or a sequence of characters used to separate individual elements in a string, file, or data stream.
deliverable milestone
Defines a targeted result and tracks the progress for the deliverable.
deputy
A user that is selected as a substitute for an out-of-office or disabled user.
design file
A file generated by a design tool, such as Smart P&ID or Aspen Basic Engineering.
display item
Object used to present data or relationships on a form in the software.
distribution matrix
A list of people who will receive a transmittal created in the software and an indication of what is expected from each recipient. Workflows can also be
configured to use a distribution matrix for sending To Do List or e-mail notifications to workflow step recipients.
document
An object used to track revisions to a design file in the software.
document attribute
A constant which allows additional information to be added to the data created in the software.
document discovery
Reads documents (typically office generated files) and using patterns loads them into the software.
document discovery pattern
Defines the base directory that needs to be crawled, the file name pattern to extract and the document name pattern that needs to be created. Additionally,
document attributes can also be defined.
document discovery task
A process that uses a document discovery pattern to crawl a directory system and load documents and files that match the specified file pattern. After which
thumbnails are created and related to the document.
document index
A defined excel list or a defined data object within the software that defines metadata about a file. This is referenced when files are loaded into the system and
if a match is found the file will be loaded with the properties defined from the document index.
document master
An object used to group all the revisions of a document in the software.
document name pattern
Defines a document name for files with a specific file name pattern comprised of the parts, constants, and documents attributes.
document naming system
Defines the parts of a file name. Additional information can be defined to define constants and relate them to existing objects in the database when data
extraction occurs.
document renditions
Copies of a document generated in formats different from the originals, typically created during the document review, technical query, or actions workflow
process. A PDF rendition is a type of alternate rendition, which is a viewable version the original file, often used for consistent display and review purposes.
document review
A document review allows your organization's technical experts, engineers, and stakeholders to conduct quality assurance reviews of documents delivered by
your suppliers and contractors.
document revision
An officially recognized change to a document.
domain
A set of data that is segregated in a domain and managed independently in the software. Data segregation improves performance and maintains data integrity.
For example, data about workflows is stored in a separate domain from data imported through SmartPlant Fusion or Data Validator.
domain tag
A representation of the tag, specifically from a discipline within a domain.
drawing reader
Extracts cross-referenced and linked information contained in a drawing file.
duplicate document
A document with multiple files attached but different file types or multiple versions of the same document attached to it.
edge definition
Single or multiple relationship definitions with direction. In the schema, an edge definition is used to traverse from a starting object to related objects.
entry point
Provides access to the underlying database data to the Web Client. It can be configured to provide full access control for each exposed object class definitions
or interface definitions. The system automatically configures any valid relationship or edge definitions between the exposed types.
enumerated entry
A member of an enumerated list that defines one possible value for a property in the schema. Enumerated values are sometimes called enumerated entries or
abbreviated to enums.
export mapping
a target system.
export process
The Export process uses a defined mapping, based on the structure of the target system, to manage the loading of the data into the final destination system.
exposes
The relationship between interface definitions and property definitions in the schema. Interface definitions expose the property definitions for class definitions.
extensible markup language (XML)
Extensible Markup Language; a format frequently used for exporting data from the database or importing data into the system. XML files must conform to the
structure defined by the application schema. In an integrated application environment, all documents published or retrieved in an integrated environment use
this format.
feature sets
A configured set of object functions that can be used to quickly access data or run reports and commands.
file index
Specifies the file name, associated name, and attributes for a document before it is processed by the document discovery task.
file name parts
Sections that represent data in a file name.
file name pattern
Consists of one or more file parts with each file part being defined as a document naming system item.
file properties or attributes
Values that are stored on the file object. The data is transferred to the document if the file is selected for data extraction.
file server
A service that handles direct file transfer between vaults and the software.
file type
A setting in the software that specifies the format of attached files based on file extension. This setting determines how files are viewed, edited, and printed in
the software.
FIPS
Federal Information Processing Standards (FIPS) are issued to establish requirements for various purposes, such as ensuring computer security and
interoperability, and are intended for cases where suitable industry standards do not already exist.
folder
A grouping object that can contain a number of items. A folder can contain other folders, to build up a folder hierarchy. A folder can have an owner, which
makes it visible only for that user, and it can have an owning group, which allows access for members of the group. Some uses of a folder include grouping
objects, putting a folder's contents through a workflow, and generating a data list from a folder's contents.
form
A part of the software user interface that allows users to specify values for class definition properties.
function
A graphical tab that runs a predefined process, task, or administrative action, such as a search. Related functions can be grouped into sets relevant to a
selected feature set.
A computed functional code run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.
function code
A computed code function run at import, where the output value of a function depends only on the arguments that are input to the function.
GET
A request-response method between a client and server. A GET requests data from a specified resource.
Graphic Data Service (GDS)
Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings streamed from
the Visualization Data Service (VDS) to a SDx for Project As-Built configuration or when viewing Hexagon 2D drawings in SDx.
graphic report
A filtered view of a drawing or model, displayed graphically in the software.
Graphic View Control (GVC)
The viewer that provides viewing capabilities of Hexagon 3D models and 2D drawings.
greenfield
A project or area that is completely new and does not have any constraints imposed by prior work or existing data.
hierarchy
A classified structure with superiors, or roots, and subordinates, or dependents, used for grouping data.
host
A computer that stores files.
hotspotting
The process of adding hyperlinks in a document on the area where the tag exists in the file.
identity token
A system object that contains information about the user and details on how that user was authenticated and validated by the software.
image reader
Extracts tag and other related information contained within any image using an Optical Character Recognition (OCR) engine.
implicit delete
A component process used by the software, where a user can decide to implicitly delete or terminate a group of objects from the target system, because they
are no longer in the supplied input data submission.
implies
The relationship between two interface definitions in the schema. If an interface definition implies another interface definition, then any class definition that
realizes the first interface definition can also realize the implied interface definition.
import definition
A defined mapping of imported file objects, properties, and relationships from existing column headers to objects, properties, and relationships found in the
staging area database during the import process.
import process
The process that manages the import of data in the data file or files to the staging area using a defined mapping.
Info Map
A display system that uses a systematic approach to analyze, organize, and present clear and user-focused information, so that you can quickly visualize the
relationship details of an individual object.
integration
Technology that standardizes and improves communication among the various authoring tools used in design, construction, and operation of a plant.
Integration manages data exchange among these authoring tools, which enables sharing and re-use of plant information throughout the plant lifecycle.
interface definition
A named collection of property definitions that represents a role for a class definition in the schema.
inverted CSV file
See raw attribute format.
issue purpose
A designation given to a deliverable item to describe why the item has been issued, such as for review or construction.
IWA
Integrated Windows Authentication (IWA) refers to automatically authenticated connections between Microsoft Internet Information Services and other active
directory aware applications.
job
A defined object that carries information for the progress of data through a selected workflow, such as when data is imported, validated, and exported to a
target system.
job definition
A combined set of components configured for the import, validation, and export of data to a specific target system or multiple target systems.
JSON
JavaScript Object Notation (JSON) is an open standard format that uses human-readable text to transmit data objects, consisting of attribute-value pairs,
between a server and web application. JSON is an alternative to XML.
laser scan reader
Captures Leica TruView HDS information created from plant scans.
markup layer
A layer that contains saved actions and comments raised by a document reviewer on a document in the collaborative document review process.
master document register (MDR)
A master document register (MDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send documentation data to the owner company.
master file
The file that is used for data and content extraction.
master tag register (MTR)
A master tag register (MTR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send master tag data to the owner company.
master tags
Extracted tags that follow a set of standard naming conventions in a project or a plant that the engineers should follow for defining tag names.
merge
To promote applicable project data to the as-built plant.
method
A call to an API that allows users to perform actions on objects or interfaces in the software.
model
A physical object which is the requisitioned item and includes a model number.
notifications
Alerts to specific events in the software that appear by registering your interest in objects or object classes, such as the latest revision to a document.
OAuth
An open standard for authorization. OAuth provides client applications with a secure delegated access to server resources on behalf of a resource owner.
object weighting
A process that emphasizes the contribution of an object in a set of data to a final effect or result, thereby deciding its weight in the analysis. This affects the
order in which the data is exported, helping to achieve the desired result.
OData
Open Data Protocol (OData) is an OASIS standard that defines the best practices for building and consuming RESTful APIs.
orphan tags
Tags that are not related to any document or domain tag.
owning group
The user group to which an object is assigned in the software.
picklist
A list of applicable values for a given property in the software.
pinboard
An area in the software where you can add or pin objects so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Objects added to the pinboard are
available to you between log in sessions.
pins
Bookmarked objects that provide quick access to new items or objects added to the pinboard so you can quickly recall and manage them in the system. Pins
added to the pinboard are available to you between log in sessions. For more information, see action pins.
plant
An object that can represent the top level in the delivered data hierarchy. If you use a custom hierarchy, the object at the top level of the hierarchy may have a
different name.
POST
A request-response method between clients and servers. A POST submits data to a specified resource for processing.
progress
Functionality that allows users to track the development of deliverables, such as documents or items, against a project plan, indicating any deviations from the
original plan.
project
A logical unit of data that is a subset of the items that make up a plant. A project is used for making controlled, incremental changes to the data in a plant.
There can be multiple projects for a plant at any given time.
property
An object characteristic or attribute.
property definition
A basic attribute shared by all members of an object class. Property definitions are grouped using interface definitions in the schema.
property group
A group of properties.
property list
Allows you to create property group names (interface definition names) and properties (property definitions), which can be related to a business object (class
definitions).
publish
To share a document and its data in an integrated environment with other authoring tools by exporting an XML file containing the document data and
relationships. When a document is published, the software places the XML file in the appropriate vault and loads the data from the XML file into the database.
After the document is published, users can retrieve the data from the XML file located in the vault into other authoring tools.
In non-integrated environments, like SDx, documents and data cannot be published or retrieved by authoring tools. However, data that was previously
published in an integrated environment can be loaded into the database, using Data Validator.
QA review
A process where the user can verify the documents issued by a contractor.
query
A search based on object properties.
query definition
A comma separated list of properties that can be entered in the Target System Query Definition field, which identifies the object in the target system along
with the class definition. These properties can be retrieved by navigating the relationships.
query scope
A configuration for data queries and relationship expansions in the software.
raw attribute format
Where the data in a CSV file is organized in a vertical format and each row contains properties and relationships for the same object. Also referred to as an
inverted CSV file. The data in a standard CSV file is typically organized horizontally.
realizes
The relationship between class definitions and interface definitions in the schema. Class definitions realize interface definitions. The interface definitions that
are realized by a class definition expose the properties for that class definition.
Reason For Issue (RFI)
The reason a document is released as part of a transmittal.
reason for receipt (RFR)
The reason why a specific recipient was included on a transmittal or workflow step.
register interest
To subscribe or indicate a stake an object. When a new one becomes available or is updated, a notification appears in your notification list.
regular expression
A pattern that is used for tag matching and defining selected file parts.
Relationship Builder
A tool to quickly and easily select objects and relate them to each other using selected relationship definitions.
relationship definition
Associations between interface definitions in the schema. Relationship definitions identify two specific objects that fulfill the roles on each end of the
relationship.
relationship property
A property on the link interface which is part of the relationship definition. The link interface allows properties to be created on the relationship itself.
rendition
An alternate representation of the original document version that is created during the document review consolidation process. A PDF rendition is a type of
alternate rendition, which is a viewable file of a different file type.
representational state transfer (REST)
A set of architectural principles used to design Web services that focus on systems resources, including how resource states are addressed and transferred
over HTTP by a wide range of clients written in different languages. Web services employing a REST architecture read a designated Web page containing an
XML file that describes and includes the desired content.
responder
A user sending a response.
response
A reply from an external organization directed to an internal organization.
RESTful APIs
When Web services use REST architecture, they are called RESTful, or REST APIs.
retrieve
A process that imports document data from an .XML file submitted to maintain data consistency across the system. When you retrieve a document, the
retrieved data is analyzed in the database, which can place workflow tasks on a user's To Do List. The tasks on the To Do List allow you to create, delete, or
modify items at the appropriate time in the design or review process.
review assignment
Review assignment rules define which documents should be assigned to a review plan and to whom, either a discipline or an individual.
review plan
A review plan allows you to control the documents you want to review when new documents are added to the system or when existing documents have been
revised or updated in the system.
review plan item
An object that is created based on the review plan and used to identify the documents for review.
revision
An officially recognized change to a document. Each revision of a document may have multiple versions.
revision scheme
A numbering convention for document revisions.
role
Determines a user's level of access to data and functionality in a specific plant/project configuration. Users can belong to more than one role per configuration.
Roles are defined by system administrators and are based on related access groups, domains, and owning groups.
rules
A logical formula used to evaluate and verify whether the data in an imported record meets the requirements and data standards specified for the target
database system.
rulesets
A combined set of rules that can be run as a set during job processing.
schema
A model used to describe and validate the structure of XML files.
scope
A selected configuration in which users can view, create, modify, and terminate data.
scoped by
The relationship between property definitions and property types in the schema. The scoped by relationship specifies the property type that defines acceptable
values, or scopes, a particular property definition. Every property definition in the schema is scoped by one and only one property type. All properties of that
property definition must be of that property type.
section
A collection of display items used on forms in the software.
security codes
Configuration-independent items that are used to set different security levels, which can be applied to objects in a configuration. They can be used to control
the confidentiality or restrict the availability of the objects.
security rules
Security measures that limit the types of data that users have access to in the system. A user’s access group is used to determine which security rules are
relevant.
separator
Separates file parts and tag parts. Common separators include a dash (-), slash (/), tilde (~), and so forth. For example, the instrument tag LCV-157 uses the
dash (-) separator to separate the tag name (LCV) from the drawing name (157).
server
A computer that stores or processes files.
sign off
To approve a particular revision of a document in the software. Signing off a document sets the document to be the current released revision, makes it official,
and supersedes any previous released revisions. Document revisions that have been signed off are frozen and cannot be checked out.
site
The server settings that point to administration and data databases. Each site allows you to run the software operations on data associated with plants and
projects configured in the database.
SSL
Secure Sockets Layer (SSL) provides a secure, encrypted connection between a web server and a browser application, such as a website. This allows you to
transmit encrypted private data online.
staging
The staging or staging area or staging database used is the first part of a combined set of processes, where imported data is held and validated before being
exported to a site.
status
The state of a change object at the completion of each step in a workflow.
step
A process that must be performed in order to complete a workflow.
submittal
A control file used to send documents and files to the owning company's system. The submittal contains all the details of the submitted objects, including tags
and any document file attachments.
sub-review
A technical query to an internal resource to provide more information to complete a document review, referred to as Internal Technical Query in the user
interface.
subscribe
To register interest in an object so that you receive a notification when the object is modified. You can manually subscribe to change notifications in the
software.
superseded
Indicates that a newer, working version of the selected document exists.
tag
A database object that is the physical part as designed.
tag discovery pattern
Used to extract the master and alias tags from the content of a master file.
tag document register (TDR)
A tag document register (TDR) is a submittal type initiated by the contractor to send tag and document relationship data to the owner company.
tag naming system
Defines the parts of a tag name and relates them to existing objects in the database.
target system
The system or database used as the destination for exported data that has been validated before export. It is also used as the basis for creating import
mappings, implicit delete, and validation rules.
technical query
A technical query is a question raised on a document or object that is progressed through a workflow.
terminate
The action of changing an objects status to terminate makes the object and its history inaccessible except for documents. For documents, terminating a
document version or revision hides it from general searches unless you select Show Removed Items icon.
text reader
Captures simple text files and processes the data directly without needing an application to extract the data.
thumbnail
A small size image representation of a larger file intended to make it easier and faster to manage it.
title block
The portion of a drawing that contains information about the drawing, such as who created the drawing, when it was created, who approved it, and so on. The
type of information included in the title block varies by drawing type, industry, and organization.
To Do List
A graphical list of tasks that require attention from the user.
In the software, the To Do List contains workflow steps assigned to the user.
In the authoring tools, the To Do List contains create, delete, and update tasks generated when a user retrieves a document, such as a P&ID.
transmittal
A transmittal is used to distribute a controlled set of documents in the system to a specific set of recipients. The transmittal records and tracks the distribution
of the documents, the recipients, and the reason for their issue.
transpose
Returns a vertical range of cells as a horizontal range, or vice versa.
UID
Unique Identifier is used to uniquely identify an object within a system. UID definitions must be set on all objects that are exported from the staging area if the
unique key or query definition is left blank. The staging area UID definitions must match the UID definitions in the target system.
unique key
A set of values guaranteed to be unique for each object in a relation, and can be used to identify objects in the target system.
unit
Group of parts of the schematic and individual worlds of a plant that together perform a given process function. The identifying number of the unit is unique
within the project and within the plant. Most companies, but not all, use the concept of unit.
UoM
A unit of measurement.
URL
A uniform resource locator is a web address that contains a specific character string that references a resource.
user
An object that specifies data about a person who is authorized to use the software.
user profile
Information about configurations, preferences, and interface settings saved by the software when you close the application and used to restore your
environment when it is reopened.
validation process
A process that evaluates the imported data against a defined set of rules to ensure the validity of the data.
validation rule
A logical formula used to evaluate the imported data in one or more fields to determine whether it matches the existing criteria and hierarchy found in the target
system database. There are two types of validation rules:
1. Rules that determine if the data is valid for the target system schema (these rules can be autogenerated.
2. Rules that determine if the data meets specific business criteria, such as naming format.
vault
A folder where files are stored on a host computer.
vendor
A vendor is a company or manufacturer that is supplying a product or service for sale.
version
An intermediate update to an existing document that is tracked by the software.
View and Markup
The functionality that provides precision view and markup capabilities for documents and is used in the document review process.
virtual directory
A Web folder created in IIS that points to a physical folder on the Web server. Virtual directories are used by the software to run applications and services from
the application server and to transfer files between file servers and clients.
visualization data service (VDS)
Provides graphics and associated information services to the Graphic View Control (GVC) when viewing Hexagon 3D models or 2D drawings in a SDx client
application.
wildcard
A character that helps you narrow your search for objects in the database. You can use wildcards in any text box in the Find and Query dialog boxes.
Text wildcards include the following:
? - Finds any single character
* - Finds any string of characters
% - Performs the same function as *
work breakdown structure
A structure for grouping documents, tags, and other information for handing over deliverables to the client.
work package
A detailed scope of work to be executed and completed in a scheduled amount of time.
work process diagram
A key part of a work process diagram is the use of color-coded swimlanes. A swimlane helps illustrate a process that spans across different applications and is
represented as a row containing activities specific to the application or role involved.
workflow
A series of steps defining actions to be taken on an object in the software.
working document
A document that has not been signed off in the software.
Copyright

that the handover costs on a conventional

29

Confidential

What is CFIHOS?

CFIHOS? (pronounced “See-Fos”)

What is CFIHOS?

Why are we adopting it?

• An emerging standard for Capital Facilities

• We can deliver a working OOTB solution

Industry Handover Specification

• Joint project sponsored by USPI
(Netherlands) and ENAA (Japan)

• Avoid redesigning tag classifications and
document types every project, reducing
time, cost and risk

• Based on Shell DEP, which has been used

in 60+ capital projects

• Demonstrable today.  Shell’s EIS
configuration in EDW is CFIHOS.

• A pragmatic extension of existing Industry
Standards (ISO 15926, ISO 14224, API,
ISA, POSC Caesar, etc.)

• OO’s are asking for it

• EPC clients are adopting it

31

Confidential

What is CFIHOS?

32

Confidential

• Generic Scope of Work
• Generic Technical Specification
• Common Database Definition – Reference Data Library
(RDL), Master Objects, Property, and Picklist definitions

• Tag Class, Tag Types and Properties per Tag type
• Equipment Class, Equipment Types and Properties per

Equipment Type

• Document Types and properties
• Required Documents per Tag Class
• Model, Manufacturer
• Discipline, Discipline to Document Type
• Data Validation Rules

What is CFIHOS?

Yellow: Operations
Blue: Engineering (Project)

Generate Loop and Segment Drawings – Detailed Design – Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Generate Loop and Segment Drawings - Detailed Design - Level 2

1.  As an instrument engineer, I need to use the instrument index, the detailed wiring, and the I/O list and I/O assignment to generate loop drawings so that construction and maintenance can use them for installation and field testing.
2.  As an instrument engineer, I need to use the instrument index, the detailed wiring, and the I/O list and I/O assignment to generate segment wiring diagrams so that construction and maintenance can use them for installation and for field testing.

Enterprise Work Process for Instrumentation

1




# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\MatSummaries.pdf
---

Manage Requisitions - Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Enterprise Work Process for Materials Management

1

Manage Requisitions - Level 2

1.  As a project planner, I need to define and set up the requisition and agreement plan so that the creation and issue of the requisitions happens in accordance with the overall project objectives and that all critical events are automatically tracked by

SmartPlant Materials so that constant control is achieved.

2.  As a materials discipline lead, I need to set up the rules for requisitions, including numbering rules, grouping strategy, split criteria, and disposition (contingency) rules.
3.  As a materials discipline lead, I need to define the MTO execution strategy so that the BOM line items are properly grouped to ensure an efficient procurement process. The grouping can be based on the entire BOM structure or any subset thereof.
4.  As a project control manager, I need to define the account structure and budget for materials.
5.  As a project planner, I need to define the plan and required on-site (ROS/RAS) dates for materials packages.
6.  As a materials engineer, I need to run the MTO process from BOM set defined as per the rules set by the MTO execution strategy.
7.  Option A <automated process> SmartPlant Materials will create or revise the requisition.
8.  Option B As the requisition owner, I might create or revise the material requisition manually.
9.  As a SmartPlant Foundation user, I need to manage the engineering and supporting documents for the project so that technical information will be available downstream.
10.  As a document controller, I need to manage engineering and supporting documents for the project so that technical information will be available downstream.
11.  As the requisition owner, I need to attach the supporting documents to the requisition.
12.  As the requisition owner, I need to maintain the requisition by defining VDRs, material group, ROS/RAS dates, budget, account codes, and so forth.
13.  As a materials discipline lead, I need to validate the requisition and submit it for approval by the project team.
14.  As a project team, we need to approve and release the requisition to procurement.

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Enterprise Work Process for Materials Management

2

About Extensibility

 This guide includes documentation for the latest update release. For information about the latest features available in each update, refer to the latest release notes.

Not finding what you're looking for? We can help.

Learn about searching tips About Hexagon Documentation Help. Or, the functionality might have changed or been removed. For more information, see HxGN SDx modified and retired functionality.

How do I find out which version of software I'm using?

1. Click Settings

.

2. Click the name under About to display the software version on the About page.

Extensibility has been introduced by Hexagon to help improve scalability and reduce software customizations. The following document provides information about using Extensibility so customers can extend and grow their server system.

The Extensibility mechanism is compatible with SDx installed on-premise and SDx deployed in Smart Cloud.

 The examples shown can be modified for use with SDx installed on-premise or deployed in Smart Cloud. Some settings and objects are shared by the applications to reduce the administration overhead. Some configuration by

Smart Cloud Support Team and implementation team is also needed to get Extensibility to work in the Cloud.

Copyright 2019-2025, Hexagon AB <https://hexagon.com/company/divisions/asset-lifecycle-intelligence> and/or its subsidiaries and affiliates

Published Thursday, June 12, 2025 at 10:07 AM

Update 68

Hexagon Policy Against Software Piracy

Customer Support

Extensibility

Why use Extensibility instead of customization?

Extensibility is better to use than customization because:

The customization mechanism requires custom code to run in-process

Customization means additional complexity to update and maintain

Multi-tenancy demands a uniform code base for all customers

Different tenants may not want customized code in-process

Tenant’s data or third-party code could have security access and resource issues

Why has Extensibility been introduced?

Extensibility has been introduced to reduce software customizations that can impact maintenance, upgrades, and hotfix cycles. Extensibility also:

Allows third-party applications to communicate and react to system events

Integrates with existing and external project systems. For example:

Project reporting systems

Custom viewer applications, both for data and documentation

In-house web applications

Bridges functionality not currently supported in SDx

Provides flexibility for work processes to use client or server extensibility

Provides a modern development experience

Leverages the myriad of modern web technologies

Allows federated searching across multiple hosted sites and third-party systems

Extensibility is a simple mechanism for administrators that allows methods to have custom views that display applications in an iframe in the SDx Web Client and allow communication with external applications.

The Extensibility mechanism provides the freedom for you to implement extensibility in your SDx Web Client, SDx server, or Smart Cloud system.

Extensibility provides:

The capability to integrate external web applications into the SDx Web Client experience

The capability for a third-party server to communicate and react to system events

The ability for a client application to perform actions with a back-end service by impersonating the identity of a user

An emphasis on the user and developer experience

An introduction to Web Development Best Practice

A clean, modern API

Hexagon recommends that custom Extensibility code should always be hosted on a separate server in a different domain. This can mitigate any browser security concerns.

For more information for SDx Extensibility, see Web Client Extensibility.

For more information for Server Extensibility, see Server Extensibility.

For more information on SmartCloud Extensibility, see Smart Cloud Extensibility Built On-Premise.

Extensibility samples, with a full set of services for interacting with the Web Extensibility API and other Web APIs, are available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find
sample data on Smart Community.

Web Client Extensibility

Using Extensibility, the SDx Web Client can communicate with external applications through an external URL and display the data in the user interface. Extensibility can also be used to provide a custom user interface to access external

data using the Hexagon server APIs:

SDx Web Client Extensibility is configured by creating methods from the WebClientExtensibility and WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client APIs.

Methods using the WebClientExtensibility client API are restricted to a single object by default.

Methods using the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API allow execution on multiple objects by default.

 Hexagon recommends that Extensibility code should always be hosted on a separate server in a different domain. This can mitigate any browser security concerns.

Technologies

 You must have knowledge of the following software technologies to use this Extensibility guide and examples provided.

The following technologies are used to implement Extensibility in SDx.

Required

TypeScript — The programming language used to develop the user interface. It is a typed superset of JavaScript that compiles to JavaScript. For more information, see https://www.typescriptlang.org/ <https://www.typescriptlang.org> .

Angular — The framework used to build SDx. For more information, see https://angular.io/ <https://angular.io> .

Angular CLI — The command-line interface for generating, running, compiling, and building Angular applications. For more information, see https://cli.angular.io/ <https://cli.angular.io/> .

Angular material — An open-source library that provides rich UI Components for Angular developed by the Angular team. Future iterations of the Web Client will be using components from this library. For more information, see
https://material.angular.io/ <https://material.angular.io/> .

RxJS — A library used by Angular to make asynchronous programming easier. For more information, see https://rxjs-dev.firebaseapp.com/ <https://rxjs-dev.firebaseapp.com/> .

PrimeNG — An open-source library that provides rich UI components for Angular. For more information, see https://www.primefaces.org/primeng/#/ <https://www.primefaces.org/primeng/#/> .

Visual Studio Code — A lightweight IDE ideal for developing Angular applications. For more information, see https://code.visualstudio.com/ <https://code.visualstudio.com/> .

npm — A package manager for adding libraries to a project. For more information, see https://www.npmjs.com/ <https://www.npmjs.com/> .

Node.js — A javascript runtime environment. For more information, see https://nodejs.org/en/ <https://nodejs.org/en/download/prebuilt-installer> .

Optional

Jasmine — A unit testing framework. For more information, see https://jasmine.github.io/ <https://jasmine.github.io/> .

Karma — A test runner for running unit tests. For more information, see https://karma-runner.github.io/2.0/index.html <https://karma-runner.github.io/2.0/index.html> .

WebDriverIo — An automation framework for writing end-to-end tests. For more information, see https://webdriver.io/ <https://webdriver.io/> .

Getting started

This section describes how to set up your development environment so that you can run the Extensibility samples available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data
on Smart Community.

Prerequisites

Before using this guide, you should be familiar with Angular and its concepts. For tutorials and explanations, see https://angular.io/ <https://angular.io> .

Install HxGN SDx

Install HxGN SDx and ensure that the Web Client user interface is installed and working correctly.

Install Node.js

 Always use the highest version of Node.js specified in the package.json file of the Extensibility samples.

1. Download the version of Node.js from https://nodejs.org/en/download/prebuilt-installer <https://nodejs.org/en/download/prebuilt-installer> .

2. Run the installer.

3. Open a command prompt, and run the command node -v to ensure that Node.js was installed correctly.

Install npm

 npm should be installed with Node.js. However, use the following steps to ensure you have the latest version of npm and that it was installed correctly.

1. Open a command prompt, and run the command npm -v.

2. Run the following command to ensure that you have the latest version of npm: npm install -g npm.

Install IDE

Install any IDE that supports TypeScript. We recommend using Visual Studio Code.

 For more information about installing TypeScript for different IDEs, see https://www.typescriptlang.org/ <https://www.typescriptlang.org> .

Architecture

What can I do with Extensibility?

You can connect to third-party system applications to display, create, or update data

You can add a method action to the Actions menu and open it to display data or run a process

You can use custom UI components to display data and show the loading indicator

You can retrieve contextual information on selected objects from remote servers

You can query the OData Services

You can handle token information for communicating with the OData web services

You can take snapshots of 2D drawings, 3D models, or laser scans using the View and Markup command.

Extensibility revolves around the use of SDx methods that are implemented using either the WebClientExtensibility client API or the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API.

When configured, SDx and an external application can then communicate securely using a wrapper library around the HTML5 postMessage API.

Each method must specify a SDx URL to the external application. The URL is used in an iframe hosted within the SDx, after undergoing sanitization and security checks. For more information, see https://angular.io/ <https://angular.io> ).

Basic SDx setup

In a basic SDx single application extensibility setup, the WebClientExtensibility client API is used to display data in an iframe in the SDx Web Client user interface from an external application through a URL connection:

1. The user configures a method to launch an external application using Extensibility.

2. The SDx Web Client creates an iframe and uses a URL to point to the external application as the source.

3. The external application loads and communicates with the SDx Web Client using a window.postMessage API.

Extensibility communication events

The SDx Web Client uses a third-party Paypal library around the HTML post.message method to send secure event communications. Using the Paypal post-robot (version 8) library overcomes the many limitations of using HTML
post.message.

The extensibility application in an iframe can send and receive the following communication events:

Init - This event includes the following contextual information:

The SDx URL, which includes the full domain and pathname.

The method and information about all of its parameters.

Object OBIDs of any selected contextural objects.

The configurations the user has currently set.

The user's preferences.

loading - This event lets you turn the loading indicator on or off.

actionMenu - This event lets you open or close the Actions menu. It can include data as a single object or as multiple objects.

Token information ("getToken) - This event allows the extensibility application to retrieve the access token so it can make http requests.

 For more information on usage, see the samples that are available on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.

Recommended setup

There are multiple possibilities when implementing a custom method for Extensibility.

 Hexagon recommends that Extensibility code should always be hosted on a separate server in a different domain. This can mitigate any browser security concerns.

Single application with multiple routes

The delivered example application is a single-host web client application in SDx that is split using routes. Each route represents the view to show for each method. We recommend that you follow this example application architecture.

The external application does not need to be on the same domain as the SDx Web Client. For more information, see Limitations and restrictions.

The advantage of this approach is that the external application can share code, making maintenance easier and increasing productivity.

Hybrid setup

It is possible that some methods can be used to display views from embedded third-party sites. In this setup, you can use the single application with a multiple routes approach to handle some methods, and select a completely different
application to handle any other methods.

 Assuming there are no restrictions on directly embedding the application in an iframe, the extensibility mechanism allows for this hybrid approach. For more information, see Limitations and restrictions.

Connecting to third-party data sources

From the external application, you can communicate with other applications in a number of ways. This communication is implemented by the external application. A common scenario is connecting to RESTful services to read from or write

to a custom data source.

For example, the external application can request contextual information from the SDx Web Client, such as the selected object's OBID. Using this contextual information, additional data can be retrieved from the web services for that
object. The external application can then query the third-party services and display the results in any way required.

A common way to authenticate with third-party systems is by using OAuth, which is an open standard for token-based authentication and authorization on the web. If you follow the recommended architecture, the implementation should
use an authorization code with PKCE to obtain a token from the external application. OAuth normally uses a series of redirects to authenticate the user. However, as the external application is embedded in an iframe, it must use a pop-up.

When you need to connect to a third-party OAuth site, we recommend you use the OIDC-approved oidc-client.js library (https://github.com/IdentityModel/oidc-client-js <https://github.com/IdentityModel/oidc-client-js> ). This library
supports pop-up authentication and has been used in several demonstration applications.

The external application must handle authentication and security when connecting to third-party data sources.

Another common scenario is where there is a need for some interception of a request through a proxy server. This interception could be the authentication request to a third-party service account or providing custom RESTFul
services to read a local database.

OAuth setup

In some OAuth setups, the external application communicates with a third-party system, which may require authentication through OAuth. The external application is solely responsible for this authentication process as SDx plays no role.

The external application uses an OIDC approved library to obtain the authentication token, which handles much of the process, and must be configured to connect to the third-party application.

The external application credentials used to acquire the token must be obtained from the third-party prior to writing code.

The external application must use a pop-up mode for authentication due to the restriction of it being hosted in an iframe.

When the external application has been authenticated and receives an authentication token, the token is used on all subsequent calls to the RESTful APIs.

This setup scenario applies when each user needs to use their own credentials to access a third-party API.

Where the third-party is setup to use an active directory for authentication, the same setup is required, but there is no login screen.

 You may need to be enable any third-party cookies for the third-party site.

Proxy service setup

In a proxy service setup, the external application communicates with a third-party system through a proxy service. The external application is solely responsible for this communication process as SDx plays no role.

The external application communicates to the third-party system through a proxy service.

 We recommend you use the Web API. For more information, see https://www.asp.net/web-api <https://www.asp.net/web-api> .

The proxy service will be a set of RESTful APIs that have been written to complement the external application.

The scenario caters for use cases where:

There is a service account to access third-party APIs. Instead of each user logging in, a service account is used to access the data store.

Custom logic has been added to access the database.

Configuring methods for Extensibility applications

All applications that use Extensibility in SDx use methods that are configured using the WebClientExtensibility Client API or the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API. This depends on whether you want to restrict the method to
single or multiple objects.

You can choose how you want your method to display the Extensibility application in the SDx as follows:

Display extensibility application as a feature set

You can display an Extensibility application as a feature set on the Home menu. This is useful for scenarios where there is no starting object, such as a Power BI report.

1. Create a method.

2. Select the WebClientExtensibility client API or select the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API for multiple objects.

3. Enter the URL of the application that you want to embed in the SDx.

4. Configure the Access Groups for the method.

5. Relate the new method to the feature set in which you want to display the Extensibility application.

Display extensibility application in the Actions menu

You can display an Extensibility application from the Actions menu. This is useful for scenarios when there is a starting object.

1. Create a method.

2. Select the WebClientExtensibility client API to restrict to a single object or select the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client API for multiple objects.

3. Enter the URL of the application that you want to embed in the SDx.

4. Configure the Access Groups for the new method.

5. Relate the new method to the required object interface definition so that the method appears in the Actions menu.

Using the Extensibility example application

Several Extensibility example applications are included in the SDx Extensibility folder available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.

The samples include a pure javascript/css/html example, which can be used for basic scenarios. They also include some sophisticated Angular examples for scenarios that require a richer application experience. There is no known limit

regarding which front end technology you wish to work with to implement an Extensibility application.

 Due to the varying schema, some examples may require further configuration changes to accommodate any differences. The Angular masterclass example is an extension of the basic Angular sample and is only there for

reference.

Extensibility Example

The next steps describe how you can create the API entry point, create the required model, and set up the local development server to run the example Extensibility application for the recommended architecture using Angular:

1. Ensure that the database is installed with the latest model and that both the WebClientExtensibility and the WebClientExtensibilityMultiple client APIs are in the database.

2. Ensure that the SDx Web Client has an API entry point that is configured for areas.

3. Create a feature set. For example, named External Application.

4. Create a method.

The following example shows how you can create a new method to configure SDx Extensibility to display directly from a feature set. This example uses the following values that you can provide for the properties of the new method:

Name - AreasFromFeatureSet

DisplayAs - Areas from Feature Set

Client API - WebClientExtensibility

URL to custom page - http://localhost:4200/#/feature-set-home-example.

 The local server (http://localhost:4200) is for use in a development environment only. Alternatively, you can test changes by using the production method, which is particularly useful when testing a https site as it does not

require installing a local certificate and can be hosted as a secure site in IIS.

a. Configure the user access for the method.

b. Relate the AreasFromFeatureSet method to the External Application feature set.

5. Create a second method.

The second new method example shows how to configure an Extensibility method for a single object. This example uses the following values that you can provide for the properties for the second new method:

Name - AreasFromActionMenu

DisplayAs - Area from Action Menu

Client API - WebClientExtensibility

URL to custom page - http://localhost:4200/#/area-example.

a. Configure user access for the method.

b. Relate the AreasFromActionMenu method to the interface definition for an area.

6. Create a third method.

The third new method example shows how to configure an Extensibility method for a multiple objects. This example uses the following values that you can provide for the properties for the third new method:

Name - AreasFromActionMenuMultiple

DisplayAs - Multiple Areas from Action Menu

Client API - WebClientExtensibilityMultiple

URL to custom page - http://localhost:4200/#/multiple-objects.

a. Configure user access for the new method.

b. Relate the Multiple Areas from Action Menu method to the interface definition for an area.

7. Copy the ClientExtension directory to a location you can access. For this example, C:\test.

8. Open a command prompt, navigate to the directory C:\test\ClientExtensibility, and run the command npm install.

 This command installs all dependencies for the project. Refer to the npm website for any troubleshooting information (https://docs.npmjs.com <https://docs.npmjs.com> ).

9. In an editor, open the C:\test\ClientExtensibility\src\settings.json file, and change the client and server URLs to match your configuration.

The clientUrl value must include the domain of the SDx to which the extensibility application communicates. For example, https://sdx.com/site1.

The serverUrl value must include the domain and site of the server that is hosting the SDx to which the extensibility application communicates. It must be appended with /api. For example, https://sdx.com/serversite1/api.

10. Open a command prompt, navigate to the directory C:\test\ClientExtensibility, and run the command npm start.

 This command compiles the sample and runs a local development server on localhost port 4200.

11. Open the SDx from your browser.

 If configured correctly, you should now see the External Application feature set with the Areas from the Feature Set function tile and be able to:

a. Select the new function tile to launch the external application embedded in a custom view in the SDx.

b. Open the Actions menu, and select the Area from Action Menu method to see the area.

c. Go back to the Areas from the Feature Set function, and to view and select several objects as required.

d. Open the Actions menu, and select the Multiple Areas from Action Menu method to see multiple areas as required.

You may need to configure the OData web services to handle any CORS requests.

You may need to close the command prompt after installing the prerequisite software to ensure that the environment variables are updated.

You can view the browser console for any errors, such as the page has not loaded.

When you encounter an error on opening an Extensibility application, ensure that the settings.json file is set to the correct SDx domain in which the Extensibility application is hosted.

Building for production

You can use the following steps to build the application and deploy it to IIS:

1. Open a command prompt, and navigate to the directory where you placed [InstallPath]\ClientExtension.

2. Run the command npm run build.

This command creates a new \dist folder, which contains the code that has been optimized for deployment. These files can be hosted on IIS and consumed using SDx.

Additional changes may be needed for some settings for the production build. For example, you might need to use https instead of http if configured for SSL or enable CORS.

View the browser console for any errors.

Limitations and restrictions

The following section contains a list of known limitations and restrictions for an external application being hosted in SDx.

iframe

The external application is hosted in an iframe, and subject to limitations of an iframe. For example, you cannot embed a non-secure (http) site into a secure (https) site.

 Directly embedding third-party web applications into SDx might not be allowed as it depends on whether the application being embedded in iframe is supported.

CORS

Depending on the infrastructure configuration, you might need to configure CORS on the OData web services, other web services, or both.

Deep linking

SDx cannot deep link into an external application. It can only load the URL specified in the method arguments.

Actions menu

Directly linking from an external application using a hyperlink is not officially supported. All interaction between SDx and the external application is done using a method configured in the Actions menu.

 Implementing a hyperlink to the info map from the external application is not supported.

Other frameworks

While you can use the extensibility application mechanism in other JavaScript frameworks, this is not officially supported. The delivered example has all the infrastructure in place to support extensibility.

External application versions

The external application can be written using any version of Angular or third-party libraries and does not need to be the same version as used in SDx.

Embedded mode

Embedded mode is an event-driven communication mechanism that allows the SDx Web Client to send and receive certain events when the SDx Web Client is embedded in another parent application.

An example of a parent application that can be used for embedding the SDx Web Client is available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.

Technologies

In order to embed SDx in another application, you must have detailed knowledge of the technologies used, which include Cascading Style Sheets (CSS), Hypertext Markup Language (HTML), and JavaScript.

Getting started

This section describes how to set up your development environment so that you can run the embedded samples available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data
on Smart Community.

Prerequisites

Before embedding SDx, you should be familiar with administering SDx and using an Internet Information Services (IIS) web server. For more information, such as documentation and tutorials, see https://www.iis.net/ <https://www.iis.net/> .

 In order to run the parent application, the sample application also needs to be hosted on Internet Information Services (IIS) web server.

Architecture

When you use Embedded mode, the SDx Web Client is embedded in an iframe of a parent application. The Web Client and parent application can securely communicate using a wrapper library that is based on the HTML5 postMessage

API.

Communication events

The communication mechanism allows you to send and receive certain events between the SDx Web Client and the parent application. The following list shows examples of communication events that can be exchanged:

The Web Client informs the parent application when it is ready to receive and send events.

The parent application listens for changes to the selected objects in context objects. For example, objects can be selected in the grid, on the Info Map, or as hotspots on 2D and 3D drawings.

The parent application receives events from the workflow path that the user is currently working in the SDx Web Client.

The parent application can request query context information. For example, it can request information on the users create and query configurations and selected roles.

The parent application can perform GET or POST queries.

The SDx Web Client informs the parent application when it is about to log off from the application

The parent application can take snapshots of 2D drawings, 3D models, or laser scans. The snapshots are returned as base64 encoded .png files.

Security

Hexagon ALI recommends you secure the SDx Web Client with the correct HTTP response headers. You can specify the parent application domains allowed to embed the SDx Web Client and secure the iframe usage by using X-
Frame-options and Content Security Policy (CSP).

You must add the parent application domain to the server domain allow list.

Any queries performed by the parent application are restricted by the role of the user logged into the SDx Web Client.

Using the embedded mode example parent application

The example parent application can be used to embed the SDx Web Client and is located in the Embedded mode samples folder available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see

Find sample data on Smart Community.

For the purposes of this example, the SDx URL is https://wc.domain.com/sdx/. To configure the example parent application:

1. Log on to the SDx Web Client.

2. Open the main.js file in an editor, and edit the clientDomain variable to https://wc.domain.com.

3. Open the index.html file with an editor, and edit the iframe src value to https://wc.domain.com/sdx/.

4. Open IIS, and set up a new application that points to the embedded samples folder. For example, https://parent.domain2.com/sampleapp/.

5. Open the application server Web.config file, and under the appsettings node add the following:

<add key="FrameOptionsWhiteList" value="https://parent.domain2.com/">

 This step is only required if HxGN SDx is installed on-premises.

6. Run the embedded SDx Web Client by navigating to https://parent.domain2.com/sampleapp/.

If your browser tries to block the authentication pop-up, add the web address to the allowed list and refresh.

If you get any errors, you can view the error console for any details.

If the Web.config file is changed, you must restart the Web Client server for the changes to take effect.

Set up your environment

The samples provided require very specific OBIDs and configs for the embedded Web Client to work. To set up your environment to work with the embedded Web Client:

1. Navigate to some of the objects in the Web Client and copy the object's URLs.

2. In IIS, locate the index.html file and replace the http://localhost URLs with the copied URLs.

Server Extensibility

 To use server extensibility, you must have an Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP) 1.0 compatible broker service installed and configured for your system.

Server extensibility provides a mechanism for a third-party server to communicate and react to SDx system events and perform actions, such as finding data, creating objects, overriding default application server behavior, auditing, and so
on.

A user interacts with the client application which makes a request to the application server.

The application server requests trigger events that are sent to the Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP) message broker.

The AMQP message broker sends the events to an external event subscriber, which receives the events and triggers actions based on the event type.

To use server extensibility, the server extensibility events are sent to the AMQP 1.0 compatible broker service. You can subscribe to these events using an AMQP 1.0 client library.

For more information on the broker services listed by Awesome AMQP 1.0 resources, see https://github.com/xinchen10/awesome-amqp/blob/master/README.md#brokers <https://github.com/xinchen10/awesome-
amqp/blob/master/README.md#brokers> .

Server extensibility samples are available from Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.

HTTP responses from OData requests return the header X-Correlation-ID. This value can be compared with the Correlation ID of the AMQP event so you can work out the origin of the event. By doing this, you can select which

events you can ignore.

Server configuration

Server Manager has several Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP) settings specifically for the configuration of server extensibility for a third-party system.

AMQPBroker-HostName - The host name of the server on which the AMQP broker is installed.

AMQPBroker-UserName - The user name to connect to the AMQP broker to send the events.

AMQPBroker-Password - The password used to connect to the AMQP broker to send the events.

AMQPBroker-Protocol - The protocol used by the AMQP broker; the AMQP protocol or the AMQPS prototcol for an SSL enabled site. The default is AMQP.

 If you use AMPQS protocol, you must ensure that your AMQP broker is configured with the correct SSL settings.

AMQPBroker-Port - The port used to connect to the AMQP broker. The default is 5672 for AMQP protocol or 5671 for AMQPS protocol.

 If you use an Azure Service Bus, you can use port 443, which creates the AMQP connection using WebSocket protocol.

AMQPBroker-ExtensibilityTopic - The extensibility topic to which the AMQP broker sends the extensibility events. The default is ext.

Server events

There are currently five types of server events:

Object events

Custom workflow process step events

Relationship object events

Change action events

File Operations

Object events

Event

OnCreated

OnCopied

OnDeleted

Created

Copied

Deleted

OnTerminated

Terminated

OnUpdated

Updated

Custom workflow process step events

Triggered when an object is . . .

Event

Triggered by the workflow . . .

Execute

When executing a custom workflow process step

Relationship object events

Event

Triggered when a Relationship is . . .

OnRelCreated

Created

OnRelCopied

Copied

OnRelDeleted

Deleted

OnRelTerminated

Terminated

OnRelUpdated

Updated

Change action events

Event

Triggered when . . .

OnChangeActionAllocated

A Change Action is related to a tool signature

OnChangeActionUpdated

A status related to the change action changed

OnChangeActionScopeChange

The scope of the objects of the change action was extended or reduced

File operations events

Event

FileOperations

Undergoes one of the supported file operation sub-events:

Triggered when a document . .

Checkout

Checkin

Revise

Replace

SignOff

UndoCheckout

UndoSignOff

Server object event payload

The server object event message contains the following payload properties:

Property

OBID

UID

DomainUID

ClassDefUID

InterfaceUIDs

ConfigUID

User

The following example shows a typical JSON payload:

{

"obid": "A543BC",

"uid": "Folder001",

"domainUID": "ADMIN",

"classdefUID": "SPFFolder"

"interfaceUIDs": ["ISPFFolder","IObject"],

"configUID": "PL_PlantA",

"user": "USR_adminuser"

}

Description

The OBID value of the triggering object (string)

The UID value of the triggering object (string)

The domain UID value of the triggering object (string)

The Class Definition UID of this object

The Interface Definition UIDs which are realized by this object

The UID of the configuration that triggered the event

The UID of user who triggered the event

Server update object event payload

The server object event message contains the following payload properties:

Property

UpdatedProperties

OBID

DomainUID

ClassDefUID

InterfaceUIDs

ConfigUID

User

The following example shows a typical JSON payload:

{

"UpdatedProperties":{"IObject":["LastUpdatedDate","Description"]},

"obid": "A543BC",

"uid": "Folder001",

"domainUID": "ADMIN",

"classdefUID": "SPFFolder"

"interfaceUIDs": ["ISPFFolder","IObject"],

"configUID": "PL_PlantA",

"user": "USR_adminuser"

}

Configuring a custom workflow process step

You can set up custom workflow process steps in the same way as you can create any workflow steps in a workflow. When these custom workflow process step events are received, you can react and perform the tasks as required.

If the custom workflow process step name does not match an existing workflow step in the server, then an extensibility event is sent with the name of the custom workflow process step in the Types value in a server event payload.

Each custom workflow process step must invoke a sign off step, as the workflow will not continue until the custom workflow process step has been signed off. If a sign off step is not invoked, the custom workflow process step remains

in Ready To Start status.

Custom process step event payload

The server custom process step event message contains the following object payload properties:

Property

OBID

UID

Description

The OBID value of the triggering object (string)

The UID value of the triggering object (string)

The DomainUID value of the triggering object (string)

Current process step class name of the triggering workflow ste

The OBID of the object the workflow is attached to

The UID of the configuration that triggered the event

The UID of user who triggered the event

DomainUID

WorkflowStepClassName

WorkflowObjectObid

ConfigUID

User

The following example shows a typical object event JSON payload:

{

"obid": "A543BC",

"uid": "Folder001",

"domainUID": "ADMIN",

"WorkflowStepClassName": "ExampleProcessStepName"

"WorkflowObjectOBID": "A0BB2",

"configUID": "PL_PlantA",

"user": "USR_adminuser"

}

Change action event payload

A change action event message has the event type OnChangeActionAllocated or OnChangeActionUpdated and contains the following payload properties:

Property

Description

OriginSignature

ToolSignature

ConfigUID

ToolUID

ObjectName

ObjectUID

Operation

Subscription

Status

CreationDate

If SDx Web Client made the request, "SDx". Otherwise, the signature of the tool that made the request, e.g., "AAAE".

Signature of tool related to the Change Action to which this message is related.

Configuration for this event.

UID of tool with the tool signature.

Name of Change Action to which this message is related.

UID of Change Action to which this message is related.

Information about the event that caused this message. For event OnChangeActionAllocated, "Allocation". For event
OnChangeActionUpdated, values include "SDAChangeActionStatus" or "SDxiHubToolStatus".

Whether the tool for the tool signature and tool UID is subscribing to the Change Action to which this message is related.

Status of Change Action to which this message is related.

Creation date of Change Action to which this message is related.

Property

ToolUID
