File operations event payload

The file operation event message contains the following payload properties:

Property

Description

EventType

OBID

The type of file operation event invoked on the document

The OBID value of the document that invokes the file operation

The following example shows a typical JSON payload for a file operation sub-event:

{

 "EventType": "SIGNOFF",

 "OBID": "6FL6000A",

}

 Other file operation sub-events supported are UNDOSIGNOFF, CHECKOUT, UNDOCHECKOUT, CHECKIN, REVISE, or REPLACE.

Subscribing to server events

You need to set up an active application or service as an event receiver so that you can receive the server events. Samples on how to set these up can be found on Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> . For

more information, see Find sample data on Smart Community.

In order to interact with the application and react to the sent events, you need to obtain an OAuth token from the authorization server used by your server site. After obtaining the token you can make OData requests using CRUD

operations to perform the desired action.

AMQP message annotations

AMQP message annotations can provide further information about the server event and allows for event filtering without having to deserialize the payload, as follows:

Reply-To: The URL of the server that sent the event message.

Message-ID: The unique identifier (UID) of the event message.

Content-Type: The content type of the payload.

Subject: The information about the event which allows the subscriber to evaluate whether the event is of interest or not:

Component

Subject component 1

Subject component 2

Subject component 3

Description

The product. For example, SDx

The server name. For example, ABCServer

The event type. For example, OnCreated

Subject component 4 and so on

A value that depends on the value type. For example, PL_PlantA/SPFFolder/IObject,ISPFFolder

Each component of the subject value is separated by a forward slash /.

The first three components Product/ServerName/EventType are always the same regardless of the event type.

Object Event Subject Definition:

The Object Event Subject Definition is made up of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ConfigUID/ClassDefUID/InterfaceDefUIDs

For example, this results on the following Subject Definition:

Product/ServerName/OnCreated/PL_PlantA/SPFFolder/IObject,ISPFFolder

Object Update Event Subject Definition:

The Object Event Subject Definition is made up of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ConfigUID/ClassDefUID/InterfaceDefUIDs/UpdatedProperties

For example, this results on the following Subject Definition:

Product/ServerName/OnUpdated/PL_APIPlantA/SPFFolder/ISPFFTRItem,ISPFExportableItem,ISPFSubscribableItem,ISPFOwnedObj,ISPFWorkflowItem,ISPFFolder,ISPFSupportItem,IObject/IObject|LastUpdatedDate,IObject|Descript

Relationship Event Subject Definition:

The Object Event Subject Definition is made up of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ConfigUID/RelDefUID/InterfaceDefUIDs

Relationship Update Event Subject Definition:

The Object Event Subject Definition is made up of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ConfigUID/RelDefUID/InterfaceDefUIDs/UpdatedProperties

Custom Process Step Event Subject Definition:

The Custom Process Step Event Subject Definition is made up of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ConfigUID/ WorkflowStepClassName

For example, this results in the following Subject Definition:

Product/ServerName/Execute/PL_PlantA/ExampleProcessStepClassName

Change Action Event Subject Definition:

The change action event subject definition is made of the following:

Product/ServerName/EventType/ToolSignature/ConfigUID

For example,

Product/ServerName/OnChangeActionUpdated/AAAD/PR_DEMOPROJ2

Server extensibility example scenarios

Server extensibility enables external applications to perform tasks based upon events happening within SDx. The following are examples of tasks based on events:

Auditing

Using the event payload, you can monitor what actions users are performing, such as create or delete.

How

When an event is received, the EventType property of the event payload is checked for the action of interest, such as OnCreated. If there is a match then the user value and object data is recorded.

Backup

You can listen for when a file is created and then upload a duplicate of the new file as a backup to another file store.

How

When an event is received, it checks for the Types array property for ISPFFolder and the EventType is OnCreated to copy to the backup file store.

Metrics

You can capture the events so that you can see how often certain actions are being performed.

How

Store events as they are received into a database. This allows you to make queries and reports that will give you insights in how your SDx system is being used.

Multiple Event Subscribers

You can have multiple event subscribers depending on whether you have setup your broker with either:

A topic (multicast), which gives each subscriber a copy of the event

A queue (anycast), which operates on a first come first served basis

Notifications

You can send an email every time a type of object is created.

How

Inspect the Types and EventType property of the event payload as the event is received. If the properties match, then it sends an email.

Overriding Default Server Behaviour

Extensibility allows you to send OData requests back to SDx in reaction to events that change the data of an object.

How

Listen for the appropriate event. For example, the EventType is OnCreated and the Type is ISPFDesignDocVersion, which sends a PATCH OData request back to SDx to update the object that was created.

Synchronisation

If you have the same file in multiple locations, such as SDx, you can listen to the file update event and automatically update the file accordingly.

How

Look for DocVersionType and OnUpdated EventType in the event payloads. When this event is received, it makes a OData request to SDx to locate and update the file using the relevant DocVersion client API.

Workflow

You can set up custom workflow process steps to perform actions when that step of the workflow is reached.

How

When your custom process step is reached within your workflow, an event is sent with the name of the custom workflow process step used as the Types value. When you receive these events, you can react and perform the tasks as

required.

Smart Cloud Extensibility Built On-Premise

In Smart Cloud, you can use Extensibility when SDx is implemented on-premise as it works in exactly the same way as in the standard SDx Web Client implementation. However, some infrastructure work is required to be done by the
Smart Cloud Support Team to fully configure your implementation.

The Smart Cloud Support Team must set up an existing estate to get the Cloud server talking to your company's infrastructure on-premises system. Once the configuration is done, Extensibility for both the server side and the client side is

the same as a standard SDx Web Client implementation.

For information on extensibility configuration, please contact the Smart Cloud Support Team, see Contact the Smart Cloud Support Team.

Smart Cloud Server Extensibility

To configure SDx with Extensibility running in Smart Cloud, you should open a Service Request to the Smart Cloud Support Team so that Smart Cloud server side extensibility is configured for your site. This service request must specify
the full SDx site name.

 To raise a Service Request, see Contact the Smart Cloud Support Team.

When the configuration is complete, the Advanced Message Queuing Protocol (AMQP) settings are correctly configured for the site, and the connection details for the third-party system are available in the Smart Cloud Portal under the

Estate SDx Topics automation tile.

 Smart Cloud connection requires some specific configuration for server Extensibility to work in the Cloud. The server connection is configured to use https through port 443, as the Cloud supports Web Sockets. No additional

firewall rules need to be provided as the Service Bus is hosted in Smart Cloud. However, if the Service Bus is hosted externally to Smart Cloud, additional firewall rules will need to be configured.

Smart Cloud Web Client Extensibility

If your SDx is running in Smart Cloud and you want to configure the Extensibility to display your application in the Web Client, this requires specific help from the Smart Cloud Support Team. You should open a Service Request with the

Smart Cloud Support Team and specify the following information:

The Title of Estate Request should include Web Client Extensibility.

The external URL of the server where the Extensibility code is hosted.

 Hexagon recommends that Extensibility code should always be hosted on a separate server in a different domain. This can mitigate any browser security concerns.

When the Web Client Extensibility configuration is complete, the connection details for the third-party system are available in the Smart Cloud Portal under the Estate SDx Topics automation tile.

 For information on contacting Smart Cloud Support Team, see Contact the Smart Cloud Support Team.

User Impersonation

User impersonation allows the SDx application to perform actions with a back-end service using the identity of a user to get data from another back-end store, such as a resource server. The SDx application performs actions on behalf of
the user to authenticate and authorize application specific actions, such as to retrieve or modify data in a resource server.

The SDx application authenticates with the authentication server using the client id and client secret to obtain an OAuth token which it uses to impersonate the user to make any second service calls done through requests by the OData

endpoint to the back-end service.

 The user client id must be present in the OAuth token so that the request can be authenticated.

You specify which user to impersonate using the header key X-Ingr-OnBehalfOf in the OAuth token and set the value to the LoginName property for the user that needs to be impersonated. For example, X-Ingr-OnBehalfOf: adminUser.

When you use user impersonation, all the roles available to the user are selected.

User impersonation can only be performed using an OAuth token obtained from the client credentials authentication workflow.

When you use user impersonation with batch requests, the impersonation header must be on every request part. You must not use the user impersonation header on the parent request. When using batch change sets, you must use

exactly the same user impersonation across a single change set.

User Impersonation security

Each client application that you want to impersonate a user with must obtain an OAuth licensing token. Every client application must also have a corresponding SPFClientApplication object created in SDx.

You control which users can be impersonated using the SPFClientApplication object.

All users in the client application can be impersonated by setting the Allow impersonation of all users property to True for each SPFClientApplication object.

You can restrict user impersonation to just a subset of users by selecting False and then creating a relationship between the subset of users you want to be impersonated and the client application.

The client application client id in each SPFClientApplication object must match the authorization server client id exactly.

The SPFClientApplication object must be created using the client id of an OAuth client, which uses the Client Credentials authorization work flow only.

Additional support

The following topics provide additional support for Extensibility applications.

Find sample data on Smart Community

To access the sample data on Smart Community, complete the following steps:

1. Go to Smart Community <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> , and log on with your support credentials.

2. On the home page, select DOWNLOADS.

3. Select the required product support page link.

4. Select Accept on the Hexagon Legal Notice that is displayed.

5. Go to the Freeware Tools and Utilities page on the product support page.

6. Download the required files.

 If you do not see the sample data you need, check back later. We are working on it.

Contact the Smart Cloud Support Team

 You can only create Smart Cloud Service Incidents or Service Requests if you have Smart Community level access by logging on to https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com/ <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com> .

You can use the following information to raise an estate request with the Smart Cloud Support Team.

1. Click the Support button

 on the Intergraph Smart Cloud Portal home page.

 If you are a Smart Community user, you will be redirected to the Smart Community page, where you can create a Smart Cloud Service Incident or Service Request.

2. Type the Estate Support details in the specified boxes, and click Submit. A support ticket is generated and an email sent to your email address with the Estate Support details, such as the estate technical contact information, and

so on.

For more information on managing estates in Smart Cloud Help, see Managing Estates.

For more information about Smart Community, see https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com <https://smartcommunity.hexagonppm.com/> .

Troubleshooting Extensibility issues

The following topics provide solutions to common Extensibility issues.

Troubleshooting Server Extensibility

No AMQP settings in Server Manager

Problem

When you install the latest update release, the updated site in Server Manager does not contain the AMQPBroker site settings.

Solution

You must run the Update Site and Upgrade Site commands in Server Manager after installing the update release so that you can see the AMQPBroker settings in the site. For example, click [application] Sites node, and right-click [Site]

> Tools > Update Site.

 The AMQPBroker site settings are only available in releases from Update 4.

Server events are not coming through

Problem

After you have correctly set up your system with an AMQP broker server and service but you cannot see any server events coming through SDx.

Solution

This is probably because either the AMQP broker server or service is not running.You must check that both the configured AMQP Broker server and service are working and running correctly.

 The AMQPBroker site settings are only available in releases from Update 4.

An invalid request URI was provided

Problem

When you upgrade to the latest update release of SDx and initialize Server Extensibility, a warning message appears that states:

An invalid request URI was provided. The request URI must either be an absolute URI or BaseAdress must be set.

Solution

You must make sure that the AuthServerAuthority setting in the authorization server does not contain any uppercase letters. For example:

<add key="AuthServerAuthority" value="https://test.hexagon.com/testconfigsvc/sdxauthentication/oauth/" />

Troubleshooting Web Client Extensibility

This section provides assistance for common problems encountered when configuring Extensibility for SDx web client.

 For more information about querying for data using OData, see OData syntax examples using Web API v1 or v2.

Cannot embed a website in an iframe

Problem

You cannot embed in the Web Client as it is being prevented from being embedded in an iframe.

Reason

Some websites use specific HTTP headers, such as X-Frame-options and Content Security Policy (CSP), to prevent the website from being embedded in an iframe. These headers are important, as they are used to stop the website from

an outside attack called click-jacking. For example, the website www.google.com prevents it from being embedded in an iframe.

Solution

Some applications will not allow a report to be embedded in an iframe by default, such as Power BI. However, the Power BI application and others offer an option allowing you to generate a link, which can be used to embed the content in

an iframe. If you have access to modify the HTTP headers of a website, you can embed the website in an iframe for a specific domain.

 If you modify the website so it can be embedded in an iframe for multiple domains, it will not be as secure.

Nothing appears when a method is executed

Problem

When you execute a method configured for the Web Client using Extensibility, nothing is displayed or returned.

Solution

You must ensure that the method argument has been configured with the correct URL added to Parameter 1 of the method. The URL should be testable by executing the method in a new tab.

Site must be embedded in the Web Client

Problem

This site must be embedded in the web client

This error is displayed when you try to open the SDx Extensibility application in a new tab.

Solution

You may encounter this error as the sample Extensibility application is loaded in a new tab. This is not an exception but part of the default sample Extensibility behavior. If you do not want this behavior, you must remove the host guard

code from the samples.

Incorrect domain communication

Problem

There is no communication between the Web Client and the Extensibility application domains.

Solution

You must ensure that the communication between the Web Client and the Extensibility application has been correctly configured. The communication must be setup to send events to the correct domain. If any Angular samples have been

used, you must ensure that the values set in the settings.json file are correctly configured.

The loading indicator continues to be displayed

Problem

When you execute a method configured to use Extensibility, the loading indicator does not stop and continues to be displayed.

Solution

The loading indicator is automatically displayed as a method is being executed. When the loading indicator does not stop being displayed, this can often be the first sign that an exception has occurred, which will need to be turned off using

code for the process to continue.

Security headers

Problem

A security header prevents the Extensibility application being loaded and displayed and an error is displayed.

Solution

HTTP security headers can prevent Extensibility applications from being embedded in the Web Client. You must ensure that any X-FRAME-OPTIONS or Content-Security-Policy header values are configured to use HTTPS to allow the

Extensibility application load correctly.

Downgrading http status

Problem

Your URL application uses HTTP protocol.

Solution

You must ensure that any Extensibility application to be embedded in an iframe uses HTTPS in exactly the same way as SDx.

Generic error diagnosis

The easiest place to check for an error is to view the details found in the Console log in the browser. The browser Console can be accessed by pressing the F12 key or by navigating to the Developer tools option in the specific browser
menu.

Ensure there are no filters applied to the Console log so that all the logs can be viewed. You must review each entry in the log when fault diagnosing and pay particular attention to any displayed errors.

For Console errors relating to Document referrer, you must ensure that both sites are using HTTPS. The parent application must have the response header Referrer-Policy set to a minimum value of no-referrer-when-downgrade.

For Console errors relating to Whitelist, you must ensure that the correct parent domain has been added to the Whitelist setting in the web.config file of the application server used by the Web Client.

For Console errors relating to location errors, you must ensure that the browser allows pop ups.

# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\P&ID Engineering Integrity.pdf
---

Validate P&ID Integrity - Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Enterprise Work Process for Process Design

1

Validate P&ID Integrity

1.  As a standards organization, we define and provide engineering best practices and standards, sometimes by industry, so that facilities are designed with sound engineering practices for safe and efficient operation.

2.  As an engineering company, we define our EPC corporate design standards to be used in our projects, based on our own experience and best industry practices, so that they can be executed as efficiently and safely as possible.

3.  As an administrator of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to create the library of rules based on the corporate and industry standards so that they can be used in projects.

4.  As an owner company, we define our standards to be used during the design and operation of our facilities, based on our own experience and best industry practices.

5.  As an administrator of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the owner, I need to create the library of rules based on the corporate and industry standards so that they can be used in projects.

6.  As an administrator of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the owner, I need to select the rules that apply to this particular project so that I can use them internally and also send them to the EPC to use during the project. I may also receive and

import some rules from the EPC.

7.  As an administrator of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to select from our corporate library of rules for use in the project and also import the rules supplied by the owner, if applicable, so that they can be used on this specific

project.

8.  As a SmartPlant P&ID user for the EPC, I need to develop P&IDs for the project.

9.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to identify the subset of rules that need to be used, based on the current design phase of the project, so that the P&IDs can be checked against them. For instance, I may select a

small subset of rules during the FEED stage and the complete set of rules during Detailed Design.

10.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the owner, I need to identify the subset of rules that need to be used, based on the current design phase of the project, so that the P&IDs can be checked against them. For instance, I may select

a small subset of rules during the FEED stage and the complete set of rules during Detailed Design.

11.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to import the P&IDs into my environment so that I can validate them against the rules.

12.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to run the rules against a set of P&IDs so that the design complies with the standards. It is possible that some rules will need adjustments based on new P&ID configurations,

Intergraph Proprietary Information

project requirements, or revised standards.

13.  <Decision point> As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the EPC, I need to determine if the P&IDs comply with the project rules.

14.  <Decision point> As a process engineer, I need to determine if the non-compliance found is acceptable to the project.

15.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID, I need to correct the P&ID so that it complies with the project standards and the rules can be re-run against it.

16.  As a process engineer, I need to issue the P&IDs to the owner so that they can be reviewed.

17.  As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the owner, I need to run the rules against a set of P&IDs received from the engineering company so that I can check if the design complies with the standards. It is possible that some rules will

need adjustments based on new P&ID configurations, project requirements, or revised standards.

18.  <Decision point> As a user of SmartPlant P&ID Engineering Integrity for the owner, I need to determine if the P&IDs comply with the project rules.

19.  As an owner company, we approve the P&IDs.

Enterprise Work Process for Process Design

2

# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\PipeStressAnalysis.pdf
---

Develop Pipe Stress Analysis - All Phases - Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

Develop Pipe Stress Analysis - All Phases - Level 2

  This work process applies to critical, as well as other (non-critical) lines.

1.  As a piping designer, I need to develop the 3D piping/equipment design so that I can convey the updated status of the plant to other disciplines.

2.  As a piping designer, I need to generate stress isometrics so that the necessary files can be sent to CAESAR II for pipe stress analysis.

3.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to import the stress isometrics (pcf files) generated by Smart 3D into CAESAR II to run the pipe stress analysis using mapping files to map supports, fittings, etc.

4.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to analyze the piping network so that I can generate the pipe stress report including stress in pipes, loads on support and equipment, pipe deflections and any proposed model changes to satisfy the stress

analysis.

5.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to reference the 3D model to see the context around the piping network and the pipe supports.

6.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to add pipe supports, or edit the existing ones, and add any special design conditions required for the analysis.

7.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to analyze the piping network again so that I can generate the pipe stress report including stress in pipes, loads on support and equipment, pipe deflections and any proposed model changes to satisfy the stress

analysis.

8.  As a stress analysis engineer, I need to generate a pipe stress report, with graphical information, that can be shared with Smart 3D for review and used to make design changes, as necessary.

9.  As a piping designer, I need to reference the CAESAR II model so that I can review the results of the pipe stress analysis.

10.  As a piping designer, I need to review the pipe stress report, with graphical information, that is generated by CAESAR II so that I can update my design, as necessary.

11.  As a piping designer, I need to place logical and/or physical pipe supports in the 3D model, based on the results of the stress analysis.

12.  As a piping designer, I need to update my piping design according to information received in the pipe stress analysis and other design criteria.

13.  As a piping designer, I need to develop the preliminary piping/equipment design so that I can convey the updated status of the plant to other disciplines.

14.  <decision point> As a piping designer, I need to determine whether the updates made require further stress analysis. If yes, I need to generate the stress isometrics again so that pipe stress analysis can be run.

DSPE1-PE-200130A - Updated Enterprise Work Process for Piping and Equipment Design

1




# SOURCE: vendor_hexagon\Plant Layout.pdf
---

Develop Plant Layout - FEED - Level 2 Diagram

Intergraph Proprietary Information

DSPE1-PE-200130A - Updated Enterprise Work Process for Piping and Equipment Design

1

Develop Plant Layout - FEED - Level 2

1.  As a process engineer, I need to develop simple P&IDs (with the same level of detail and information as PFDs) so that the plant layout can be developed.

2.  As a process engineer, I need to publish the simple P&IDs.

3.

If the document has publishable data, the document and data are loaded into SmartPlant Foundation and are subsequently available for use by other applications. If the document does not have publishable data, it is available for viewing in SmartPlant
Foundation.

4.  As a process engineer, I need to develop PFDs and data sheets.

5.  As a process engineer, I need to generate a stream report spreadsheet from Aspen Basic Engineering so that it can be used by the plant layout specialist to import run data.

6.  As a process engineer, I need to add a few properties to the spreadsheet report so that I can provide the plant layout specialist with the information needed to route pipes. Properties to be added include the following:

Intergraph Proprietary Information

  Pipeline name

  Pipe run name

  Pipe spec

  From Type

  To Type

7.

(Option A) As a plant layout specialist, I need to place equipment and zones so that I can prepare to retrieve P&ID pipe runs, route the pipelines, and optimize the plant layout.

8.

(Option A) As a plant layout specialist, I need to retrieve P&IDs from SmartPlant P&ID so that they can automatically create pipelines and pipe runs in the 3D model and I can correlate the equipment and nozzles.

9.

(Option A) As a plant layout specialist, I need to correlate equipment and nozzles that were retrieved with the existing equipment placed earlier.

10.  (Option B) As a plant layout specialist, I need to place equipment and zones so that I can prepare to manually create pipelines.

11.  (Option B) As a plant layout specialist, I need to manually create pipelines so that I can route them and optimize the plant layout.

12.  (Option B) As a plant layout specialist, I need to manually select, in the 3D model, the From and To items so that I can route the pipelines between them and optimize the plant layout.

13.  (Option C) As a plant layout specialist, I need to place equipment and zones to prepare for routing the pipelines and optimizing the plant layout.

14.  (Option C) As a plant layout specialist, I need to create pipelines so that I can route pipelines and optimize the plant layout.

15.  (Option C) As a plant layout specialist, I need to import the enhanced Excel report from Aspen Basic Engineering so that I can route pipelines and optimize the plant layout.

16.  As a plant layout specialist, I need to auto-route pipelines so that I can create the optimal plant layout.

17.  <decision point> As a plant layout specialist, I need to determine if I have created the optimal plant layout. If the routing is not optimized for some lines, I need to auto-route them again, or make manual adjustments to them.

DSPE1-PE-200130A - Updated Enterprise Work Process for Piping and Equipment Design

2

строительстве и эксплуатации автомобильных дорог. Общие требования». 
по теме: 
Доработка проекта ПНСТ с учетом полученных замечаний и разработка окончательной редакции ПНСТ 
«Автомобильные дороги общего пользования. BIM-технологии при строительстве и эксплуатации 
автомобильных дорог. Общие требования»

заинтересованных лиц с составлением сводки замечаний.

Цель работы: 
- актуализация регламентов взаимодействия участников процесса 
строительства и эксплуатации автомобильных дорог с учетом поэтапного 
внедрения BIM технологий; 
- разработка общих требований к BIM-модели объекта в увязке с 
требованиями к программным комплексам, применяемым каждым из 
участников процесса строительства и эксплуатации дорог; 
- разработка Предварительного Национального Стандарта (ПНСТ) 
«Автомобильные 
дороги общего пользования. 
BIM-технологии 
при 
строительстве и эксплуатации автомобильных дорог. Общие требования».

Краткая характеристика объекта стандартизации (ПНСТ) 
Предметом 
стандартизации 
является 
формирование 
общих 
требований к применению BIM- технологии на стадии строительства и 
эксплуатации 
автомобильных 
дорог. 
Разработанный 
в 
результате 
выполнения данной НИОКР 
документ содержит подробное описание 
регламентов 
взаимодействия 
всех 
участников 
строительства, 
6 
реконструкции, 
ремонта, 
капитального 
ремонта 
и 
эксплуатации 
автомобильных дорог в процессе поэтапного внедрения BIM-технологий. 
Проработаны общие требования к BIM-моделям в увязке с требованиями к 
программным комплексам и с учетом специфики процессов строительства и 
эксплуатации 
автомобильных 
дорог. 
Определены 
нейтральные 
(совместимые) 
форматы 
промежуточных 
данных 
и 
результатов 
информационного моделирования для обеспечения беспрепятственного 
обмена 
информацией 
между 
всеми 
информационными 
системами 
участников процесса. 
Сведения 
о 
взаимодействии 
с 
техническими 
регламентами, 
национальными стандартами и стандартами организаций, согласованными 
с Росавтодором

характеристика принципиальных замечаний и предложений 
(сводка 
замечаний) 
Согласно технического задания [приложение №1] на проведение 
научно-исследовательской работы был сформирован и согласован с 
Заказчиком список рецензентов, состоящий из следующих организаций: 
1. АО «Институт «Стройпроект», 
2. ООО «ВТМ Дорпроект СТОЛИЦА», 
3. ООО «Саратовский научно - производственный центр «РОСДОРТЕХ», 
4. ООО «Малое инновационное предприятие «МАДИ - цифровая 
дорожная инфраструктура», 
5. ООО «Компания «КРЕДО-ДИАЛОГ», 
6. ООО «Научно-производственная фирма «Топоматик». 
В процессе работы были учтены замечания и предложения всех 
8 
организаций рецензентов. Общее количество замечаний - 109, ответы на 
замечания отражены в сводке. 
Описание выполненных этапов научно-исследовательской работы 
Настоящий отчет подготовлен согласно техническому заданию на 
выполнение работ по государственному контракту № ФДА47/327 от 07 
ноября 2016. 
На I этапе выполнения работы был изучен и проанализирован 
отечественный 
и 
зарубежный 
опыт 
использования 
концепции 
информационного 
моделирования 
процессов 
промышленного, 
гражданского и дорожного строительства. Рассматривались существующие 
практики использования элементов BIM методологии при проектировании, 
строительстве и эксплуатации объектов инфраструктуры, в частности, 
дорог и дорожных объектов. 
В исследованиях, предпринятых на II этапе НИР, 
была сделана 
попытка подробного описания процессов информационного обмена на 
протяжении 
двух этапов жизненного 
цикла 
- «Строительство» 
и 
«Эксплуатация», дорожных объектов с точки зрения информационного 
моделирования. Полученные результаты были сведены в ПНСТ (первая 
редакция). 
Как отмечалось в предыдущих отчетах, в настоящее время большая 
часть информации относительно дорог и дорожных объектов (проектная и 
рабочая 
документация, 
планы 
организации 
дорожного 
движения, 
материалы диагностики и паспортизации, документы земельного учета и 
т.д.) 
циркулирует 
между 
участниками 
процесса 
проектирования, 
строительства и эксплуатации в «бумажном» виде. Лишь небольшая часть 
представлена в электроном виде. Это приводит зачастую к потере, 
искажению или несвоевременному поступлению информации, что в 
конечном счете существенно увеличивает количество коллизий, а, значит, 
9 
ведет к несоблюдению сроков и удорожанию строительства. Этими 
обстоятельствами и обусловлена необходимость эффективного внедрения 
BIM-технологии на всех этапах жизненного цикла объекта. 
Суть концепции информационного моделирования (BIM) состоит в 
том, что на всех этапах выполнения проекта строительства и эксплуатации 
дороги создается и циркулируем между участниками проекта не плохо 
структурированная 
совокупность 
чертежей 
и описаний 
(текстовых, 
табличных, графических) а его информационная модель (см. п. 3.20-3.22 ). 
Наличие такой модели, как общего ресурса, содержащего необходимый на 
каждом этапе объем данных об объекте, а также надлежащая организация 
регламентированного доступа всех участников к ней, дает возможность 
обеспечить процесс своевременного и качественного выполнения проекта. 
Основным содержанием отчета по III, заключительному этапу 
научно-исследовательской 
работы 
является 
окончательная 
редакция 
проекта ПНСТ «Автомобильные дороги общего пользования. BIM-
технологии при строительстве и эксплуатации автомобильных дорог. 
Общие требования», написанная на основании исследований, которые 
проводились на первых двух этапах НИР. Структура документа отражает 
основные вопросы внедрения и использования BIM-технологий на этапах 
строительства и эксплуатации автомобильных дорог и искусственных 
сооружений:

2 Нормативные ссылки. 
3 Термины и определения.

новому этапу жизненного цикла объекта.
